That typically provides upward price support for aluminum in the coming days to weeks. | Vietnam imposed a 15-day nationwide lockdown, limiting consumption and business operations and weighing on demand for Chinese aluminium. China customs data show Vietnam received a meaningful share of Chinese aluminium products. With global lockdowns also shrinking China’s aluminium exports and delaying production, near-term aluminium supply/demand expectations turn bearish.

Shanghai aluminum prices plunged to a four-year low as forecasts of falling demand grew, driven by rising coronavirus infections, tighter lockdowns, and weak global factory activity. The most-traded ShFE contract fell about 2.6%, reflecting deteriorating near-term demand for aluminum used in transport and packaging. | Shanghai aluminum prices plunged to a more than four-year low on weak factory data and rising coronavirus infections, with the benchmark ShFE contract down as much as 2.6% on Thursday. The article links the move to deteriorating manufacturing activity and demand uncertainty from pandemic-related disruptions. | Shanghai aluminium prices on the ShFE fell to a more-than-four-year low after poor factory data and worsening coronavirus infections raised concerns about weak demand. The contract dropped as much as 2.6% before a small rebound from bargain buying, but sentiment remains bearish amid broader manufacturing slowdown. | The U.S. Commerce Department launched investigations into imports of common alloy aluminum sheets from 18 countries, checking for dumping and subsidies and possible U.S. injury to domestic producers. If preliminary findings turn positive, tariffs could be imposed, tightening supply prospects. An ITC preliminary decision is due by April 23. | Shanghai base metals closed mixed: copper rose 1.02% on a crude oil rebound tied to expectations of Saudi-Russia output talks, nickel edged up 0.16%, while aluminium fell 0.56% as oversupply persisted. SMM notes primary aluminium inventories kept expanding this week and highlights key support/resistance levels. | Queensland has appointed Glencore as the preferred proponent to develop the multi-billion-dollar Aurukun bauxite deposit in Cape York. Indigenous leaders criticized the process and consultation, but the decision supports progress toward bauxite production, which may increase perceived aluminium supply prospects.

London copper eased 0.2% on Friday amid weaker investor sentiment as Covid-19 spread, though it remained on track for its first weekly gain in six weeks after better-than-expected Chinese factory data earlier. The piece also flags grim demand outlook and mentions LME aluminium rebounding from recent lows after a four-year low. | A market research report discusses expected 2020 growth changes for the global aluminum market, attributing near-term outcomes to COVID-19 disruptions and a supply-chain recovery that may begin around Q2 as quarantines and travel bans ease. It provides general demand/supply scenario framing through 2025 but no concrete near-term pricing trigger. | LAlumina LLC, a specialty alumina producer in Ascension Parish, plans layoffs for 302 workers over late May into early June, with a full shutdown after limited inventory runs out. Management blames a coronavirus-driven slump in alumina prices. Plant disruptions and demand weakness may pressure nearby aluminium prices.

London copper rose 1.1% as Chile’s top miners consider production cuts during strict coronavirus measures. The report cites recession fears but highlights supply restraint from Chile. Nickel edged higher and aluminium was flat, reflecting mixed metal-specific sentiment despite broad pandemic-driven uncertainty. | Copper prices firmed as coronavirus deaths and new infections eased in several European countries and LME/SHFE copper inventories fell, after factory data in China improved sentiment. At the same time, Antofagasta said it will suspend work on a Chile expansion for four months, raising supply disruption concerns. | Chemaf, Congo subsidiary of Shalina Resources, halted operations at its Usoke copper-cobalt processing plant and paused construction at Mutoshi because COVID-era travel restrictions prevented sourcing enough oxide ore, leaving Usoke loss-making. It also shut the Mutoshi artisanal cobalt site, likely extending closure, while Etoile continues producing. | Copper rose 0.8% as coronavirus deaths and new infections eased in parts of Europe, improving risk sentiment, while LME-approved copper inventories fell and SHFE stocks declined further. It also flagged coronavirus-related operational suspensions for copper and possible aluminum output cuts, alongside expectations of nickel supply tightening. | Russian aluminium producer Rusal has approved a $16.3bn long-term supply deal with Glencore to sell up to 6.9Mt of primary aluminium from 2020-2024 (plus an option to extend). The contract replaces an expiring agreement and supports primary aluminium sales at market price.

Copper rallied to a nearly three-week high as slower coronavirus death tolls and easing-lockdown expectations boosted investor sentiment. LME three-month copper rose to around $5,025/t, with support from lower exchange inventories and possible supply disruptions. The piece also mentions gains in aluminium and a nickel supply-tightness offset. | Copper prices jumped about 4% to a near three-week high on hopes that the coronavirus outbreak is slowing and that stimulus will offset economic damage. The same relief/risk rally lifted other industrial metals, with aluminium also firmer. Aluminium premiums fell on weak demand, while nickel rose modestly. | Copper prices jumped to the highest in nearly three weeks as reports suggested COVID-19 cases may be slowing and markets bet on substantial economic stimulus. LME three-month copper rose about 3%. Aluminium also edged up, while spot premiums fell and cash discounts widened, reflecting still-weak physical demand.

Australia’s ATO escalated its transfer-pricing probe into Rio Tinto’s aluminium division, moving to a formal amended assessment. The probe adds pressure to ongoing negotiations with the federal government over a rescue package for Rio’s three loss-making Australian smelters, raising near-term production and supply uncertainty for aluminium markets. | SMM reports SHFE base metals ended mixed, with copper and nickel up and aluminium slightly down. Copper rebounded on stronger oil prices and recent mine closures/curtailments tied to virus containment, keeping ore-supply concerns. Nickel rose on aggressive long buying and short covering. Aluminium recovered but finished marginally weaker. | Aluminium prices fell to a four-year low on coronavirus-linked demand weakness and renewed expectations of a sizeable 2020 surplus. LME warehouse stocks rose sharply, cash discounts widened, and analysts cited auto sector slumps and ongoing production growth, indicating increased glut risk and further near-term downside. | Victory Nickel reported completion of two diamond drill holes at its Minago sulphide nickel project in Manitoba, noting assay results may be delayed due to COVID-19 movement restrictions. The company also stated its 7P sand plant in Alberta remains an essential service and continues selling sand to customers. No aluminium-specific information was provided. | Century Aluminum is investigating a fatal incident at its Sebree smelter in Kentucky, where an employee died after injuries in the anode production facilities. The worker was treated on scene and transported to a hospital. The company has not provided downtime details, but the incident may disrupt near-term aluminium production.

Shanghai-based SMM data shows China’s social inventories of primary aluminium ingots across eight consumption areas fell 69,000 mt to 1.607 million mt in the week ended April 9, the first weekly decline since Lunar New Year. Lower prices prompted smelters to hold back sales and cut warehouse deliveries, while demand improved. | SMM data show China’s aluminium billet inventories fell for a fifth straight week. In five major consumption areas, 6063 billet stocks dropped 4,100 mt in the week ended April 9 to 170,100 mt after an 18,500 mt decline the prior week, indicating tighter near-term availability. | A Reuters poll expects copper, aluminium and nickel to weaken as coronavirus lockdowns reduce industrial activity, including autos and construction. Analysts forecast copper prices grinding lower, with a large surplus. Aluminium surpluses are set to expand further, while nickel faces delayed stainless demand despite supply constraints. | Copper is set for its biggest weekly gain in 14 months as signs of slowing coronavirus spread improve risk appetite and equities. China copper import premiums rise and LME price strength follows. Offsets include higher LME warehouse inventories and a copper concentrate force majeure from Las Bambas, but near-term tone stays bullish. | A Reuters poll says coronavirus lockdowns have frozen industrial activity, hitting copper, aluminium and nickel demand. Analysts expect large surpluses—especially for aluminium—and forecast sharply lower prices, including new lows for copper in Q2. Nickel demand from stainless steel and construction is also weakening. | The piece says aluminium refineries are finding it difficult to stay cash positive because alumina prices are at the lowest levels since Feb 2016. It frames weak alumina economics as a near-term stress for the aluminium refining chain, which can weigh on aluminium price sentiment.

China’s exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium products fell around 5% YoY to about 518,600 mt in March, with total first-quarter exports down 17.5% YoY. The report expects further YoY declines in Q2 as coronavirus containment measures weaken overseas demand.

Copper prices eased from a four-week high as investors grew more anxious about the global economy, citing IMF warnings of a deep 2020 recession tied to coronavirus activity collapse. LME three-month copper fell 0.1% and ShFE copper dropped 0.5%, despite China easing funding rates amid supply-risk support. | Law360 reports that the U.S. industry filed new anti-dumping and countervailing duty petitions for common alloy aluminum sheet imports from 18 countries despite COVID-19 disruptions. The article notes pandemic-related demand declines and argues lower net prices and low-priced import inventories will intensify pressure on aluminum sheet markets.

Wenshan Aluminium’s 500,000-mt aluminum smelting and hydropower project in Yunnan will bring its first potline online by end-April. The company also runs an alumina refinery. The region has additional under-construction/built aluminium smelting capacity, implying rising near-term supply and potential downward pressure on aluminium prices. | Shanghai aluminium futures jumped to a nearly four-week high as China aluminium inventories fell for multiple weeks and social stocks hit a five-week low. Analysts cited increasing expectations of supply cuts. LME three-month aluminium also rose, though LME warehouse stocks increased, suggesting abundant prompt supply. | Group Ten Metals reported drill results from its Stillwater West project, highlighting wide intervals of palladium/platinum and also nickel and copper sulphide mineralization. The release supports potential future resource development but provides no immediate changes to production or supply affecting copper or nickel prices in the next days-to-weeks. | Bonnell Aluminum said it is protecting worker health across five states while continuing production and shipment of custom aluminum extrusions used in critical infrastructure and other essential products during the COVID-19 response. The company emphasizes no disruption to supply and rapid fulfillment despite supply-chain challenges. | Copper firmed on Thursday after China data suggested gradual recovery in the property sector, while LME inventories eased. However, recession concerns and anticipated metals demand destruction capped gains. Aluminium also rose for multiple sessions on expectations of supply cuts and improving Chinese market conditions.

SMM reports SHFE base metals opened higher as worries eased amid tentative US steps to restart the economy. Copper rose over 2%, supported by supply disruptions and an accelerated copper inventory draw in China, plus Rio Tinto cutting 2020 copper production targets. Aluminium edged up, while nickel steadied after losses. | LME copper was set for its best week since Feb 2019 as Trump outlined a staged U.S. reopening plan. Copper rose up to 1.9%, with reports of strong Chinese physical premiums and tight scrap/concentrates. The article also notes LME aluminium and nickel gains alongside improved market sentiment.

Social inventories of primary aluminium ingots in China kept falling over the weekend. SMM data show stocks across eight consumption areas declined by 60,000 mt from April 16 to about 1.435 million mt by April 20, with demand described as strong, suggesting tighter near-term availability for aluminium. | Vale slashed its 2020 nickel output forecast due to COVID-19, along with additional mine shutdowns, pushing LME nickel up about 2.6% to a one-month high. Vale also cut copper output estimates, though copper eased. Aluminium is weighed by a large rise in LME stocks and oversupply expectations, with prices down. | Shanghai base metals closed mostly higher after China cut lending rates for the second time this year and stimulus expectations increased. Nickel surged 4.4% on the SHFE and LME. Copper and aluminium also posted gains, with copper supported by ongoing ore-supply concerns and aluminium stabilizing after strong weekly moves. | The live futures report shows strong buying in LME nickel futures, lifting prices 3.7%. Copper is reported to have dipped below $5,200/t. It also notes procedural corrections and delayed European low-carbon aluminium differential assessments, alongside commentary on aluminium supply and shipping disruptions, suggesting mixed near-term effects.

China’s refined copper cathode output fell 2.5% year on year in March to a 10-month low of 771,000 mt, with COVID-19 disruption cited as the cause. Daily output also declined versus Jan-Feb. China’s alumina production fell 3.6% y/y, which may modestly affect aluminium supply expectations. | AL-Monitor’s piece focuses on US anti-dumping duties and their impact on Egypt’s aluminum industry. Such trade measures can alter demand for Egyptian aluminum exports to the US and/or shift regional supply and pricing expectations, creating near-term downside pressure for aluminium. Details are not provided in the excerpt.

SHFE nonferrous metals opened lower as oil prices collapsed, weighing on sentiment and prompting renewed demand and cash-crunch concerns. LME base metals fell overnight; nickel led with a 2.86% drop and copper fell 2.85%, while aluminium declined 1%. Traders expect continued weakness amid crude weakness. | China’s spot aluminium premiums over SHFE May continued widening as trade stayed brisk. Prices in east China were about 30–50 yuan/mt above May, higher than Tuesday. Social inventories of primary aluminium fell by over 100,000 mt week-on-week, with deeper backwardation and stronger demand indications. | Copper prices steadied after an oil-driven selloff as miners warned coronavirus would reduce output and financial markets stabilized. China’s reopening, higher import premiums, and falling treatment charges suggest tightening supply. Antofagasta and Teck actions reinforced supply concerns. Aluminium demand in China rose on scrap shortages, supporting prices modestly. | Global aluminium production rose 2.1% in the first quarter, while usage/demand failed to keep pace. LME CMAL3 hit a four-year low around $1,455/t earlier in March and remains near $1,490. Producers are restarting and ramping capacity, while LME stocks have surged and spreads have loosened. | A U.S. investigation into aluminum sheet imports from 18 countries is moving forward. Such probes can result in tariffs or other import barriers that tighten supply entering the U.S., often lifting expectations for near-term aluminum prices as buyers anticipate reduced import availability. | Copper strengthened after two days of heavy selling as miners warned coronavirus would cut output and China began reopening, easing demand shock. Indicators such as falling Chinese treatment charges and higher Yangshan import premiums signaled tighter supply. LME aluminium also rose on reported growth in China’s primary demand driven by scrap shortages. | The headline says Trump will restore Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminium for Brazil and Argentina. Tariffs on aluminium imports are expected to tighten regional supply into the tariff-receiving market and lift import costs, which typically adds upward pressure to aluminium prices over the next days to weeks. | Alcoa said it halted its global quarterly supply-and-demand projections for alumina, aluminium and bauxite because of the coronavirus pandemic, while keeping all bauxite mines, alumina refineries, aluminum smelters and rolling mills operational. The move pauses market guidance but does not indicate immediate production cuts, suggesting limited net price impact.

The headline says FIMI is seeking withdrawal of an export duty on non-metallurgical-grade bauxite. If adopted, it would likely ease export economics and improve near-term availability of bauxite feedstock connected to the aluminium value chain, supporting aluminium price expectations over the coming days to weeks. | Henan Zhongfu’s aluminium smelting and hydropower project in Sichuan is expected to bring potline 2 online in June, with full capacity reaching 250,000 mt/year. Potline 1 has been operating since November 2019. The new plant replaces an old smelter in Linzhou, Henan. | Oil’s rebound lifted SHFE aluminium futures, but spot aluminium premiums over futures stayed firm. East China A00 premiums were about 40–50 yuan/mt, while Guangdong widened to 100–120 yuan/mt. Strong demand, weak arrivals, aggressive buying, and weekly inventory declines supported prices. | WBMS reported the global primary aluminium surplus widened to 684,000 mt in January–February 2020 versus 492,000 mt for all of 2019. Demand rose 4.1% YoY, but production increased faster (+8.3%). LME-approved stocks fell, yet surplus growth dominates the near-term balance outlook. | Indonesian regional authorities are tightening controls on Chinese foreign workers during COVID-19, ordering some workers to return and denying others entry. The restrictions include Chinese technicians expected to repair nickel smelters at PT Gunbuster Nickel Industries. Separate cases also involve an aluminium producer denied foreign-worker access. | The US International Trade Commission found a reasonable indication of material injury to a U.S. industry from imports of common alloy aluminum sheet from multiple countries, alleging dumping and subsidization. Commerce will continue antidumping and countervailing duty investigations with preliminary rulings scheduled for June 3 and final determinations later in 2020. | Iranian President Rouhani inaugurated the country’s largest, modern aluminum production complex in Lamerd, with 300,000 tons/year capacity and about $1.2 billion investment. Officials said it will raise Iran’s aluminum output by over 63% in the current year and that output should further jump with the nearby SALCO smelter opening.

Reuters column argues a looming aluminium “crisis” as production rose 2.1% in Q1 while aluminium usage fell much less, reflecting a demand shortfall from COVID-19 lockdowns. It cites LME prices hitting a four-year low and notes most smelters continued operating, worsening oversupply risk. | Indian miners urged Finance Minister to withdraw the 15% export duty on non-plant grade bauxite from Gujarat and Maharashtra because low-grade ore is not viable for domestic refiners/smelters. They cite declining exports and request duty removal to keep bauxite exports competitive during Covid-19 and shipping risks. | Copper fell 0.6% on recession worries and negative COVID-19 drug-trial sentiment, with demand pressure expected. The report cites mine shutdown-related supply expectations but says macro fears outweighed them. Nickel eased with on-warrant inventories at a multi-year high, while aluminum remained under pressure amid a ballooning surplus.

Rusal said the coronavirus pandemic cut global aluminum demand by about 6% in Q1 2020, with transport and auto production hardest hit. It also reported rising aluminum supply and a 1.7 million tonne surplus. Rusal still increased output slightly, adding to bearish balance-sheet expectations. | The article says the early coronavirus-driven rally has reversed for Australian producers, with steep falls since February. It highlights significant declines in coal and alumina prices, and notes that aluminium (along with copper and zinc) is also suffering amid pandemic-linked demand concerns, pointing to near-term bearish pressure for aluminium. | London LME copper rallied up to about 2.3% toward a nearly six-week high as major economies planned to reopen after pandemic lockdowns. The article also shows nickel rising ~2.1% and aluminium up ~0.5% alongside, indicating a broad, demand-sentiment-driven lift in industrial metals. | London copper rebounded toward a six-week high as investors expected reopening and factory restarts to revive industrial metals demand, supported by stimulus news. Aluminium stocks are still rising in warehouses, while China’s stockpiling plans and trade/import data bolster sentiment. Nickel also gained on the day. | China’s bauxite imports rose 10.6% year on year in March to 10.01 million tonnes, with Guinea the main supplier. Primary aluminium imports increased 35% to 5,409 tonnes and alumina imports climbed to 338,653 tonnes. Aluminium scrap imports fell 39% year on year to 76,672 tonnes. | En+ says it sees a more visible impact in Q2 from the COVID-19 disruption, citing a drop in aluminium demand and falling aluminium prices. The company’s outlook suggests reduced downstream consumption and weaker pricing pressure for aluminium over the immediate weeks ahead. | Copper rallied to a six-week high as investors bet easing lockdowns, factory restarts, and Japan stimulus would revive industrial demand. European plants and Chinese operations were resuming. LME nickel also rose, while aluminium inventories kept climbing and aluminium was marginally weaker.

China’s aluminium scrap imports rose about 30% in March as domestic secondary producers restarted after a COVID-19 shutdown, rushing to clear backlogged orders and boost processing-trade inflows. March imports reached 77,000 mt. Despite weaker export orders in April, scrap supply appears adequate for Q2. | LME base metals prices were described as consolidating, with copper supported by 7,300 tonnes of fresh cancellations, implying improved near-term tightness versus outstanding supply. The text also mentions procedural corrections/delays to Fastmarkets aluminium assessments and commentary on aluminium supply conditions, but without a clear directional aluminium move.

SMM reports mixed nonferrous performance after Tuesday’s decline tied to falling oil prices. Copper rebounded, aluminium gained more than 1%, and nickel rose modestly (~0.5%). The note attributes gains mainly to macro optimism around economy reopenings and general market mood, plus some supply-related comments.

Copper prices rose after China reported expansion in manufacturing activity for a second straight month in April, improving demand hopes. LME three-month copper increased and ShFE June copper also gained. The report notes aluminum edging up and nickel rising, indicating broader risk-on sentiment across industrial metals. | Copper is set for its biggest monthly gain since Dec 2017 as China’s recovery supports demand expectations. Official factory data improved, while Caixin weak demand tempered sentiment. Supply is supported by miner output cut forecasts and falling Chinese bonded and exchange inventories; aluminium sees smaller stock cancellations. | Glencore reduced 2020 capex and cut production guidance amid COVID-19 operational disruptions and cost pressures. It reported weaker first-quarter copper output and lowered full-year copper guidance, and also trimmed expectations for nickel. The spending/output cuts imply near-term supply pressure and bearish sentiment for copper and nickel markets. | Copper prices eased 1.3% after China private factory data unexpectedly shrank, reflecting COVID-related demand weakness, though official data and recovery hopes limited losses. The market also drew support from supply cut expectations, falling China bonded and ShFE stocks, and rising copper premiums. Aluminium slightly eased on LME stock dynamics. | The U.S. Commerce Department proposes an Aluminum Import Monitoring and Analysis System requiring importers to obtain auto-generated licenses for basic aluminum shipments. It is designed to monitor and potentially limit import surges under Section 232 exemption reviews (e.g., Canada and Mexico). The rule would apply broadly, including FTZ entries. | Century Aluminum Co reported improved Q1 adjusted earnings of $1.0 million (EPS $0.01) versus analysts’ expectations of -$0.09 per share. Revenue fell to $421.2 million from $490.0 million a year earlier, but the earnings beat may support sentiment around aluminium producer profitability and supply outlook in the near term.

London copper fell to a near two-week low, down about 1% on Monday, as some producers signaled they may resume operations while the coronavirus clouded global demand. Although production cuts previously supported prices, Peru eased mining restrictions in May. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Lindian Resources says early XRF and digestion test work from its Gaoual bauxite project in Guinea shows high-grade conglomerate bauxite, with 56% alumina on average and low reactive silica. The company expects an easier processing flow sheet and will proceed toward resource work, but production timing is not yet defined. | Copper prices fell to a two-week low as coronavirus-driven demand worries resurfaced, particularly around China’s ability to sustain base-metals consumption. The piece cites weak PMI data, potential oversupply signals, and Peru easing mining restrictions. Aluminium and nickel also declined on the same risk-off demand concerns. | The U.S. primary aluminium industry praised an import monitoring program and urged immediate action to address a reported Canadian import surge. The push suggests expectations for tighter oversight or mitigations that could reduce unwanted inflows, supporting near-term aluminium market balance in the U.S. | Europe’s copper smelter Aurubis received EU antitrust approval to acquire Belgian-Spanish recycler Metallo for €380 million. Metallo processes substantial scrap volumes at plants in Belgium and Spain, mainly supporting copper recycling capacity. Approval reduces deal uncertainty and supports expectations for continued copper recycling throughput, with limited direct implications for prices immediately.

Richmond, Ind. approved tax abatements for Element 13 LLC to build a facility making aluminum billets by melting scrap and supplying extruder companies. The project includes abatements for real estate, manufacturing equipment, and IT. The expansion suggests incremental aluminum processing capacity, though the local scale may limit market impact. | Aluminium prices dropped to their lowest level in over a decade in April as demand weakened and Rio Tinto continues to consider the future of its Tiwai Point smelter. It also expects aluminium stocks to rise to record levels this year, signalling ongoing bearish fundamentals for aluminium. | LME aluminium slid toward four-year lows as investors feared demand collapse could combine with rising output to create a large oversupply. ING projected a surplus of around 1.5 million tonnes for the year. LME cash discounts widened and warehouse inventories rose, reinforcing plentiful near-term supply signals.

SMM data show Chinese social inventories of primary aluminium ingots fell 32,000 mt from April 30 to May 6, totaling 1.172 million mt, after a larger prior-week drop. The decline is attributed to strong demand and limited arrivals across major consumption regions including SHFE warrants. | Reuters factbox says the coronavirus is slashing aluminium demand while producers still add supply, implying a large annual surplus. However, several smelters and plants have been curtailed or shut due to lockdowns (e.g., Alcoa Intalco, Rio Tinto Tiwai Point pot-line, Aluar Puerto Madryn), and restarts are delayed. | Coronavirus lockdowns are sharply reducing aluminium demand, while analysts expect a major market surplus (2.6 million tonnes in 2020). Producers are idling capacity at multiple smelters and delaying restarts or launches (e.g., Alcoa, Rio Tinto, Norsk Hydro, Aluar, Rusal), but supply is still projected to exceed demand. | China’s aluminium billet inventories rose during the Labour Day holidays, adding 2,700 mt to 132,100 mt as of May 6. The increase follows an eight-week decline and was driven largely by Foshan, Guangdong (+5,200 mt), with other regions mixed. Wuxi and Changzhou were slightly lower. | Sharjah’s Abbco Tower fire spread faster due to banned aluminium composite panel cladding installed before a 2017 UAE prohibition. Authorities plan to assess how widely such cladding was used and whether it should be removed from older towers. Retrofitting and façade upgrades are cited as costly but feasible, implying potential aluminium-related demand.

SMM notes base metals strengthened after the post–Chinese New Year return to trading, with nickel adding over 2% and copper also rebounding, while aluminium is mixed but supported by falling domestic inventories. LME prices were broadly higher overnight; nickel and copper led gains, and aluminium outlook hinges on demand recovery concerns. | China’s exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium products fell about 11.4% YoY to 441,000 mt in April, extending a worsening decline from March. April exports and the first-four-month total both dropped versus last year, attributed to weaker overseas demand as global lockdowns reduce consumption. | China reported April unwrought copper imports rising 4.4% month-on-month, helped by recovering manufacturing activity and arbitrage to Shanghai prices after LME declines, alongside refined copper inventory drawdowns. By contrast, aluminium exports fell 14.9% on weaker overseas orders amid COVID-19 disruptions, signaling softer external demand. | China’s unwrought copper imports rose in April as manufacturing activity resumed, pricing encouraged arbitrage to Shanghai, and refined copper inventories in bonded warehouses were drawn down. Copper concentrate imports also increased. Meanwhile, aluminium exports fell to the lowest monthly level since October as overseas orders weakened during the coronavirus spread. | The article says U.S. aluminum tariffs have failed to support domestic supply: Alcoa is putting the Ferndale smelter on care and maintenance and curtailing capacity due to uncompetitive conditions. Meanwhile imports and semi-manufactured aluminum inflows keep rising amid China’s overcapacity, weighing on LME aluminum prices near multi-year lows. | Reuters column says U.S. 10% aluminium tariffs are not working, with rising aluminium product imports despite an exclusion system. It cites Alcoa putting the Ferndale smelter on care and maintenance and an LME aluminium price near a four-year low. The author blames China’s expanding overcapacity for ongoing pressure.

London copper rose to an eight-week high on expectations that easing lockdowns will improve demand for metals. The article cites economic reopening in Australia, France, Spain and limited UK easing, with three-month LME copper up 1.8%. LME aluminum and nickel were also higher on the same risk-on demand outlook. | London copper rose to an 8-week high as countries began easing COVID restrictions, lifting hopes for metal demand. LME three-month copper climbed up to 1.8% and was still higher at 1.2%. LME aluminum also rose and nickel in Shanghai edged higher amid improving economic signals. | SMM reports SHFE base metals closed mixed but copper and aluminium finished higher (copper +0.39%, aluminium +1.05%), supported by reopening-driven demand prospects and supportive technical/inventory signals. Nickel edged down slightly (-0.15%) yet remains supported by bullish indicators and a stainless-steel futures rally, limiting downside.

Guizhou Zhengzhongyuan Mining plans to invest 600 million yuan to build a high-end special aluminium finishing project in Weng’an County. The project targets 500,000 mt/year capacity, with full operations expected to generate about 1.2 billion yuan in annual output value. The article does not specify near-term commissioning. | SMM reports mixed SHFE/LME base metals after China’s April inflation data missed expectations: CPI rose 3.3% and PPI fell 3.1% y/y. Nickel jumped over 1% on SHFE and LME, copper fell on SHFE, and aluminium gained modestly, keeping overall price impact mixed.

Copper prices fell as markets worried that a second wave of COVID-19 after reopenings could further pressure the global economy. The IMF signaled potential additional growth forecast cuts. LME copper fell 0.8%; aluminium rose slightly but nickel declined 0.8%, with broad risk-off sentiment. | Nespresso and Amcor received an EAFA award for an aluminium coffee capsule using ASI-certified aluminium. The piece highlights sustainability, chain-of-custody certification, and responsible sourcing across the aluminium value chain. It does not report any supply disruption, quota, tariff, or demand shift that would affect near-term aluminium prices. | Copper prices extended losses as traders feared a second wave of coronavirus infections would delay global economic recovery. Three-month LME copper fell 0.6% and ShFE copper slipped 0.5%. The piece cites risk-off market moves and also shows smaller simultaneous declines in aluminum and nickel. | Copper eased on fears of a second COVID-19 wave in China and South Korea, prompting fresh restrictions and expected lower demand for base metals. U.S. warnings about lockdown rollbacks reinforced demand concerns. The selloff spilled into other industrial metals, with aluminium and nickel also falling on the day.

LME and ShFE copper edged higher as traders pointed to steady demand from China and factory restarts, with arbitrage helping prices. However, the outlook remains clouded by the Fed’s warnings of prolonged weak growth and pandemic-driven demand destruction. The piece also references moves in aluminium and nickel. | Copper prices fell on LME and ShFE as fears of prolonged weak U.S. growth—stemming from the Fed’s comments on the coronavirus fallout—raised concerns over metal demand. The piece also cites pandemic-driven demand destruction and reports concurrent moves in nickel and aluminium prices, pointing to broadly bearish near-term sentiment. | Blackstone Minerals announced strong drilling results at its King Cobra nickel-PGM project in Vietnam, including ~38m at 1.12% nickel from near-surface and additional thick, higher-grade intervals. The deposit remains open and the company is progressing scoping and resource work, supporting incremental bullish sentiment for nickel. | Copper prices extended declines for a fourth session as the Fed warned of prolonged weak U.S. growth amid coronavirus fallout. Concerns about a possible second COVID wave in China and renewed U.S.-China frictions weighed on demand sentiment. LME copper inventories jumped 25% and market structure stayed in deep contango. | A Fact.MR press release says aluminum casting demand is stagnant amid the COVID-19 pandemic. It attributes the weakness to reduced workforces and ongoing logistical challenges, projecting such conditions through 2020. The article implies near-term soft demand for aluminium castings, weighing on aluminium consumption expectations. | Fact.MR says aluminum castings demand is stagnant amid COVID-19, with the automotive industry facing major shocks. Oversupply from China plus travel restrictions, reduced workforce, and logistics issues are highlighted as concerns. The study expects a recovery by end-2020, but Q2 2020 demand remains weak. | Australia’s government announced a long-term production credit to help aluminium smelters qualify for support while transitioning to reliable renewable electricity, targeting lower-carbon output. Rio Tinto said the scheme will help sustain and grow smelting operations and investment. The policy is intended to strengthen supply competitiveness for global low-carbon aluminium demand. | En+ Group supports a Carbon Trust report proposing universal standards for labeling “low-carbon” primary aluminium, including a 4.5 tCO2/t threshold and cradle-to-gate accounting. The focus is transparency for buyers and potential product differentiation, but the note does not indicate any immediate supply or demand shock for aluminium prices.

China’s NBS data show primary aluminium production of 2.97 million mt in April and 11.83 million mt in Jan–Apr, up 2.4% year-on-year. Production of 10 nonferrous metals (including copper and nickel) also rose to 4.93 million mt in April, suggesting supply is increasing, a bearish signal for prices.

SMM says nonferrous metals rose on Monday morning as oil prices jumped and the US dollar index slipped. The LME complex reversed a red week, with nickel rebounding and copper and aluminium also higher. SHFE positioning was mixed, but copper and aluminium showed recovery signals despite recent weakness. | Copper prices rose 1.3% on the LME as global equities improved with economies reopening after COVID lockdowns. However, China’s copper demand is expected to slow slightly in May. The article also notes increases in LME aluminum and nickel, signaling broader industrial metal support.

Lindian Resources reports “stunning” alumina grades from drilling at extensions (Bouba North and Bouba South) of its Gaoual bauxite project in Guinea, with many samples averaging above 50% and up to 58.5% alumina. Follow-up digestion tests are planned as the company prepares a JORC resource update. | London copper rose to a two-month high on optimism over very early COVID-19 vaccine data, supporting expectations of a faster economic recovery. China stimulus hopes also added support. U.S.-China tensions and yuan/equities pullbacks capped gains, but LME and Shanghai aluminum and nickel also edged higher. | Korean aluminum producers have jointly opposed a large Chinese aluminum manufacturer, Henan Mingtai Aluminum, which signed an investment plan in 2018 to build an aluminum products plant in South Jeolla’s Gwangyang Bay area. The project, opposed by local industry, raises concerns about Chinese supply entering Korea and affecting prices. | The live futures report says 26.5kt inflows are capping gains in LME aluminium, implying reduced upside pressure. It also states nickel is up 1.6%, indicating positive near-term momentum. The piece therefore signals offsetting short-term price pressure across aluminium and nickel. | The live futures report notes that LME aluminium gains are limited by a 26.5kt inflow, implying near-term supply/flow pressure weighing on upside. Nickel, by contrast, rises 1.6%, signaling stronger demand/positioning support for nickel prices over the same short horizon. | Lindian Resources reported ultra-high-grade alumina results from drilling at its Gaoual conglomerate bauxite project in Guinea, with alumina grades up to 58.5%. The company says resources have grown without grade loss and plans further digestion tests for silica classification ahead of a mid-year JORC resource estimate.

Coronavirus lockdowns and border closures disrupting transport have raised sulphur and granulated sulphur prices and, by extension, sulphuric-acid costs for DRC miners. With sulphuric acid used to extract copper and cobalt, delayed reagent shipments may force mines into more expensive, potentially constrained operations, weighing on copper supply expectations. | Alumina says job retention is vital as governments consider another round of taxpayer support to keep Alcoa’s Portland aluminium smelter running after subsidies expire next year. The company cites ongoing low aluminium prices and challenging trading conditions, but highlights the need for reliable, lower-cost power and continued operation. | Portland aluminium smelter owners say Australia’s economy restart needs better energy policy and avoiding further job losses as Alumina Limited and Alcoa seek a government-backed lifeline for the loss-making plant. The negotiations highlight operational risk from high power costs, affecting near-term aluminium supply expectations.

Copper prices extended gains as hopes for a rapid global economic recovery lifted risk sentiment, pushing London copper to a more than two-month high. ShFE copper also rose sharply. The article adds that China’s copper smelting recovered in April and strengthened in May so far, supporting demand expectations. | SMM notes volatile but generally firmer base-metals trading ahead of China’s “two sessions,” with oil prices rising and a slightly stronger dollar. LME copper and aluminium gained about 1.7%-1.8% on Wednesday; SHFE copper and aluminium rose too. SHFE nickel jumped more than 2%-3% before easing. | SMM data shows China’s 6063 aluminium billet inventories fell further in the week to May 21, dropping to 92,800 mt from May 14. Stocks declined in four of five major consumption areas, with Foshan down sharply, while arrivals stayed limited. The drawdown suggests tighter near-term supply conditions for aluminium. | WBMS reported a 709,000 mt global primary aluminium surplus in Q1 2020 versus a 761,000 mt surplus for all of 2019. Demand rose 3.5% y/y but production grew faster (5.8%), and reported stocks rebounded to 2.17 million mt by end-March, suggesting near-term price pressure. | China’s bauxite imports hit a record 28.41 million mt in Q1, driven by higher demand and lower sea freight costs, supporting alumina feed supply for aluminium. However, the appeal of imported bauxite is fading as domestic ore losses widen, caustic soda/coal prices fall, and ~2 million mt/year of imported-bauxite alumina capacity went offline. | Rusal said it expects all its aluminium to meet “low carbon footprint” criteria by 2023. The move is aimed at aligning output with buyer and policy preferences for lower-emissions aluminium. While it may support demand for compliant metal, the article does not signal immediate supply disruption or urgent price pressure. | Technavio’s report forecasts growth in the aluminum welding wires market, attributing demand to stricter vehicle-emissions rules pushing lightweight, fuel-efficient vehicles and to investment in EV charging and power infrastructure. The press release expects COVID-19 effects to be most significant in Q1, with gradual easing. | FMI revised its forecast for the alumina trihydrate market, citing the continuing rapid expansion of the COVID-19 pandemic. Because alumina trihydrate is a key feedstock for aluminium refining, the update likely shifts near-term expectations for aluminium input availability and demand, weighing on prices.

SMM reports broad declines in base metals amid US-China tensions, China’s “two sessions” and weaker economic data. SHFE nickel hit limit down, while SHFE copper slipped and SHFE aluminium rose slightly. LME copper fell and LME aluminium gained modestly. Moves largely reflect macro sentiment and positioning, not fresh fundamentals. | Copper prices fell 1.9% on the LME and 2.1% on the Shanghai Futures Exchange after China omitted an annual GDP target and U.S.-Sino tensions rose. Nickel dropped more than 4–5% and aluminium slipped slightly, reflecting risk-off sentiment, though contracts were still positioned for a weekly gain. | Copper prices slid about 2% as investors feared renewed U.S.-China tensions, possible sanctions, and renewed protests weighing on global growth sentiment. China’s lack of an annual growth target and demand concerns pressured copper. Aluminium and nickel also fell, with LME and SHFE declines reflecting broader selling and inventory signals.

LIVE FUTURES REPORT shows LME copper price up 1.5%, pointing to immediate bullish momentum. It also reports a 25k inflow of aluminium at Port Klang, which implies increased nearby aluminium supply/inventory and may limit upside for aluminium. Other items are mainly data/methodology updates. | A live LME futures update shows copper up 1.5%, pointing to near-term strength in copper prices. It also reports a 25k aluminium inflow at Port Klang, which generally increases available metal at the port and may weigh on aluminium pricing. Net impact is mixed across metals. | SMM reported SHFE nonferrous prices were mostly higher on May 26. Copper recovered 0.94% and aluminium gained 0.9%, with copper bouncing above the five-day moving average and briefly testing session highs. Nickel edged slightly down (-0.01%). Gains were capped as US-China tensions kept investors cautious.

Copper trades in a tight range as hopes for reopening are offset by renewed fears that U.S.-China friction could weaken global activity. Reports of possible U.S. action over Hong Kong heighten uncertainty. LME aluminium edges up, while nickel falls, indicating broadly mixed risk sentiment. | The article states that the Covid-19 outbreak has affected the aluminium extruded products market, shifting market share. This implies reduced consumption and demand disruption for aluminium extrusions in the short term, which can pressure aluminium prices downward during the initial outbreak period. | APAA urges the Trump administration to restore Section 232 tariffs by repealing Canada’s exemption, citing a surge of Canadian aluminum imports hurting U.S. primary smelters. The group warns of potential shutdowns and requests reimposing a 10% tariff to reduce import pressure and protect domestic aluminum production.

Fastmarkets issued a pricing notice saying it corrected and updated several base metal assessment entries due to procedural errors, including European low-carbon aluminium differentials and aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premium midpoint assessments. It also references nickel arbitrage methodology versus SHFE and LME, but no market-moving fundamentals were provided. | Reportlinker’s article forecasts growth in the global aluminum castings market amid COVID-19 and recession concerns, citing projected expansion in die casting and regional demand outlooks. It highlights drivers like lightweighting and automotive/electrical applications but provides no aluminum supply disruptions, inventory data, or pricing catalysts for the metal itself. | Pan Global Resources announced additional drill results at Spain’s Escacena project, extending a shallow copper mineralized zone and confirming multiple copper horizons associated with EM conductors. Intersections include notable copper grades, but it is an exploration campaign with no production timing, offering limited short-term impact on metal prices. | SMM says SHFE base metals ended mixed amid reopening optimism and US-China friction. Copper fell 0.34% after Peru’s Antamina resumed operations, raising oversupply worries, though China inventories supported prices. Aluminium rose 1.47% for a fifth day as Chinese primary ingot and billet inventories fell. Nickel added 0.13%.

Shanghai aluminum futures rose to a near three-month high as demand recovered in top consumer China and aluminum stockpiles fell. ShFE’s most-traded contract hit 13,190 yuan/tonne. LME three-month aluminium also nudged higher, supported by inventory declines and demand-positive sentiment. | London aluminum prices rose and were set for the strongest monthly gain in 16 months, supported by a recovery in China demand. The move was also linked to a quick decline in ShFE aluminum inventories and improving sentiment. LME and ShFE aluminum contracts tested recent highs as trading increased. | China is preparing for record primary aluminum import volumes after post-coronavirus recovery creates a Shanghai–LME price spread. Traders say sizable inflows, including Russian metal, could enter in May-June. The extra supply may lift stocks and reduce the rally in Shanghai aluminum as the arbitrage narrows or closes. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly rose on May 29. Copper gained 0.73% supported by decreasing domestic inventories; aluminium rose 0.84% amid tighter spot availability. Nickel edged down 0.13%. Broader support came from improved economic sentiment, though US-China tensions capped gains.

ReportLinker released a report forecasting growth in the global aluminum market for packaging from 2020-2024, citing rising demand for sustainable packaging and aluminum containers for liquid products. The piece is forward-looking and does not provide immediate supply, inventory, or pricing changes that could strongly shift near-term aluminum prices.

Copper rose to an 11-week high as signs of China’s economic recovery strengthened, including higher May vehicle sales and no new COVID cases in Wuhan. Analysts cited strong physical demand and tight scrap supplies prompting short covering. Per reports, LME aluminum and nickel also edged higher.

The piece cites Morgan Stanley views that copper prices should remain resilient despite China-linked downside risk, forecasting strengthening later in 2020. For nickel, it highlights Indonesia’s ore export ban and pandemic mine closures tightening supply, but also weak end-demand, leading to a more mixed outlook. | Maoist rebels in Jharkhand torched 11 vehicles, including nine excavators and other equipment, used in bauxite mining at Pakhar mines in Lohardaga district. The attack targeted operations of two private companies. Police said an investigation is underway, potentially disrupting local bauxite output and supply to aluminium production. | The live futures report highlights that LME copper is consolidating above $5,500/t, suggesting near-term price support. It also discusses aluminium market conditions—such as supply remaining broadly intact and earlier shipping disruptions—though the excerpt does not provide a direct aluminium price direction. Zinc is mentioned but not tracked here. | Lanka Aluminium Industries said Sri Lanka’s aluminium extrusion market could shrink about 40% due to COVID-19’s hit to the property and construction sector. Curfews forced the company to close its factory and sales reportedly stopped in April and May, with demand only starting to return partially by May. | Constellium says Airbus has awarded a multi-year, multi-program contract worth a disclosed value to supply advanced aerospace aluminium rolled and extruded components, including wing and fuselage panels and aluminium-lithium solutions. Element 13 plans to build a 60,000 sq ft aluminum billet facility in Richmond, Indiana, using recycled aluminum scrap as its feedstock. The project involves about $15.8 million investment and a state/local tax grant to offset costs. The new capacity could modestly influence near-term aluminium supply expectations.

London copper was set for a third straight weekly gain as economies reopen and China demand recovers, supported by government stimulus and businesses rebuilding output after lockdowns. While no nationalization is confirmed, the episode highlights political risk to nickel operations, and could weigh on aluminium via Rusal’s dividend links. | Alcoa began layoffs at its Intalco aluminum smelter in Ferndale, Washington, ahead of schedule, signaling the end of most jobs for the plant’s ~700 workers. The state had offered assistance for a restart or sale, but officials say appeals fell through, implying reduced aluminum supply.

China’s exports of unwrought aluminium and aluminium products fell again in May, down 28.56% YoY to 382,900 mt, after a smaller decline in April. Cumulative exports for January–May were down 18.6% YoY to about 2.01 million mt, signaling weaker trade demand and putting pressure on aluminium prices. | London copper slipped from a three-month high as data showed China’s exports weakened in May, raising short-term demand concerns. Traders expect a rebound supported by stimulus measures and recovery hopes, though resistance is seen at higher price levels. Shanghai aluminium surged to a 4-1/2-month high as strong China demand lifted the July contract on the SHFE. London aluminium posted a sixth straight gain. Rising Chinese spot premiums, firmer European premiums, and Chinese aluminium stockpiles falling to a 4-1/2-month low reinforced the bullish tone. | Shanghai aluminum futures hit a 4-1/2-month peak as China downstream users de-stocked and began a new restocking cycle, supporting expectations of consistent demand. Shanghai copper surged to a 20-week high on strong China demand as lockdowns eased, supported by low on-warrant LME stocks and reduced ShFE inventories. LME copper also rose. The piece cites strong ShFE aluminum prices but falling nickel, reflecting mixed cross-metal reactions. | ABB was appointed to install ring-geared mill drives and control systems at Zijin Mining’s Veliki Krivelj copper mine in Serbia, part of a broader investment to expand and transform the Bor copper mine and smelter. The upgrades target higher productivity, reduced downtime and better energy efficiency, supporting copper production outlook.

Rail damage has halted supply to Russia’s top alumina plant, according to the article title. The stoppage can interrupt alumina feedstock deliveries to smelters, reducing aluminium output prospects in the short term and supporting higher aluminium prices over the coming days. | SMM reports that recycled aluminium substitution for primary metal is expected to remain steady long term, but China’s aluminium users temporarily shifted back toward primary due to scrap import/policy changes, COVID disruptions, and sharp falls in aluminium prices. Secondary plants increased primary use, while later reductions occurred as premiums narrowed. | China’s aluminium billet inventories rebounded in the week ended June 11, rising 900 mt to 74,000 mt. The increase was driven by growth in arrivals and reduced warehouse shipments. Stocks rose in Wuxi, Changzhou and Nanchang, while Foshan and Huzhou declined slightly.

London copper was flat at $5,764.50/t and was on track for a fourth straight weekly gain, driven by hopes that central-bank stimulus could lift demand despite a gloomy global outlook. It notes a lack of Fed purchases, some USD support, and shows aluminum and nickel falling in separate moves. | Fastmarkets/Fastmarkets-Metal Bulletin content notes procedural corrections and schedule changes to aluminium and other metal assessments, but also comments that aluminium has been “hard to move.” It references curtailed Gulf output, drawn-down inventories, and severely disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, implying some supply constraints without clear immediate price direction. | Banks and investors are joining commodity traders to stockpile aluminum amid a large surplus. Storage arbitrage is profitable because spot aluminum trades below three-month forwards, after the pandemic hit auto and aerospace demand. Discounting and hedging can increase apparent supply, weighing near-term aluminum prices.

China’s primary aluminium output in May was 2.98 million mt, up 0.4% from April but down 0.1% year on year, per NBS data. January–May production rose 2.1% but slower than earlier months. This suggests slightly tighter YoY aluminium supply. | Copper prices fell 1.9% as renewed coronavirus worries raised uncertainty about how quickly demand will recover. China’s manufacturing recovery looked fragile despite increased production. Chile miner unions also sought updated continuity plans amid rising worker cases. Aluminium and nickel also dropped on weaker sentiment and global demand concerns.

Copper rose 1.1% on the LME as investors welcomed the Fed’s widened bond-buying program, boosting risk-on sentiment. LME/Shanghai aluminium and nickel also increased alongside copper. However, China spot copper remained at a widening discount to futures, hinting weaker physical demand. | SMM says SHFE nonferrous prices mostly rose after overnight recovery and supportive macro sentiment from the Fed. LME copper gained on optimism around OPEC+ cuts and crude demand, while Chile extended a coronavirus catastrophe, raising copper-ore supply concerns. Aluminium and nickel also rebounded, with aluminium supported by positioning and fundamentals. | Copper prices rose after the Federal Reserve widened its corporate bond buying, supporting U.S. risk sentiment and coinciding with a weaker dollar. The LME and Shanghai contracts both gained. The small eighth batch highlights tight oversight as China targets zero scrap imports by year-end but allows compliant scrap under new July 1 standards, influencing near-term supply expectations.

The USMCA interim Uniform Regulations clarify how vehicle producers must meet Article 6 originating steel and aluminum purchase requirements, including eligible products, calculation methods, and documentation timing. The rules take effect July 1, 2020 with a six-month grace period, potentially boosting expectations for USMCA-compliant aluminium demand. | Copper prices rose after trial results for dexamethasone improved COVID-19 treatment expectations and sentiment, alongside a sharp rebound in U.S. retail sales indicating economic recovery. The report cites higher LME and Shanghai copper prices and also shows aluminium rising, implying broader risk-on support for industrial metals. | London copper rose modestly on hopes for U.S. demand recovery and potential infrastructure spending, plus a COVID-19 treatment breakthrough. Gains were capped by a growing China coronavirus outbreak, raising second-wave fears. Aluminum prices also edged higher, but overall sentiment is mixed between U.S. stimulus optimism and China demand disruption risk. | SMM says operating rates at major aluminium processors are largely stable (avg ~79.4%), but high aluminium prices are cooling downstream buying. Finished inventories are expected to rise, and new orders slow—especially exports—while billet processing fees decline and substitution of scrap for primary likely weakens further in June. | Blackstone Minerals’ maiden drilling at the Ban Chang West prospect in Vietnam returned a high-grade massive sulphide zone, including 0.66% nickel over 5m and an interval up to 2.98% nickel plus 1.22% copper. Management says results could add high-grade feed near its Ban Phuc facility, boosting nickel and copper sentiment. | Alcoa will begin a formal 30-day consultation with Spain’s works council on collective dismissals tied to significant recurring losses at its San Ciprián aluminum smelter. The restructuring could retain part of the casthouse, but smelter output may be reduced, pressuring short-term aluminum supply expectations. | SMM says China’s east-coast spot aluminium premiums rose for a second day, reaching about 200–220 yuan/mt over the July SHFE contract, near May’s peak. Availability remains tight, with sellers holding firm and some delaying shipments. Downstream buying is weaker, while social inventories have fallen sharply. | Alcoa says it will start a formal consultation process for collective dismissals at its San Ciprián aluminum smelter in Spain on June 25, following earlier talks with worker representatives. The restructuring would keep part of the casthouse running, but the plant has recurring losses, suggesting possible supply tightening for aluminium. | U.S. aluminum producers are again lobbying for tariffs on Canadian aluminum as the new NAFTA nears implementation. Analysts say Chicago aluminum futures have risen as markets price likely quotas or restrictions. Supporters cite a Canadian export surge harming U.S. smelters, while opponents argue the impact is overstated and consumer costs rise. | Alcoa plans to start a formal consultation process from June 25 that could result in cutting up to 534 jobs at its San Ciprian aluminum smelter in Spain. The move follows May talks citing significant and unsustainable circumstances and continuing recurring losses. The alumina refinery is not affected.

London copper prices fell as rising COVID-19 cases in Beijing renewed lockdown fears that could hit demand. China also signaled monetary easing support, but risk sentiment turned cautious. The article notes concurrent easing in LME nickel and weakness in ShFE aluminium, indicating broad metal pressure. | An SMM survey says China’s secondary aluminium operating rates rose to 48.69% in May, supported by moderately improving auto sales linked to stimulus. Meanwhile, foreign aluminium alloy ingot imports did not significantly pressure producers in May, though increasing imports in June may slightly reduce operating rates among smaller firms. | SMM said China’s aluminium billet inventories fell 2,500 mt in the week ended June 18 to 71,500 mt, after a 900 mt build the prior week. Shipments increased from warehouses but arrivals were limited across major consuming regions, with Foshan showing the largest drop.

The Trump administration is considering reimposing tariffs on Canadian aluminum and steel imports after talks with Ottawa, citing surges in shipments. U.S. producers are lobbying for action under a 2019 tariff-reinstatement clause tied to export spikes. The possibility triggers market anxiety in Canada amid election-year uncertainty. | London copper was little changed amid mixed sentiment: worries about a potential second COVID-19 wave offset by hopes for economic recovery. China copper premiums and spot-futures spreads rose, pointing to near-term tightness. The report also notes aluminium easing, with prices moving modestly in both directions. | CRU reports US AD/CVD petitions against common alloy aluminium sheet from 18 countries, with preliminary AD set for Aug 17 and preliminary CVD decisions due Aug 7. Imports from in-scope countries are already down sharply year-to-date, and an affirmative ruling could remove most 2019 import volumes, tightening supply for US buyers. | Technavio forecasts growth in the global aluminum alloy wheel market, attributing demand to lightweight automotive designs that improve fuel economy and meet emission standards. Overall, the report points to higher aluminum alloy wheel production, supporting aluminum consumption expectations in the near term. | Trigon Metals agreed to acquire Technomine Africa’s 100% interest in Morocco’s Silver Hill copper-silver project. London copper fell as accelerating COVID-19 cases raised worries about a quicker recovery and near-term demand. LME three-month copper eased to $5,846/ton. China prices were supported by low inventories and potential Chile supply disruption, while LME aluminum slipped and LME nickel declined. | SMM data show China’s social inventories of primary aluminium ingots fell 17,000 mt from June 18 to 737,000 mt as of June 22, across eight consumption areas including SHFE warrants. Copper prices fell after a U.S. official said the U.S.-China trade deal is “over,” raising concerns about the global economic outlook and worsening sentiment as the dollar firmed. The LME copper contract reversed recent gains. The article also notes declines in LME aluminium and nickel amid broader risk concerns. | The U.S. is reportedly preparing to announce aluminum tariffs on Canada by the end of the week. An announcement could come by end of the week, with tariffs effective July 1, ahead of a new USMCA timeline. U.S. aluminum demand weakened during the pandemic, and prices fell. | The headline says the US will announce aluminum tariffs on Canada by the end of the week. Aluminium tariff news is also cited. | U.S. officials are considering reimposing tariffs on Canadian aluminum unless Ottawa agrees to export quotas by July 1. U.S. aluminum producers are lobbying for action, while industry groups argue over whether imports have surged. | Copper prices rose 0.4% to near a five-month high after Trump said the U.S.-China trade deal is intact and European PMIs beat expectations. Analysts cited Chile supply disruptions, falling LME copper inventories, and stronger Chinese demand and import premiums. Aluminium tariff re-imposition plans were also reported as a support factor. | Constellium announced the launch of “Aheadd,” a new high-performance aluminium powder offering for additive manufacturing, and said it is working with Poly-Shape (AddUp) to develop components using these powders. Industry groups are split, but the tariff threat is likely supportive for aluminum prices. | BNN Bloomberg reports that the U.S. may announce aluminum tariffs on Canada this week. If Canada does not agree to aluminum export restrictions, the U.S. would impose 10% tariffs starting Friday and implement them by July 1, affecting trade and pricing expectations. | Canada’s official said Ottawa believes its aluminum exports do not harm the U.S. market, after Bloomberg reported Washington planned to reimpose tariffs. Canada signaled it would defend its aluminum sector and emphasized the export impact to U.S. partners, highlighting renewed trade risk for aluminum flows. | The Trump administration is considering reinstating tariffs on Canadian aluminum amid concerns that Canadian exports to the U.S. are rising, ahead of the USMCA entering into force July 1. Companies are split, with some seeking protection while critics warn retaliation and another trade war could pressure aluminium sentiment. | Canada’s Treasury Board President Jean‑Yves Duclos said federal officials would oppose any new U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum, after a Bloomberg report claimed the U.S. could impose a 10% tariff on Canadian aluminum imports. By signaling resistance to potential tariff imposition, the news improves near-term expectations for aluminum trade conditions and limits the risk of demand destruction that tariffs could cause. | The article features a trade lawyer commenting on potential aluminum tariffs. Such tariff developments can quickly change expectations for cross-border aluminum supply, pricing, and downstream demand, influencing near-term aluminium market sentiment. Depending on scope and timing, tariffs could add costs or curb trade volumes, weighing on aluminium prices. | Canada denies that its aluminum exports harm the U.S. market while U.S. officials may reimpose tariffs on Canadian aluminium imports. The dispute follows complaints from U.S. primary producers about surging Canadian inflows. Chalco said it will receive its first large bauxite shipment from Boffa, Guinea, and the project is on track to complete two months ahead of schedule. China’s aluminum production is rebounding and growing toward its 45Mt capacity cap, with new smelters coming online. Western smelters face margin stress from low prices and rising LME inventories. Even as macro concerns and a stronger dollar capped gains, copper sentiment remained supported by constrained stocks. | Constellium will lead a £15 million ALIVE project to design, engineer, and prototype structural aluminium battery enclosures for electric vehicles. Copper prices extended gains for a sixth straight week as supply fears rose with coronavirus disruptions in key producing countries. China’s May bauxite imports fell 3.98% m/m to 9.54 million mt, with Guinea volumes down as refiners cut output on cost concerns. A tender has been launched for a consulting office, with bids due 15 July, and participants included the World Bank, AfDB, IFC, EBRD and EIB. | Copper is set for its biggest quarterly gain in a decade as China’s demand rebounds, inventories at LME and ShFE warehouses fall, and COVID-related disruptions hit supply in major producing countries like Chile and Peru. SMM reports SHFE/LME base metals were mixed, but copper and aluminium extended gains after China manufacturing PMI beat expectations, boosting demand sentiment, while the Fed signaled low rates and liquidity support. SMM reports mixed SHFE base-metal trading as investors assess US economic data and coronavirus vaccine optimism. Copper and aluminium rose modestly, with copper reaching a multi-month high on the LME/SHFE. Nickel rebounded slightly but is described as rangebound without major fundamentals driving a sustained move. | SMM data show China’s social inventories of primary aluminium ingots across eight consumption areas fell to 719,000 mt as of July 2, down 3,000 mt from June 24. The prior week saw a larger 32,000 mt decline, indicating continued but slower inventory tightening. | China’s new import standards for high-grade copper and aluminium scrap were published but have not yet been implemented at customs because procedures are still being finalized. Until then, imports remain under quota restrictions, with aluminium scrap quotas for Q3 barely changing from Q2. This reduces near-term clarity for scrap supply flows. | SMM reports that China’s 6063 aluminium billet inventories increased 6,900 mt from June 24 to 73,200 mt as of July 2, following a prior weekly decline. The rise was driven by increased arrivals, with Foshan still falling while other consumption areas built up stocks. | China’s planned July 1 customs implementation of new high-grade copper and aluminium scrap standards is delayed because import procedures are not finalized. China aims to cut solid waste imports to zero by year-end, but quotas for aluminium scrap are expected to change little. | Copper prices rose for a sixth session as COVID-19 disruptions in Chile, the top global producer, increased perceived supply risk. BHP plans to ramp down Cerro Colorado activity and Codelco will close refinery and foundry operations at Chuquicamata. The piece argues industrial metals are tight as China recovers and pandemic-related disruptions persist. It highlights falling copper inventories, LME backwardation and deficit forecasts, pointing to supported copper prices. Nickel is linked to hard-hit supply from disruptions and an expected 2021 recovery. Aluminium is only indirectly referenced via alumina producers. | London copper is little changed in early Asia but is set for a seventh weekly gain as traders focus on Chile supply worries, despite Chile keeping May output steady versus a year earlier. Copper is supported by higher LME/SHFE prices and ongoing China smelter TC/RC discussions for Q3. | The U.S. is threatening new tariffs on Canadian aluminum exports, citing a surge of Canadian metal that allegedly caused price collapse and threatened U.S. primary producers. Canadian producers say the move is contradictory, while U.S. producers argue Canadian imports have caused price to collapse. The article discusses smelting constraints, warehousing, and Glencore/Rusal trade links tied to tariff impacts. | Copper prices fell as traders saw the recent rally extending too far amid worries about China demand, including a summer construction slowdown and rising Shanghai-tracked inventories. The article highlights policy uncertainty but links directly to tariffs. | Trudeau said he is weighing whether to attend a USMCA summit in Washington, citing concern over possible U.S. tariffs on aluminum and steel. China’s alumina rally accelerated after Chalco announced “flexible production” output cuts at three alumina lines in Shanxi. The move is expected to tighten alumina supply and support downstream aluminium sentiment. | Copper prices jumped to five-month highs as investors priced recovering demand from top consumer China and weighed COVID-19 risks to Chilean mine supply. LME copper rose about 1.7% to around $6,118/t. Aluminium and nickel were also higher, reflecting broader support for industrial metals. | The U.S. is threatening fresh tariffs on Canadian aluminum, influenced by COVID-driven demand shifts, trading/warehousing dynamics, and pressure from U.S. primary producers. The article cites a Canadian import surge that contributed to price collapse, and links tariff policy to political and corporate structures around Glencore and Russian aluminum supply.

London copper rose to a five-month high, up as much as 0.7% on strong U.S. services activity and a risk-on economic rebound narrative. LME copper inventories fell to the lowest since mid-March, reinforcing demand sentiment. Nickel ticked higher while aluminium edged down amid the same market shift. | Some Japanese aluminium buyers agreed to pay a $79/tonne premium for July–September primary aluminium shipments, down 3.7% from the prior quarter. China policy and economic data boosted sentiment, while aluminium edged lower and nickel rose. | Shanghai copper rose to a 2020 high as China economic recovery hopes combined with supply fears supported sentiment. The group argues recent Canadian subsidies and production restart boosted flows, and seeks trade enforcement to support domestic producers. | Rio Tinto said it plans to close the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter in August 2021, and the New Zealand government appears to accept the decision. The smelter employs more than 1,000 people directly, indicating a meaningful production cut that can affect aluminium supply expectations. | Rio Tinto says it has begun plans to wind down and close the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter after giving Meridian Energy notice to terminate its power contract. Closing the smelter reduces aluminium output and supply availability, which can be bullish for aluminium prices in the near term. | Rio Tinto says New Zealand’s only aluminium smelter at Tiwai Point will wind down operations in August 2021. That expectation would likely be supportive for aluminium pricing in the days following the announcement. | Rio Tinto plans to shut down its New Zealand aluminium smelter, raising concerns about jobs and the local economy. The reported closure implies reduced aluminium production capacity, which can tighten supply expectations in the short term and provide support for aluminium prices. | Trustpower notes Rio Tinto’s announcement to wind down operations at the Tiwai aluminium smelter (NZAS), with expected closure in August 2021. The smelter shutdown implies reduced aluminium supply. | South Port says the NZAS Tiwai Point aluminium smelter closure in August 2021 will end a major customer relationship and reduce smelter-linked cargo. Canadian officials are urging the U.S. not to move forward with unjustified tariffs. | SMM data shows China’s primary aluminium ingot inventories fell for the second consecutive week. The decline followed a rise in east-China shipments, with stocks dropping in Wuxi, Changzhou, and Nanchang, while Foshan and Huzhou saw gains. | Rio Tinto says it will wind down and eventually close New Zealand Aluminium Smelters (NZAS) at Tiwai Point after a strategic review. The company cites persistent losses, high energy costs, and inability to secure a lower power price. It notes large-scale infrastructure replacement and renovation programs, implying rising demand for aluminium conductors, while expecting aluminium prices to remain stable. | Copper prices rose to a 14-month high on hopes of a faster China recovery and concerns over disruptions in Chile, the world’s biggest producer. LME copper climbed on higher cash premiums and falling on-warrant inventories. China factory price softness was partly offset by improving sector signs; speculators increased net longs. | New Zealand’s Tiwai Point aluminium smelter is set to close, cutting about 1,000 jobs directly and 1,600 in supply chains, despite government subsidy support. While the shutdown is planned by next year, it signals falling aluminium production and supply constraints. | New Zealand power companies are assessing where electricity prices will go after Rio Tinto plans to close its Tiwai Point aluminium smelter. The news indicates reduced aluminium refining capacity, which would tighten supply expectations and likely support aluminium prices over the coming weeks.

London copper was steady but poised for an eighth straight weekly gain as China’s demand improved and COVID-related mine disruptions cut supply, with visible inventories down sharply since March. Three-month LME copper held near recent highs. Aluminium also rose, supported by demand recovery and higher alumina costs. | Japanese buyers agreed a July–September aluminium premium of $79/t over the LME cash price, down 3.7% from the prior quarter and the fourth straight quarterly decline. The firm says domestic supply can’t meet U.S. demand and warns Canada may retaliate, risking broader trade-war spillovers. | Southland mayors are urging the government and Rio Tinto to keep the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter open, after Rio Tinto said it plans to close in August next year due to high energy costs and a difficult outlook. Mayors seek more information and a chance to negotiate potential retention options. | U.S. political pressure is reviving talk of reimposing tariffs on Canadian aluminum under the U.S.-Mexico-Canada framework. Glencore is a major trader and investor in a U.S. aluminum producer lobbying for tariffs. Market participants link the renewed tariff speculation to rising U.S. forward premiums despite weak physical demand from COVID.

SMM says nonferrous metals traded mostly higher, with copper leading on growing mine-supply concerns, including strike action at Antofagasta’s Zaldivar mine in Chile. Rio Tinto will close New Zealand Aluminium Smelters’ Tiwai Point smelter within 18 months, citing high energy costs and a challenging outlook for aluminium. The closure follows a reported hit from falling global aluminium prices and comes amid broader COVID-19-driven industrial slowdowns, weighing on aluminium market sentiment. | A UN investigation reports that criminal gangs largely control some gold, diamond and bauxite mines in Venezuela’s Amazon and that workers face exploitation and violence. It also says security and military forces fail to prevent crimes. The bauxite finding implies potential disruption to supply affecting aluminium/alumina expectations in coming weeks. | Copper steadied after earlier losses tied to U.S.-China tensions, trading up slightly on the LME ahead of Chinese manufacturing data. The piece cites strong recent demand indicators but limits further upside. It also highlights Chile COVID-related disruption/safety concerns and mentions marginal aluminium and nickel moves. | Pennsylvania’s administration announced plans by a Poland-based manufacturer to establish an aluminum can manufacturing facility and a North American center of excellence in Lackawanna County, creating over 400 jobs.
