Producers cite capacity constraints for can manufacturing, not shortage of aluminum metal. | The piece claims nationwide shortages are already affecting products packaged in aluminium cans, alongside other pantry staples. Such packaging shortages suggest aluminium supply constraints and tighter near-term availability for canstock, which can support aluminium prices in the coming days as buyers adjust to limited supply. | Alcoa shares jumped after the aluminum and alumina producer reported a narrower Q2 loss, slightly beat sales expectations, and noted lower production costs, stable shipments, and improved liquidity. It kept 2020 bauxite/alumina/aluminum shipment guidance and flagged higher Q3 aluminum results, despite higher energy costs in Australia.

SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals opened lower and extended losses, driven by risk aversion over COVID-19 and US-China tensions. Copper fell as much as ~2.8% in early trade, with nickel down >1% and aluminium down ~1.2%. LME also closed mostly weaker for copper, nickel, and aluminium. | London copper extended losses as concerns about China’s economic recovery and demand durability weighed on prospects for a rebound. Rising COVID-19 cases and U.S.-China tensions reduced expectations for metal consumption, dragging the market. Nickel and aluminium also fell, reflecting broader bearish sentiment across base metals. | New Zealand’s coalition partners are split over the fate of the Rio Tinto-owned Tiwai Point aluminium smelter. The plant said production ends in 14 months, risking job losses. New Zealand First urges government action to “save” it, while Ardern emphasizes transitions and avoiding bailouts. The uncertainty affects aluminium supply expectations. | The piece argues that despite some demand recovery signals, China is raising aluminium production and supported by low-cost finance and energy, while global demand remains weak. It warns India may face higher imports and price pressure as Chinese surplus is redirected to world markets, keeping aluminium prices low. | SMM data show China’s primary aluminium ingot social inventories fell to 708,000 mt as of July 16, down 4,000 mt week-on-week across eight consumption areas, including SHFE warrants. Jaguar Land Rover is piloting an aluminium upcycling “Reality” project that mixes recycled automotive scrap with less primary aluminium to form a comparable prototype alloy. Phil Scott joined New Hampshire and Maine officials in urging the Trump administration to remove a reinstated 10% tariff on Canadian aluminium. Operating rates at major aluminium processing enterprises fell 3.6 points to 71.8% last week, driven by reduced activity ahead of Chinese New Year and tighter logistics. Rusal said 2020 aluminum production was 3.755 million tonnes, nearly unchanged from 2019, while sales fell 6%. The average sold aluminum price dropped 6% to $1,805/t. Aluminium and nickel also increased modestly alongside copper moves. | Fact.MR’s report highlights recovery in high-purity aluminium demand and continued growth supported by semiconductor and aerospace use cases, including AI/IoT-driven chip penetration and demand for aircraft alloys. With inventories not clearly addressed, the news could translate into softer aluminium demand expectations and downward price pressure over coming sessions. | Cameroon’s Alucam cut aluminium exports from about 62,000 tons (2018) to 52,000 (2019) and 49,000 (2020) amid financial problems after Rio Tinto’s divestment and a January 2018 energy disruption. Lower shipments and potential further power-payment issues could reduce supply, weighing on aluminium prices.

BMW signed a UAE deal to buy “green” solar-powered aluminium produced in Dubai, worth in the three-digit million euros, covering about half the aluminium needs of its Landshut foundry. The import surge suggests higher near-term demand and potential tightness, which is generally supportive for copper and aluminium prices. | China imported record volumes of refined copper and unwrought aluminium, driven by a “scrap gap” and a rapid manufacturing rebound after COVID-19 lockdowns. The jump in imports suggests stronger short-term feedstock and consumption demand, which is supportive for copper and aluminium price expectations in coming weeks.

London copper rose to an eight-year high on Wednesday, driven by tight supplies, more buyers than sellers and low inventories, plus a reported copper concentrate shortfall. Optimism over further U.S. coronavirus stimulus improved sentiment for base metals demand. The article also notes increases in aluminum and nickel prices. | SMM data shows China’s primary aluminium ingot social inventories across eight consumption areas rose by 61,000 mt from last Thursday to 775,000 mt as of Feb 10. Queensland’s Coordinator-General approved Alpha HPA’s $300 million high-purity alumina plant in Gladstone. The project will use solvent extraction technology and leverage the port for export. Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin reports an aluminium foundry wrap with US and Eastern Europe premiums moving higher. Copper prices eased on Friday, but the article highlights tight supply and low inventories supporting an exceptional monthly gain outlook. Shanghai copper fell from multi-year highs after China reported weaker-than-expected February factory growth, missing market expectations following earlier COVID-related disruptions. The April copper contract dropped 2.7%. Shanghai aluminium and nickel also declined around 2% as the disappointing data weighed on industrial metal demand expectations. | SMM reports alumina demand stayed soft after CNY, with alumina plants cutting offers as inventories rose. Hydro agreed to sell its Rolling division to KPS Capital Partners for €1.38bn, including seven plants and about 5,000 employees. Copper edged down in London on Tuesday as a firm dollar, supported by higher U.S. yields and faster recovery expectations, weighed on dollar-priced metals. LME three-month copper fell 0.7% to $8,935/t. Aluminium and nickel also declined, while copper inventories rose to high levels. | SMM reports that aluminium processors’ operating rates rose week-on-week, helped by backlog orders and production resumption. However, high aluminium prices limited downstream purchases for construction extrusions and reduced new orders (e.g., State Grid, wheel plants). The remainder of the text focuses mainly on Fastmarkets methodology/publication corrections rather than new supply-demand drivers, but the headline pricing indicates immediate bearish movement in aluminium and nickel. | SMM reports that most SHFE nonferrous metals fell on Tuesday. Copper slid 0.68% as a firmer US dollar weighed on prices. Aluminium dropped 2.13%. Nickel plunged 3.81% amid wider risk-off/commodity weakness. Fitch expected softer fiscal consolidation. Henan Shenhuo will buy 300,000 tonnes of alumina from Eurasian Resources Group this year, doubling its prior ERG intake. The kazakhstan-refined alumina will feed Shenhuo’s Xinjiang smelter, alongside domestic alumina capacity ramp-ups. More secure alumina supply supports aluminium production and sentiment. | ALRO’s Vimetco Extrusion plans to spend €10m to buy and install a new automated extrusion line for aluminium profiles. The company cites worldwide aluminum can shortages and retailer demand, noting that competition and major beverage firms control much of global aluminum supply. The partnership aims to resolve X2’s inventory constraints via additional aluminum can sourcing. | Metal Bulletin reports that for the first time since 2018 there is no aluminium queue at LME Istim warehouses in Port Klang. The update suggests improved availability versus prior congestion, which can ease near-term supply tightness expectations for aluminium prices. | Rio Tinto says it has made its first commercial sale of high-performance aluminium-scandium alloy to Amaero, delivered from its Canadian hydro-powered smelters and scandium oxide supply from a Quebec metallurgical complex. The company is also building a new Quebec plant to expand scandium oxide output using titanium-dioxide waste streams.

Rio Tinto said it has signed an agreement to deliver the first commercial batch of aluminium-scandium alloy for 3D printing to Amaero, using low-carbon aluminium from Canadian hydro-powered smelters and high-purity scandium oxide from a Quebec complex. The supply helps commercialize aerospace/defence high-temperature aluminium alloy demand. | Rio Tinto signed an agreement to supply Amaero with a first commercial batch of high-performance aluminium-scandium alloy for 3D printing. The billets will come from Rio’s hydro-powered Canadian smelters, while scandium oxide is sourced from its Quebec complex. The deal targets aerospace and defence high-temperature uses. | Copper rose 0.6% on the LME and 0.5% on the Shanghai market as U.S. lawmakers moved toward passing a $1.9 trillion stimulus bill. Separately, Chilean mine workers at Antofagasta’s Los Pelambres voted to reject a contract, raising strike supply risk. | Fastmarkets issued a pricing notice amending the publication timing/holiday schedule for CIF South Korea registered copper premium assessments starting May 18. It also corrected procedural errors affecting certain European low-carbon aluminium differential and Aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premium midpoint assessments. Changes relate to assessment publication, not underlying market supply/demand. | SMM says operating rates for aluminium wire and cable producers fell to 26.63% in February due to shorter production time, weak order intake, and downstream inability to absorb high raw material prices. Some companies face losses from pricing tied to average prior-month aluminium. March is expected to rebound. | Copper prices rose sharply on Thursday toward 10-year highs on supportive macro sentiment, including U.S. stimulus approval, benign U.S. inflation, and stronger-than-expected Chinese lending and lending-linked demand indicators. The article also mentions aluminium and nickel rising alongside copper, though some analysts warn China demand may be overstated.

Copper prices in London fell as demand concerns and higher supply pressured a recent rally to a near-10-year peak. The Yangshan premium declined, LME/SHFE inventories rose, and nearby supply tightness eased. China smelters increased cathode output. Aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | The live futures report notes an additional 320kt of aluminium delivered into LME, alongside price moves in tin and lead. The key market takeaway is that European aluminium premiums have surged to more than two-year highs, pointing to firmer physical demand/supply conditions for aluminium in the near term.

Copper prices rose to a near two-week high as China’s industrial output growth beat expectations and global supply concerns resurfaced. Trafigura flagged a copper supply deficit and a prolonged high-price cycle. Aluminium also advanced, but nickel fell during the same session, showing mixed cross-metal moves. | Live futures commentary says LME aluminium has risen to about $2,200/t, but “cancellations follow” a reported 600kt inflow. The combination of large warrant/inventory inflow followed by cancellations points to increased near-term supply availability, likely weighing on aluminium price expectations over coming days. | Copper rose on Monday on stronger-than-expected Chinese industrial growth, though trading volumes were thin and investors were cautious ahead of central bank meetings. The story also cites rising Chinese aluminum output and ongoing copper mine labor/supply disruptions in Chile and Peru, supporting bullish sentiment across copper and some spillover to aluminum and nickel. | Copper rose modestly on stronger-than-expected Chinese industrial growth, suggesting improved near-term demand, though trading volumes were thin. Aluminium jumped about 2% after Chinese city Baotou ordered industrial and power plant shutdowns to meet energy targets, tightening supply for energy-intensive production. | Copper rose modestly on stronger-than-expected Chinese industrial growth, indicating improving near-term demand expectations, though trading volumes were thin. Aluminum surged after Baotou ordered shutdowns/production curbs to meet energy targets, tightening power-intensive supply. The piece also notes broad base-metals gains, including nickel. | The live futures report says LME aluminium is edging close to its 2021 high on supply concerns. It also references zinc gains, but the tracked linkage here is aluminium. Overall, the stated market driver points to tightening supply conditions supporting higher aluminium prices near term.

Shanghai aluminium surged to a 9-1/2-year high as Baotou, a key Inner Mongolia producer, ordered power cuts and industrial shutdowns to cut carbon emissions. Analysts expect sizable output curbs, tightening supply. LME and ShFE aluminium contracts rose, with April aluminium up as much as 2.6% and LME three-month also gaining. | SMM says operating rates at secondary aluminium producers fell to 41.35% in February, down 20.8 points m/m, due to CNY shutdowns and maintenance. Aluminium prices rose after the holiday, discouraging downstream purchases and leaving scrap recyclers holding cargoes, tightening feedstock supply. Rates should rebound in March as scrap recycling and demand recover. | Shanghai aluminium jumped to a 9-1/2-year high after Inner Mongolia’s Baotou ordered power cuts and shutdowns to meet energy targets, potentially reducing aluminium output. The April SHFE contract rose up to 2.6%. LME cash and three-month contracts also edged higher despite mixed inventory signals. | Shanghai aluminium jumped to a multi-year high as Baotou in China ordered power cuts and shutdowns to meet early-quarter energy targets. CRU estimates the curtailments could cut annual output by about 100,000 tonnes. Prices rose sharply on the ShFE and modestly on the LME, reflecting tighter supply expectations. | Emirates Global Aluminium reported strong 2020 results despite Covid disruption, citing ramp-up of its alumina and bauxite assets and improved smelting margins. Europe is expected to spearhead the market, with food packaging a major end use. It’s a demand outlook without explicit short-term supply constraints or price signals. | A Russian press release says BK-ALPROF LLC expanded rapidly since 2003 and now produces about 20,000 tons of aluminum profiles annually, using its own foundry and extrusion lines. It claims reliable ingot supply (including from RUSAL) and lists downstream uses across construction and manufacturing. | Nornickel said water inflow forced partial suspension at the Oktyabrsky and Taimyrsky mines, downgrading 2021 output: nickel -35,000 tonnes, copper -65,000 tonnes, and PGM -710,000 ounces, with restart unlikely for 3-4 months. Markets initially lifted nickel, but nickel gains faded quickly. | Copper fell 1.8% on profit taking and a firmer dollar ahead of the Fed, with investors cautious on inflation. Aluminum weakened after China ordered power cuts and output curbs in an Inner Mongolia hub. Nickel also declined around 1%, reflecting broader downside pressure in industrial metals. | Copper prices fell sharply on the LME as investors took profits ahead of the Fed meeting amid inflation worries and higher U.S. yields. The piece also flags rising aluminum supply risks from China power cuts and output curbs, while nickel edged lower, pointing to softer near-term sentiment. | CAMALCO, a subsidiary of Canyon Resources, plans to meet local Cameroonian partners to develop its Minim-Martap, Makan, and Ngaoundal bauxite deposits in Cameroon. Aluminum hit a 9-1/2-year high on supply concerns from China power cuts. Nickel also dipped, indicating broad downside pressure across industrial metals. | BK-ALPROF LLC says it has become Russia’s fastest-growing aluminum producer, expanding extrusion and downstream processing (casting ingots, anodizing, and profile sales) and reporting about 20,000 tons per year of aluminum profiles capacity. The update is mainly copper-sentiment positive but not a near-term physical supply change.

Rio Tinto is cutting 41 jobs at its Weipa bauxite operations as part of cost reductions. Management links the restructure to “falling aluminium prices” and lower alumina prices amid competition. The job cuts are said not to affect the Amrun expansion, which is still under construction. | Morgan Stanley says aluminium supply remains tight, supported by restocking and constrained LME scrap/tonnage despite higher production and falling alumina; shortages and elevated freight keep spot near $2150/t. It also notes the squeeze may ease in Q2. For nickel, Credit Suisse says Tsingshan’s planned ramp could create a surplus (even if constraints exist), pressuring prices later. | Copper rose on Thursday after the Fed projected rapid U.S. growth while keeping stimulus, a dovish tone that weakened the dollar and made dollar-priced metals cheaper for other-currency holders. Nickel also increased, while aluminium was slightly lower. LME copper inventories hit a local high, but prices still firmed. | Mitsubishi agreed with Glencore to acquire a 30% interest in Australia’s Aurukun bauxite project. As bauxite is aluminium feedstock, the deal is broadly supportive for future aluminium supply expectations. | SMM data on March 18 showed Chinese 6063 aluminium billet stocks across Foshan, Wuxi, Huzhou, Changzhou and Nanchang fell 3,900 mt to 232,900 mt versus the previous week. Declines in Changzhou and Huzhou outweighed increases in Wuxi, indicating tighter local billet supply. | Rockridge Resources began a funded diamond drilling program at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan, targeting VMS-style copper (plus associated silver, zinc and cobalt) along newly defined conductors and at depth. The release is exploration-focused with no production volumes or timing impacts for near-term copper supply. | China’s aluminium imports in January-February surged 150.7% year on year to 455,128 tonnes, following an arbitrage window that reopened as overseas metal became cheaper. Customs data also shows record 2020 imports. Bauxite imports fell slightly. The increased inflow could add near-term supply pressure on aluminium prices. | Rockridge Resources started a fully funded diamond drilling program (1,600 m in 6–8 holes) at its Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan. The company targets newly defined conductors and deeper extensions under the existing deposit. Management links the timing to a strengthening copper market, but no immediate production change is stated. | BK-ALPROF LLC in Russia is described as rapidly expanding aluminum production, including a casting foundry and extrusion capability, with stated capacity of about 20,000 tons of aluminum profiles per year. EPA released revisions to the U.S. Lead and Copper Rule, tightening service-line inventories, sampling (including fifth-liter draws), public notification, corrosion control, and accelerated replacement triggers for exceedances. Utilities must build and update inventories and begin replacement planning, increasing compliance and potential pipe-material demand. | A Victoria energy-retailers deal backed by more than $150m in federal and state support will keep the Portland Aluminium smelter operating until at least 2026. The funding enables a new electricity supply arrangement, crucial because the plant uses about 10% of Victoria’s power. Output remains ~20% of Australia’s aluminium production. | Rusal has suspended investments in a troubled aluminum plant near Ashland, Kentucky, having already spent at least $65 million and waiting for U.S. partners to raise needed funds. The project has faced fundraising issues and prior sanctions controversy, and Kentucky funding is contingent on the mill materializing. | Alcoa announced new five-year power supply agreements for its Portland Aluminium smelter in Victoria and government underwriting via Australia’s RERT scheme, with matching state funding in principle. The measures improve electricity-price certainty and grid reliability for smelter operations, supporting primary aluminium output stability. | Alcoa said it has signed five-year electricity supply agreements for its Portland Aluminium smelter in Victoria, starting Aug. 1, to replace expiring power arrangements. The federal government will underwrite participation in a grid reliability scheme, with Victoria matching support. Terms are confidential but improve power-cost certainty. | Australia’s Prime Minister announces a deal to extend Alcoa’s Portland aluminium smelter life by five years, backed by discounted power supply to 2026 and about $80m each from federal and Victorian governments. The smelter is framed as a grid “battery,” implying steadier aluminium output and supportive sentiment for aluminium markets. | Camalco presented business opportunities for Cameroonian firms tied to the Minim-Martap bauxite project in Cameroon, citing large, high-alumina bauxite reserves and planned substantial investment in transport and port logistics. Because bauxite is the key feedstock for aluminium, the project may support expectations for future supply. | SMM reports broadly lower SHFE nonferrous prices: copper fell 1.64% and aluminium 1.55%, while nickel slipped 0.12%. The move is attributed to risk-off sentiment as US-China talks start badly and global stock markets decline. Additional context for aluminium includes regional supply constraints and shifting demand.

Fastmarkets’/live futures update notes LME zinc up 1.3% but highlights that aluminium prices are still rising, with cancellations continuing. It also reiterates that aluminium supply has remained technically intact despite curtailed output in parts of the Gulf and severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, supporting tightness. | Santee Cooper approved a new multi-year electricity contract for Century Aluminum’s Mount Holly plant, using an experimental rate tied to excess power capacity and enabling the plant to continue operations. The deal is expected to avoid shutdown risk and supports potential expansion, helping stabilize near-term aluminium supply expectations. | An AP report says Matalco will invest $53 million to convert a vacant Kentucky facility into an aluminum ingot plant using recycled aluminum. The project, planned for Simpson County, is expected to start operating by 2022 and produce up to 270 million pounds annually, creating 60 jobs.

Shanghai aluminium fell to a one-month low as traders feared Chinese authorities may release ingots into the market, adding to supply concerns. Reuters notes rising LME and ShFE aluminium inventories and a sharp jump in China’s imports. Primary margins and robust production further suggest ample near-term supply, pressuring prices downward. | SMM reports slightly higher operating rates at multiple aluminium processing segments as rigid demand and order backlogs keep producers running despite high, volatile aluminium prices. Some demand is constrained by customers’ delayed acceptance of prices and weaker overseas orders, but overall operations remain supported, especially for plate/strip and wire/cable. | SMM reports SHFE 2105 aluminium rose about 1% last week, while LME aluminium gained about 2%. Sentiment was supported early by Inner Mongolia smelter output-cut news and slowing domestic inventory growth, alongside seasonal demand recovery. Macro risks (lower oil, higher yields) may limit upside; price is expected to stay firm this week. | China is considering selling roughly 500,000 metric tons of aluminum from state reserves to cool prices and support emissions/energy objectives. Aluminum futures in Shanghai fell to a daily limit on the speculation. The proposed stock release would likely add near-term supply and pressure aluminum prices. | SMM says SHFE nonferrous metals closed mixed: copper +0.33% and nickel +2.12% rose, while aluminium fell 2.57% to a one-month low before partial recovery. It cites US/EU sanctions and China countermeasures boosting risk-off sentiment, alongside a US green-infrastructure plan and trade/market developments. | A shipment of “materially deficient” Chinese aluminium was found by Australian shipbuilder Austal, forcing remediation of Cape Class patrol boats and delaying launch dates by four to 16 weeks. The supplier removed the mill and lab from approved lists and is replacing out-of-spec material, adding maintenance costs. | Aluminium futures tumbled about 2% as markets reacted to rumors that China’s stockpiles would be cut. The article frames the move as a near-term sentiment shock affecting aluminium supply expectations, leading to immediate downside pressure in aluminium benchmarks and related futures. | Mitsubishi will acquire a 30% interest in Glencore’s Aurukun bauxite project in Queensland, adding bauxite exposure to its existing aluminium smelting and trading operations. The mine is undeveloped and currently under feasibility assessment, so the agreement may support longer-term supply prospects for aluminium rather than immediate output changes.

Copper prices fell as renewed European coronavirus lockdown fears boosted demand for the U.S. dollar and weighed on global growth sentiment. Three-month LME copper and Shanghai May copper dropped. Nickel also eased on the day, while aluminum premiums rose, indicating regional tightness despite broader macro headwinds. | SMM cites China Customs data showing aluminium bars and extrusions exports in early 2021, with aluminium extrusion exports rising 35.9% year-on-year to 153,200 mt in January–February. February exports fell versus January. Destinations are scattered, with Southeast Asia and Guangdong as leading contributors. | Damm and Ball Corporation announced that Damm’s beer will use only ASI-certified aluminium beverage cans from March 2021. The ASI certification covers responsible sourcing and stewardship across the aluminium lifecycle, including can production and recycling. The news is about sustainability standards rather than a measurable near-term supply or demand shock. | SMM reports weak demand and poor sales at secondary aluminium producers have pressured aluminium scrap prices. Tight supply from import restrictions keeps scrap tense supply limited, but secondary aluminium (ADC12) lacks momentum, with orders only slightly improving. Prices are expected to decline this week, with downstream losses expanding if aluminium rises. | North China alumina trading was thin and prices stayed largely stable around 2,340–2,350 yuan/mt. SMM data show China’s primary aluminium ingot inventories fell during the week, dropping 7,000 mt from last Thursday to about 1.24 million mt as of March 25. The decrease was mainly driven by lower stocks in Wuxi, Nanhai, and Hangzhou, including SHFE-warranted supplies. | SMM data show China’s aluminium billet stocks across key consuming regions fell 34,700 mt WoW to 198,200 mt as of Mar 25, driven by outbound activity, price volatility, and tepid but concentrated downstream restocking. With prices stabilized, inventories are expected to stay broadly stable next week. | Century Aluminum says it has finalized a new three-year power contract with Santee Cooper, rescinding a WARN notice tied to potential job losses if the Mt. Holly aluminium smelter could not restart. The contract takes effect April 1, supports rebuilding cells and restarting the potline, and runs through Dec. 2023.

London copper rose 1.1% after positive U.S. labor data and faster COVID-19 vaccination rollout boosted optimism for global recovery. However, both copper contracts remain set for weekly losses amid Europe’s COVID-19 wave, rising inventories, and a stronger dollar. LME aluminium and ShFE nickel also edged higher. | London copper rose 1.1% as equities climbed on optimism for global economic recovery, supported by positive U.S. labour data and faster-than-expected COVID-19 vaccination progress. The note cited metals tracking stock market gains. LME aluminium and ShFE nickel also advanced, though copper inventories were rising. | SMM reports SHFE nonferrous metals mostly rose on Friday. Copper closed flat, but aluminium gained 1.52% and nickel rose 1.93%. The commentary cites supportive US policy signals alongside infrastructure/innovation rhetoric, consistent with improved sentiment for industrial metal demand in the near term. | The article says EU plans to toughen waste packaging rules could push aluminum can recycling toward 100% by 2030. It notes significant ongoing “metal loss” from not collected cans in Europe and the US, implying a scrap supply gap. Recycling is also framed as crucial for decarbonizing aluminum production.

SMM data show China’s primary aluminium ingot inventories fell over the weekend. Social stocks across eight consumption areas declined 9,000 mt from last Thursday to 1.23 million mt as of March 29. Drops in Wuxi and Nanhai drove most of the decrease, while Hangzhou rose slightly. | London copper fell 0.7% to around $8,900/ton, driven by higher LME exchange inventories and signals of weakening demand from China, the top consumer. The report also cites LME aluminum down about 1%. Nickel direction is mixed, with ShFE nickel up and ShFE tin higher. | BTU Metals announced expanded land and ongoing IP geophysical surveying near its TNT copper-silver-gold discovery in Ontario’s Red Lake area. The release mainly affects copper exploration sentiment, but it does not provide resource or supply changes, limiting near-term price impact.

The EU has imposed anti-dumping duties of 21.2% to 31.2% on Chinese aluminium extrusions after finding they were sold at unfairly low prices. Duties are lower than provisional levels. The action targets products used in transport, construction and electronics and should reduce low-priced imports. | The update focuses on litigation and renewed legislative interest around Section 232 tariffs. It notes reinstatement of aluminum import duties from the UAE shortly after removal and highlights that the Commerce Secretary called the duties “effective.” With the U.S. defending duties in multiple court and WTO cases, near-term removal appears unlikely.

A container ship grounding has blocked two-way traffic through the Suez Canal, disrupting shipping between Europe and Asia. SMM links the disruption to delayed China aluminium semis exports (plate, strip, foil, extrusion, wire/cable), with detouring via the Cape expected to add about one month and affect April shipments if prolonged. | LME copper is poised for its first monthly fall in a year, down around 0.5% on the day and nearly 3.8% month-to-date, pressured by a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed COVID-19 risks in Europe. The piece also cites copper concentrate force majeure and notes nickel weakness and flat aluminium. | SMM reports muted domestic alumina trading with mostly steady prices, but limited upside due to a market shifting toward surplus. China’s March copper imports rose 25% year on year to 552,317 tonnes and first-quarter imports hit 1.44 million tonnes, the highest since at least 2008. Shanghai aluminium rose, with the May contract near a 10-year high, as traders feared China could curb aluminium output in Xinjiang to meet pollution-control environmental targets. The article reports broker optimism for copper and aluminium, citing Goldman Sachs upgrades and forecasts for higher 2022 prices and long-term deficits on renewables-driven demand. China’s aluminium extrusion exports in March fell, with EU shipments down sharply. Vietnam’s Ministry of Industry and Trade has decided to continue anti-dumping duties on selected Chinese non-alloy aluminium products (bars, rods, extruded and surface-treated/unprocessed) with duty rates from 4.39% to 35.58%. Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin’s live futures report highlights market pricing and logistics. It notes LME copper cash reaching $10,000/t and records 64kt aluminium delivery in Port Klang. Copper prices extended their rally above $10,000 per tonne on the LME, nearing the 2011 all-time high. Macquarie expects nickel to stay in deficit into the June quarter after a March production shortfall; disruptions reduced briquette output and port stocks fell, supporting nickel tightness. While demand supports lower inventory days, higher supply outlook is likely pressure on aluminium. | Copper prices hit record highs on the LME and Shanghai after hopes for stronger economic and green-energy demand coincided with tighter concentrate supply. Chinese smelters agreed to cut concentrate purchases, reinforcing scarcity sentiment; aluminium also rose. | Copper surged to record highs on the LME and Shanghai as investors chase momentum and some physical buyers capitulate after waiting for lower prices. Support came from tight copper concentrate supply and reports that Chinese smelters agreed to cut 2021 concentrate purchases. Aluminium also hit fresh peaks; nickel was slightly down. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals broadly rose, with SHFE copper jumping 4.81% and aluminium up 3.68% as the USD index weakened. It notes high copper prices cooled consumption and some smelters halted production for maintenance, while nickel was nearly unchanged. | The headline reports a 199-day queue for aluminium at Istim warehouses in Port Klang, suggesting significant delays in metal availability and constrained warehouse throughput. Such physical-market tightness can support higher aluminium premiums and near-term prices as buyers face longer delivery/handling times. | Copper prices extended their record run, rising to a fresh LME high on optimism for environmental-driven demand and potential tightening in Chinese concentrate flows, alongside expectations of supportive central-bank policy. The piece also links the broader commodities boom to inflation concerns and notes aluminium slipped after earlier gains. | Copper rose to a new record $4.90/lb, with gains attributed to a rebound in Chinese demand, supply-side jitters (Chile producer tax risk and stricter Chinese emissions targets for smelters), and accommodative monetary policy. The broader industrial-commodity rally also lifted aluminium alongside copper. | Sky News reports the Australian federal government is considering repurposing an alumina refinery for fuel storage. Shanghai copper prices slipped 1.5% after a rally to a record high, as tepid demand from China’s industrial buyers weighed. The Yangshan copper premium fell to the lowest since February 2016, indicating subdued appetite for imported copper. Nickel and aluminium also fell amid cautious sentiment. | London LME copper rose about 0.9% as traders bet on stronger demand from renewable energy and EV sectors alongside gradual economic recovery and stimulus. The piece also notes rising copper related supply tightness and speculative positioning. Aluminium also increased, while nickel fell on Shanghai data. | Max Resource reports expanded assay results widening the CONEJO copper-silver zone at its CESAR North project in Colombia, with multiple high-grade copper and silver intercepts and the zone remaining open in all directions. India’s Aluminium Association urged the government to implement at least a 5% RoDTEP remission rate, saying delays and ambiguity in notified sector-wise rates are harming aluminium exporters. Copper increased 1.62% and nickel 1.42%, while aluminium added 0.25%. Citing an OECD report, they argue China’s subsidized financing expanded aluminium capacity and depressed global prices, harming unsubsidized competitors. Copper fell 1.8% as a firmer dollar and weaker China TSF/loan growth pointed to slowing demand. Traders also worried Chinese authorities may cap commodity prices. Silver Elephant plans to spin out its nickel (Minago) and vanadium projects, returning to a pure silver vehicle focused on its Pulacayo silver asset. It notes nickel prices are near multi-year highs and details EIA renewal and prior investment, but the article does not imply near-term nickel supply changes. | China Hongqiao plans to shift additional aluminium smelting capacity from Shandong to hydropower-reliant Yunnan, adding a further 1 million tonnes to the Wenshan/Honghe projects. SMM reports the SHFE most-traded aluminium contract fell 2.7% last week, while LME aluminium dropped 3.8%. Despite weaker liquidity sentiment from higher US PPI and the Fed’s policy stance, domestic fundamentals remain positive and the peak season continues. SHFE copper slipped 0.07%, while nickel rose 1.98% and aluminium gained 1.39%. Century Aluminum announced CEO succession: Mike Bless will retire July 1, 2021, and Jesse Gary will become President and CEO. The company cites continuity and industry knowledge, referencing prior operational expansions and pandemic leadership, but provides no new production, capacity, or cost guidance that would immediately affect aluminium market pricing. | Security officers were killed while protecting substations from armed copper cable thieves in South Africa, amid repeated thefts causing outages. The municipality reports millions in losses and notes City Power is replacing copper cables with aluminium, reflecting demand substitution pressure alongside disrupted copper cable networks. | Copper prices rose on a weaker U.S. dollar. The report also mentions aluminium smelters in Yunnan being asked to test power-consumption staggering ahead of celebrations, supporting tight supply expectations, while LME and SHFE nickel both climbed. Overall, the news signals broad bullish pressure for copper, aluminium, and nickel. | The EU and US began talks to resolve their steel and aluminium trade dispute, with the EU temporarily suspending an increase in tariff rebalancing measures on US 232 steel and aluminium. The move aims to address market distortions and global excess capacity. It should support near-term aluminium pricing sentiment. | Missouri manufacturers and other U.S. metal users urge President Biden to lift Trump-era Section 232 tariffs on foreign aluminum, saying tariffs raise costs and extend delivery lead times amid strong demand for metals tied to autos and appliances. They warn tariffs could worsen shortages and delay infrastructure spending; Raimondo says tariffs remain. | Industrial metals rose as investors shifted toward riskier assets on bets for an extended period of low interest rates, lifting global equities. The US dollar fell to its weakest since February, making dollar-priced metals cheaper for non-US buyers. Aluminium was among the gainers reported on Tuesday.

The article reports broad commodity rallies in 2021, linking gains to global reopening, stimulus-driven demand and inflation concerns. It states copper has surged through $10,000/ton on tight supply expectations. Brent crude also hits $70, reinforcing a “reflation trade” that typically supports industrial metals sentiment. | SMM reports that low rainfall in China’s Yunnan province is pressuring hydropower supply, leading the government to reduce electricity load for aluminium smelters during the dry season. Curtailment is expected to cut primary aluminium production capacity by about 76,000–380,000 mt for ~one month, potentially longer. | Copper fell 2.6% on the LME as inflation fears drove risk-off flows, eclipsing concerns about potential South American supply disruptions. It also notes broad downside moves across base metals, with nickel down 1.4% and aluminium down 1.2%, alongside a firmer dollar and contango implying ample supply. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals fell broadly on May 19. SHFE copper dropped 2.01% and aluminium plunged 2.28%, while nickel edged down about 0.05%. The NDRC noted commodity prices should return to supply-demand fundamentals and is investigating market conditions, contributing to a pessimistic market mood and long liquidation.

SMM reports Chinese aluminium billet stocks fell 16,600 mt (down 15.8%) to 88,600 mt as of May 20, while outbound shipments rose sharply. With aluminium prices easing, downstream buying increased on fears of a rebound. SMM expects inventories to decline slightly next week, keeping prices tangled. | SMM data show China’s primary aluminium ingot social inventories fell 41,000 mt from last Thursday to 1.02 million mt as of May 20. Stocks declined across seven of eight consumption areas, with the exception of Chongqing. The drawdown signals tighter near-term availability for the market. | SMM says operating rates at China’s secondary aluminium producers fell to 41.35% in April and are expected to decline further in May. China’s commodity-price management and other factors capped gains, limiting the rally’s strength. | Trigon Metals announced assay results from drilling at its Silver Hill copper project in Morocco, including a highlight of 31 metres at 0.74% copper. ARENA has allocated $11.3m to Alcoa for a low-emissions alumina investigation at the Wagerup refinery. The project will test renewable-powered MVR to supply process heat and, if feasible, deliver a 3 MW MVR module. It aims to decarbonise energy-intensive alumina refining and support competitiveness. | London copper is set for its biggest weekly drop since Sept 2020 as fears grow that China will curb a commodity price rally. Holly, South Carolina primary aluminum smelter alongside Governor Henry McMaster. The company cites significant capital investment to increase production capacity and create new jobs. The piece reviews bullish expert views on nickel and aluminium. For aluminium, Citi expects structurally higher prices as China adds less capacity and demand eventually catches up. | The piece highlights Indonesia’s expansion plans for nickel/cobalt and warns that rising Indonesian supply could weigh on nickel fundamentals in the second half. Separately, Citi is bullish on aluminium, arguing China’s slower supply additions may eventually tighten the market, pushing prices higher in 2022. | Copper prices eased as signs of weakening demand in China emerged, while a firmer U.S. dollar increased the cost of dollar-priced metals for other currency holders. The article also notes aluminium in Shanghai declined, pointing to overall softness in industrial metals. | Peru’s energy and mines ministry says 10.7% of global copper output in 2020 came from Peru (2.15 million FMT) and highlights strong post-COVID recovery, plus reserve shares. Century Aluminum will hold a ceremonial restart and jobs announcement at its Mt. Holly, South Carolina primary aluminum smelter. Century Aluminum announced a $60+ million expansion of its Berkeley County smelter operations, aimed at creating 100 new jobs. The project increases primary aluminum production capacity, which can pressure market expectations for aluminum supply and weigh on prices over the next several days to weeks. | Canada announced a $40,000 investment (with Alcoa and Énergir bringing total funding to $350,000) to add an energy information system for Alcoa Canada’s Deschambault aluminum smelter. Holly, South Carolina operations with a $60+ million investment, adding production capacity to cast molten aluminum into ingot and other primary products. The article says the copper market is near peak supply but unprepared for demand tied to renewables, with Goldman predicting deficits over 2021-2022 and inventory depletion if prices stay low. Analysts expect SHFE aluminium to fall slightly amid limited immediate SRB release risk. | SMM reported SHFE nonferrous metals finished mixed after investors reacted to China trade data and US macro signals. London copper rose slightly as a weaker dollar provided support, but it failed to break $10,000/t amid tepid demand from China. China copper imports fell 8% in May on high prices. The piece also notes LME nickel down and ShFE aluminum lower, reflecting broader market softness.

Copper prices fell as Chinese factory gate input costs rose at the fastest pace in 12 years and a Chinese agency said it would increase monitoring of commodity markets. Arizona temporarily halted production of license plates because of a worldwide aluminum shortage after a missed delivery of aluminum to the state’s plate maker. ADOT also faces impacts for highway signs.  | Copper fell as markets worried China will curb commodity prices after renewed pledges to monitor spot and futures, following surging producer inflation. LME copper dropped 0.6% and ShFE copper 0.5%. The article also notes LME aluminum and nickel weakening, with attention on upcoming U.S. inflation data. | Copper prices fell as China pledged to cool base-metal prices and as low Chinese copper premiums and cash-to-3-month discounts pointed to soft demand. China’s State Reserve Bureau is reported to be selling “chu” (reserve metal) directly to downstream firms, targeting copper and aluminium (and zinc). Arizona’s transportation department said a worldwide aluminum shortage temporarily halted license plate production, creating a backlog of about 68,000 plates. Supply fears eased after workers at BHP’s Spence mine in Chile agreed a contract deal, avoiding a strike. Aluminium and nickel were slightly higher, indicating limited net near-term price pressure. | Copper fell on the LME as markets fretted about China taking steps to curb further industrial metals price gains and as demand fundamentals looked less supportive. The piece cites potential Chinese reserve releases for non-ferrous metals and upcoming industrial production data, reinforcing near-term downside pressure. Aluminium and nickel were also mentioned with gains. | The PRNewswire item promotes a ResearchAndMarkets report on the global aluminum extrusion market. It cites projected demand growth from construction, automotive, and electrical end-uses, plus a COVID-19 temporary downturn tied to weaker automotive and construction demand and supply-chain delays. No immediate pricing or policy catalyst is provided.

Copper prices fell to a 7-week low as investors feared Chinese authorities would curb the recent commodity rally via increased monitoring and a crackdown on speculative positions. The article expects China may release copper and aluminium state reserves. Guangdong power curbs partially eased. | Copper prices slid to seven-week lows as funds and traders cut long positions, driven by concern that China may curb further price rises. Copper is down sharply versus its recent record. The article also cites aluminium easing and a related June delivery dynamic, while nickel falls alongside broader nonferrous weakness. | Mytilineos’ Aluminium of Greece agreed a new electricity supply contract with PPC until 2023, aiming for cheaper, greener power as the plant shifts to a nearby 826 MW gas unit and bilateral renewables. The firm also secured a 10-year Glencore deal for alumina/aluminium with guaranteed prices, plus major investments. | Rio Tinto and ARENA will fund a $1.2 million feasibility study to assess whether clean hydrogen can replace natural gas in the calcination step of alumina refining at the Yarwun plant. Citi says copper destocking tied to strong end-demand is unsustainable and expects a refined drawdown and higher prices over the next 3–4 months, with a possible upside from proposed Chilean taxes. The article also notes reported Chinese state reserve selling of aluminium, cautioning about speculation. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium rebounded sharply and expects further gains this week. Support comes from a weekly drop in aluminium ingot social inventory, peak-season consumption, and slower restart of closed capacity in Inner Mongolia and Yunnan. The move targets copper and aluminium, aiming to cool a sector where prices have surged amid recovery, liquidity and speculative buying, likely adding immediate supply pressure. | China will release national reserves of base metals over the near term to help stabilise critical commodity prices. The move targets copper and aluminium amid strong price gains this year driven by recovery, liquidity, and speculative buying. Additional supply from reserve releases is likely to weigh on prices in coming days. | China’s National Food and Strategic Reserves Administration will release copper and aluminium (and zinc) stocks under State Council guidance. The release targets non-ferrous processing and manufacturing firms through public bidding, with the National Reserves Adjustment Centre handling implementation. By limiting outward shipments, the policy change could tighten near-term supply for global buyers, supporting expectations for higher aluminium prices over the next days to weeks. | China’s National Food and Strategic Reserves Administration will release a second batch of national reserves: 30,000 mt of copper and 90,000 mt of aluminium (plus zinc). Companies can bid July 21–26, with open bidding on July 29. The additional supply may pressure near-term prices. | China National Food and Strategic Reserves Administration will release a second batch of national reserves. The plan includes 30,000 mt of copper and 90,000 mt of aluminium (plus zinc). Bidding applications run July 21-26, with open bidding July 29, signaling increased near-term supply that may weigh on prices. | Rusal said it expects EU support for low-carbon aluminum as the European Commission prepares climate-related customs duties on aluminum imports. Rusal argues its “green” hydraulic-power production has lower carbon intensity. It notes import duties could raise average aluminum import costs and affect competitiveness of EU buyers.

Human Rights Watch and Inclusive Development International urge carmakers to address human-rights abuses in aluminum supply chains, citing bauxite mining land destruction, red-mud pollution, and coal-based smelting emissions. The report highlights Guinea and Brazil cases and calls for stricter audits and supplier consequences, with potential long-run cost/risk effects. | Copper rose on Thursday as a weaker dollar and improved risk appetite supported buying. China’s July 29 state-reserve sales were smaller than expected, including 30,000 tonnes of copper, helping sentiment. The piece also notes China will sell 90,000 tonnes of aluminium, but the price surge focus remains copper. | SMM data show China’s aluminium billet stocks fell 1,200 mt to 119,600 mt, with declines in Foshan, Changzhou and Nanchang. Henan rainstorms caused some aluminium plants to stop production and blocked the Longhai line, reducing arrivals to Wuxi warehouses. Inventory build-up next week is unlikely. | Rights groups (HRW and IDI) released a report urging carmakers to address abuses in aluminium supply chains tied to bauxite mining, refining “red mud” pollution, and coal-powered smelting. It cites potential farmland and water damage in key countries and highlights scrutiny of carmakers’ sourcing and certification efforts. | Human Rights Watch and IDI urge carmakers to address human-rights impacts in aluminium supply chains, highlighting Guinea’s major bauxite exports to China. The report cites alleged land and water damage from bauxite mining and calls for tighter procurement and corrective-action requirements, but no immediate supply cutoff is announced. | Eagle Plains Resources and Canter Capital executed an option to earn a 60% interest in the Schott’s Lake copper(-zinc) project in Saskatchewan via $5M exploration spend, cash payments, and share issuance. The company also completed electromagnetic surveying and plans further modelling, targeting extensions. No production or major market impact is stated.

Global aluminium production growth slowed in Q2 as prices hit multi-year highs. Output expansion is restrained by power-related smelter constraints in China, drought curtailments in Yunnan, and tighter energy rules. China continues importing primary/aluminium and plans additional state reserve sales, while restarts elsewhere remain limited.

Copper prices rose to near six-week highs as investors shifted from weaker Chinese equities into commodities and floods in central China sparked supply concerns. Flood-driven infrastructure rebuilding is expected to consume industrial metals. The piece also notes nickel and aluminium reaching multi-month highs, supporting broad bullish sentiment. | Copper prices rose to near a six-week peak after China floods in central industrial areas triggered supply concerns and boosted expectations for metals demand from infrastructure rebuilding. Equities sold off as investors rotated to commodities. Nickel also hit recent highs and aluminium reached its highest since May. | Operating rates for major aluminium processors fell 4.3 percentage points to 66.5% last week, with Henan down 24.6 points to 41% due to severe floods. Plate/sheet, strip and wire/cable output were hit, transportation disrupted, and recovery timing uncertain, pressuring near-term aluminium sentiment. | SMM reports broad nonferrous gains, with SHFE copper +1.71%, aluminium +1.14%, and nickel +2.98%. Copper rises on improving macro recovery signals easing funding fears. Aluminium lifts as de-stocking shrinks inventories and government reserve releases disappoint. Nickel strengthens on Indonesia pandemic-linked ferronickel supply worries. | Copper surged to a 6-week high as China floods in Zhengzhou and central provinces raised disruption and rebuilding demand hopes, while LME inventories were falling. A softer US dollar further supported prices. The piece also reports aluminium and nickel rising modestly alongside copper.

Flooding in China’s Henan paralyzed aluminium production and logistics, including an explosion at an aluminium plant and rain-hit output at other producers. Bauxite mining and alumina roasting capacity were reduced, and rail/vehicle transport was suspended, delaying deliveries. With fewer arrivals, social inventory is expected to stay low and the market remains firm. | Copper prices fell 0.8% after a five-session run as a rebounding U.S. dollar and caution ahead of the Fed meeting pressured sentiment. Separate factors cited include Peru’s blockade disrupting Las Bambas mine operations, while heavy rains in China and aluminium production halts from floods offered some support. | Nickel reversed earlier gains and most LME-listed metals were down at least 1% in the live futures update. The excerpt also includes Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin procedural and methodology correction notes for certain aluminium and steel assessments, but the market update points to near-term bearish price pressure. | U.S. buyers of physical aluminium ingot are paying a record premium of about 30 cents per lb ($670/tonne) over the LME cash price to source metal for processing plants. The article links this premium to aluminium prices near recent highs, suggesting tight near-term supply.

Copper prices hovered near multi-week highs as investors priced in a strong second-half outlook, supported by falling ShFE inventories and rising physical premiums pointing to solid demand. The article also flags expectations of a looser monetary policy and shows LME nickel and aluminium moving modestly, reinforcing bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | A Reuters poll expects copper to keep falling from record highs as abundant mine supply ramps up and China seeks to cap commodity prices. Aluminium Q4 forecasts also point lower prices amid potential demand moderation. Nickel faces downside risks from higher Indonesia supply and an increased 2021 surplus.

Showa Denko said it is installing additional production lines at its Oyama plant to double capacity for aluminum heat radiators used in power modules for electrified vehicles. The extra line is slated to start in early 2022, aiming to meet rising demand for these aluminum components tied to xEV growth. | London copper rose 0.6% as a weaker dollar improved non-USD buying appetite and Fed remarks reduced rate-hike expectations. LME nickel gained 0.8% while LME aluminium slipped 0.4%. ShFE copper and some other contracts fell, indicating mixed regional demand/FX-driven effects across metals. | Showa Denko will double capacity at its Oyama plant to produce aluminium heat radiators for power modules used in battery and hybrid electric vehicles. The firm is installing additional lines with startup planned for early 2022, citing rising demand. The radiators use aluminium fins and vacuum brazing. | Copper rose after the Fed signaled it was “some ways away” from tapering and as China tried to calm regulatory fears, boosting risk sentiment and weakening the dollar. Additional support came from a potential strike at BHP’s Escondida mine and progress on a U.S. infrastructure bill. | SMM data showed China’s social aluminium inventories fell 51,000 mt week-on-week to 758,000 mt as of July 29 across eight consumption areas. Limited shipment efficiency and constrained south-China arrivals reduced inflows, while outbound volumes stayed relatively high. The article flags logistics as key to near-term inventory dynamics. | SMM data show aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas fell 20,600 mt to 99,000 mt week-on-week, a 17.2% drop, with declines in all regions due to Henan flood-related rail blockages. Outbound shipments rose on improved downstream buying, and arrivals should normalize next week. | Grizzly Discoveries reports completion of Phase 1 sampling at its Robocop copper-cobalt project in southeast British Columbia, following VTEM/magnetic survey anomalies. Results are pending for multielement assays, with drill testing planned for fall 2021. The release cites historical high-grade Cu/Co/Ag intersections and new copper showings in float.

SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium rising sharply above 20,000 yuan/mt amid supply disruptions. Truck transport in Henan remains impaired after flooding, while power curtailment in south-west China tightens local aluminium production. A weaker US dollar and rising SHFE lift LME. Inventory decline, COVID impacts, and possible reserve releases are key. | Vedanta Aluminium says it became India’s largest green power purchaser on IEX’s Green Term-Ahead Market, sourcing 354 million units of solar and non-solar renewables for its Jharsuguda integrated aluminium facility in Q1 FY22. The company links this to lower emissions and plans a shift toward low-carbon energy. | Copper rebounded as the dollar weakened and traders increased hopes for China stimulus after soft factory data. Supply tightness was also cited following recent floods in China, alongside potential labor disruption in Chile. The report notes gains in LME copper, aluminium, and nickel alongside expectations of bullish base-metal momentum in August.

Taber Extrusions announced it added a new Haas VF-12 CNC machining center to support its larger aluminum extrusion capabilities. The company says the added in-house machining capacity reduces processing time and costs for long aluminum profiles and can work across various aluminum alloys for sectors like automotive, defense, transportation, and marine. | Grid Metals reports completion of an eight-hole drill program at its Bannockburn nickel project with seven remaining holes awaiting assays, while East Bull Lake sampling and metallurgical tests are progressing for PGM-focused targets that may include copper/nickel recovery. No new price-relevant assay or production outcomes are provided.

London copper rose 0.5% on a weak dollar, but prices were tempered by fears that Delta-variant COVID outbreaks in major Chinese cities will weaken industrial activity and copper demand. Traders also watched U.S. jobs data for rate cues. LME aluminum edged up and nickel was slightly lower. | Element 29 Resources began a 4,000 m drill program at its Elida porphyry copper-molybdenum project in Peru to expand and upgrade known copper mineralization and potentially estimate a resource. The article does not indicate any near-term production, export, or demand changes that would move metal prices immediately. | Copper prices drifted sideways ahead of U.S. non-farm payrolls, which are expected to clarify the timing of Federal Reserve stimulus tapering and affect market liquidity. The dollar stayed near lows, while Delta COVID-19 concerns weighed on China’s demand; Yangshan premium improved. Aluminium slipped modestly, suggesting limited incremental impact. | China imported an additional 158,000 tonnes of primary aluminium in June, taking its half-year total to 744,000 tonnes. The report suggests China has continued accessing global supplies since early last year, implying reduced availability elsewhere and potential near-term support for aluminium prices as imports rise.

Copper prices fell as COVID-19 outbreaks spread, raising fears of weaker base-metals demand, while a firmer dollar pressured greenback-priced commodities. Reuters cites lower Shanghai copper and mixed but weaker nearby base-metal performance, including comments about premiums and weaker volumes in China. | SMM data showed China’s social aluminium inventories across eight consumption areas fell 26,000 mt on the week to 732,000 mt as of Aug 5. The drop was mainly attributed to low arrivals in Gongyi and Hainan, with Henan also affected by inefficient truck transport amid pandemic conditions. | SMM reported aluminium billet outbound shipments fell to 46,100 mt as downstream buying turned quiet amid surging aluminium prices. Social inventories in major consumption areas rose to 103,200 mt, with gains across most regions. Pandemic-driven production cuts and power-related plant reductions weakened demand, suggesting inventories may climb further next week. | Ubique Minerals entered a non-binding MoU to earn up to 80% of the Port Loko bauxite deposit in Sierra Leone via funding infrastructure, infrastructure spend, and a production shipment trigger. The project was previously halted due to COVID and is described as partially developed, with potential restart in a short timeframe.

Copper prices fell in London and were set for a weekly decline as a stronger U.S. dollar increased the cost of dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium and nickel also dipped slightly. The dollar firmed ahead of U.S. employment data that could support faster policy tightening. | Vedanta Aluminium Business, India’s largest aluminium producer, won multiple energy-management awards for its Jharsuguda smelters and captive power assets. Amex Exploration announced discovery of a copper-rich VMS zone at its Perron property, reporting 2.40% Cu over 7.8 m (including 3.92% Cu over 4.4 m) and accompanying Zn/Au/Ag grades. The article is copper-focused and exploration-stage. | Antaike said China’s 22 surveyed smelters produced 747,200 tonnes of refined copper cathodes in July, roughly flat versus June, with output expected to rise in August. Copper concentrate treatment charges rose to $57.50/t. The report covers tin separately and includes no aluminium or nickel impact. | Japanese beverage vendors are gradually shifting from plastic bottles to aluminum cans to reduce marine plastic pollution. The move suggests incremental demand for aluminium packaging materials and recycling streams as brands replace one-way plastic with can formats, potentially supporting short-term sentiment for aluminium prices.

Copper prices rose as supply worries grew in Chile, with mine workers at Caserones walking off and mediation talks extending at BHP’s Escondida to avoid strikes. Separately, power consumption restrictions in China’s Guangxi region could temporarily shut a large share of aluminum smelting capacity, supporting aluminium. | SMM reports aluminium futures at SHFE have risen and are near a 13-year high, with international prices up ~29% YTD. It cites strong earnings and demand outlooks from Alcoa and higher sales at Rusal, robust profit expectations at Chalco, and limited supply recovery plus low inventories, while noting possible Fed hikes and reserve releases. | Forum Energy Metals began drilling at its 100%-owned Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan after EM survey targeting. The release discusses planned follow-up drilling and prior intersections/grades for copper and nickel. However, it does not announce new assays or production, limiting near-term price impact. | Eagle Plains reports Rockridge will mobilize a summer/fall exploration program at the Knife Lake copper VMS project in Saskatchewan, using VTEM plus geophysics and follow-up drilling. The update references prior copper results and plans funded by Rockridge, but provides no near-term production or supply change. Impact on metals prices is likely limited. | Forum Energy Metals commenced drilling at its Love Lake nickel-copper-PGM project in Saskatchewan. The program targets electromagnetic anomalies for magmatic nickel-copper-PGM mineralization, with follow-up planned on copper and nickel occurrences reported in prior work. The release is exploration-focused and does not indicate production or trade changes.

SMM reports secondary copper-based rod producer operating rates fell to 59.53% in July, down 7.59 points month-on-month, amid power curtailment, late-July floods, and furnace repairs in Henan. Tight secondary copper supply forced plants to reduce or suspend output, with August rates expected to decline further. | SMM says operating rates of copper billet producers fell to 57.64% in July, down month on month, with backlog from June only partially supporting output. SMM reports aluminium billet stocks in major consumption areas rose 14,400 mt to 117,600 mt (+14%) on the week, while outbound shipments fell 4,900 mt to 41,200 mt amid quiet downstream purchasing. Downstream orders are sluggish and SMM expects demand to soften and supply to improve, with possible restocking next week. | SMM data showed China’s social aluminium inventories rose 7,000 mt on the week to 739,000 mt as of Aug 12. London copper prices rose 0.4% after strikes at two Chilean mines (Codelco’s Andina and JX Nippon’s Caserones) heightened supply disruption risk. Escondida approved a contract, limiting upside. SMM says operating rates for large aluminium processing firms fell due to spreading pandemic disruptions and increasing power curtailment, especially in Henan, with many plants cutting output or shutting. Off-season weakness in real estate and automotive reduces demand, while higher aluminium prices further pressure orders, limiting production in coming weeks. | Copper prices fell as China economic data (slower factory output and retail sales, weaker exports, COVID-19 disruptions) raised worries about reduced near-term demand. LME copper dropped 1.6% and ShFE copper eased. The release outlines further surveys to define drill targets but provides no resource or production figures. | Indian Railways expects to receive its first aluminium-bodied metro coaches in early 2022 after delays in a $17.3m contract with Dawonsys. Shanghai and LME aluminium prices hit multi-year highs as China’s aluminium output faces ongoing power curtailment and declines in electrolytic production. The state planning review limits for high energy-consuming projects in major producing provinces adds supply concern. Higher alumina costs from reduced refinery output further supports bullish sentiment. | Putin’s administration is moving toward emissions reporting and an emissions-reduction plan as the EU’s carbon border tax raises costs for carbon-intensive Russian exporters. Copper prices fell for a third day to the lowest in almost two months, driven by weak economic data reinforcing demand concerns and nervousness ahead of central bank messaging at Jackson Hole. It would require importers to buy and surrender certificates based on embedded emissions, using EU ETS auction prices. Aluminium is explicitly in scope; carbon-costs may raise import costs and weigh on demand.

Copper prices fell to a more than four-month low as a stronger U.S. dollar weighed on sentiment and investors saw easing supply disruptions. Blockaded transport near MMG’s Las Bambas mine in Peru reopened and operations resumed at Teck’s Highland Valley Copper after evacuations ended. The piece also shows aluminium down and nickel slightly higher. | China Customs data show July imports of aluminium ores and concentrates at 9.25 million mt, down 15.7% YoY, and YTD imports down 7.1% YoY. July exports of alumina rose sharply YoY, while imports of unwrought aluminium/products fell. The weaker ore inflow may tighten aluminium feedstock expectations. | Teck Resources resumed operations at its Highland Valley copper mine in British Columbia after an evacuation linked to wildfire threats. The company had restarted an oxygen plant to address smelting idling from poor air quality, but warned further smoke could cause outages. It may update guidance once wildfire risk eases. | SMM data said China’s social aluminium inventories across eight consumption areas rose by 2,000 mt on the week to 741,000 mt as of Aug 19. Copper fell 3.27% and LME copper declined. Aluminium dropped 1.68% on soft macro and slowing de-stocking, while nickel slid 2.09% amid weaker stainless steel prices. | Copper fell 2.9% to the lowest in more than four months as China dampened growth measures, the Fed signaled reduced bond buying (supporting a stronger dollar), and COVID-19 cases spurred demand worries. The sell-off also weighed on aluminium and nickel, with both down on the day. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) said it has refinanced $5.5bn of corporate debt, reducing leverage and cutting scheduled repayments by about two and a half years. The new terms lower the cost of debt, improving the company’s balance sheet, but the article does not indicate near-term production or market supply changes. | Tiger Finance provided $15.5 million in debtor-in-possession financing to Aluminum Shapes LLC, a New Jersey aluminum extruder and distributor, to fund Chapter 11 restructuring and a potential going-concern sale. The liquidity is intended to keep production and sales running, lowering near-term disruption risk for aluminum supply.

London copper is set for its steepest weekly loss in two months despite a Friday uptick, as China’s slowing growth, COVID-19 outbreaks, and U.S. bond-buying taper worries dent metal sentiment. Copper hit four-month lows on weak data. The piece also reports day moves in nickel and aluminium amid China reserve release uncertainty. | Marubeni began selling a carbon-neutral aluminium ingot (“Neutr-Al”) for Japanese buyers seeking lower emissions in transport, packaging and construction. It offsets GHG from bauxite, alumina, smelting and shipping using carbon credits. Pricing is variable by customer needs, with no fixed rate, and no supply disruption noted. | Copper prices rebounded 0.4% but are still headed for the biggest weekly drop since June, driven by concerns over slowing Chinese demand, expectations of U.S. monetary tightening boosting the dollar, and rising COVID-19 Delta cases. SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium volatility amid stronger USD and Fed hawkishness, which pressured prices. However, power curtailment and energy controls have already removed significant capacity and further cuts are possible. The peak-season recovery could reverse rising social inventories, offering support and upward momentum for aluminium. | Copper prices rose on Monday as risk-on sentiment returned, supported by signs China’s COVID outbreak is tapering and bargain hunting after recent weakness. Additional support came from a labor contract rejection at Chile’s El Teniente mine, raising supply concerns. Nickel and aluminum also increased alongside broader base-metal strength. | GlobalData forecasts global bauxite production to rise about 3.8% in 2021 to 372.8 million tonnes, citing higher output in Australia and Guinea and return to pre-COVID levels. It also highlights mine restart/expansions and a new railway improving freight volumes, plus plans for alumina refining in Boke. | Copper jumped 2% on the LME as investors returned after a recent slump, attracted by lower prices and hopes for improving global growth and electrification-driven demand. The piece also references improving euro-zone activity and fewer China COVID cases, alongside a May refined-copper surplus shift. Aluminium and nickel rose too. | Edgemont Gold Corp. said drilling equipment is on site and Phase I drilling is about to start at its Dungate copper-gold porphyry project in B.C., targeting IP anomalies and a quartz-pyrite breccia zone reported in older drilling. It is an exploration update without assays or production impact, limiting immediate price effects.

Six Southern California companies were convicted for allegedly scheming to avoid paying $1.8 billion in duties on imported Chinese aluminium. The case implies tighter compliance and enforcement of aluminium tariff obligations, potentially increasing effective import costs and reducing near-term inflows to the US market. | London copper slipped 0.5% as traders turned cautious ahead of a U.S. Fed symposium that may clarify stimulus tapering timing. A potential tightening outlook could support the dollar and hurt demand expectations. LME aluminium eased slightly, while ShFE nickel rose, showing mixed regional reactions. | Australia’s leading alumina exporter says pandemic disruptions have surged shipping rates for moving alumina to China to about $50/t versus ~$18/t historically. It also covers Fastmarkets’ procedural corrections to certain European low-carbon aluminium differential and extrusion billet premium assessments. The net near-term signal is mild upward pressure, mainly via the copper/LME move. | Copper prices rose for a third session on renewed physical demand, with buyers seeking physical metal and China’s Yangshan premium improving. Gains were restrained by uncertainty ahead of U.S. Fed comments at Jackson Hole affecting the dollar. Separately, alumina hit six-month highs after a Jamalco refinery blaze, supporting aluminum. | SMM reports aluminium prices fluctuating but trending higher, with A00 up 50 yuan/mt and secondary aluminium offers rising 100 yuan/mt. Scrap prices increased, imported ingots thinned and immediate import losses rose slightly. Oxygen and ADC12 costs continued to climb, and secondary plants raised quotes, though deals were subdued. | Tomago, Australia’s largest aluminium smelter, plans to source 100% renewable electricity by 2029 after its current power contract tied to AGL’s Bayswater coal plant ends in 2028. The shift raises doubts about Bayswater’s extended operation and signals lower-carbon, potentially lower-cost aluminium production going forward. | Capella Minerals signed a binding LOI to earn-in to Cullen’s Katajavaara and Aakenus gold(-copper) projects in northern Finland, requiring upfront and multi-year exploration payments.
