The piece headline states aluminium is trading near a three-year high as disruptions tighten the market. This suggests near-term supply constraints are improving aluminium price expectations, with bullish pressure likely in the coming days as market tightness outweighs any near-term demand weakness. | London three-month copper fell 0.3% to $9,350/t as investors stayed cautious before the Fed’s Jackson Hole meeting, weighing possible tapering and its impact via the US dollar and growth expectations. LME aluminium edged up 0.2%, with alumina prices rising after a Jamalco refinery blaze, suggesting limited near-term pressure. | China Customs data show July 2021 imports of unwrought aluminium alloy at 88,000 mt, down 48.5% year-on-year and 2.3% month-on-month, while exports also fell. The decline is linked to last year’s scrap restrictions, COVID-driven lower overseas demand, and closed import profits, plus Malaysia lockdown curbing shipments. | Henan bauxite production is declining amid environmental/safety checks, flooding, and COVID-19, leaving local supply unable to meet alumina demand. Operating rates are recovering, but tighter bauxite availability has pushed bauxite prices higher. Alumina plants are increasing imported bauxite use, which can tighten costs and supply conditions for aluminium. | ReportLinker’s market report highlights growing demand for aluminum extrusion from automotive/EV penetration, construction and green buildings, and increased use of recycled/value-added aluminum, supported by sustainability and BIPV incentives. It also notes COVID-19 disruptions but expects gradual improvement, implying supportive near-term aluminum sentiment. | The article says the Taliban controls Afghanistan and holds deposits worth over $1 trillion, including copper (and iron ore and gold). It argues improved stability could enable much greater mineral development over time, but it does not describe imminent production or export changes, limiting near-term price implications. | Altus Strategies started a 135 line-km induced polarisation geophysical survey at its Agdz copper-silver project in Morocco to prioritize targets for trenching and drilling. The release reiterates historical rock-chip copper grades up to 8% and additional positive results, but provides no direct aluminium-related market impact.

SMM reports that Chinese aluminium prices fluctuated at high levels. A00 aluminium eased by 60 yuan/mt to 20,540 yuan/mt, while secondary aluminium prices remained firm. Imports of aluminium ingots thinned, and die-casting demand stayed relatively rigid despite high production costs. Secondary aluminium is expected to fluctuate. | SMM data showed China’s social aluminium billet inventories fell 6,200 mt week-on-week to 124,400 mt as of Aug 26, led by declines in Foshan, Wuxi and Nanchang. Downstream restocking reflected high aluminium prices, but billet demand weakened; inventories are expected to stay muted next week due to low arrivals. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) is considering an IPO for H1 2022 and is weighing valuations and investor appetite. Sources say recent record aluminum prices in Europe and the US could influence the decision. EGA has smelters in the UAE and a bauxite mine in Guinea, valued by banks at $16–19B. | Reuters reports that Changji prefecture in China’s Xinjiang ordered several aluminium smelters to limit production from August, cutting monthly output by about 10% versus Jan–Jul levels. Xinjiang is a key global supply hub, so the supply restriction is supportive for short-term aluminium pricing.

Bruks Siwertell won an order for a road-mobile Siwertell unloader to discharge dry alumina at Marseille for Trimet France. The equipment claims dust-free, low-spillage handling with minimal material degradation and low energy use. The operator says reduced fines can lower energy needs in aluminium smelting. | London copper slid 0.1% as investors stayed cautious ahead of the Jackson Hole Fed symposium, where expected plans to cut stimulus could boost the dollar and make greenback-priced metals costlier. Copper also faced softer Chinese industrial profit growth. Aluminium rose on Xinjiang smelter output-limit risks. | SMM reports aluminium processing operating rates rose after Henan power curtailment was lifted and transport recovered, with Guangxi also improving. However, aluminium and silicon price surges lifted input costs and weighed on some secondary producers due to raw material shortages. Wire/cable and extrusion face pressure from higher aluminium prices, while demand expectations are mixed into peak season. | SMM reported that aluminium inventories in Shanghai’s bonded zone increased by just 1,700 tonnes week-on-week to 102,800 tonnes as of Aug 27. The weekly inventory growth rate was much slower than the previous week, suggesting less rapid accumulation and potential support for near-term aluminium sentiment. | Aluminum prices jumped to their highest since April 2018 after Xinjiang imposed output limits on five smelters and curbed electricity usage, reducing domestic supply. Benchmark aluminum rose about 1.5% and ShFE prices hit multi-year highs. The piece also mentions upcoming Chinese metal reserve auctions, including aluminum. | SMM reports the State Reserve Bureau will release the third batch of state metals soon: 30,000 mt of copper and 70,000 mt of aluminium, alongside 50,000 mt of zinc. The added official supply is likely to weigh on copper and aluminium prices over the coming days as inventories rise. | SMM reports that China’s State Reserve Bureau will begin releasing its third batch of strategic metals into the market soon, including copper (30,000 mt) and aluminium (70,000 mt), alongside zinc. LME copper futures dropped over 3% as perceived tightness in nearby supply eased and traders shifted focus to weakening Chinese demand amid growth concerns and power shortages. The headline suggests worsening energy-related conditions and investor risk appetite are weighing on industrial metals prices in the near term.

Giga Metals says it is progressing its Turnagain sulphide nickel-cobalt project in Canada toward carbon-neutral operations using hydro power and tailings carbon sequestration. This is supportive for nickel sentiment near term. | Europe faces a “catastrophic” magnesium shortage as China’s magnesium output slows due to power cuts and high energy costs. With European stockpiles expected to run out by late November, 25 key plants have shut or cut output sharply. Downstream demand disruption could increase imports. | The article headline reports a growing aluminum shortage that is increasing prices and reducing shipments of aluminum cans used by breweries in Southwest Florida. The situation suggests tighter availability of packaging-grade aluminum, likely supporting near-term upward pressure on aluminum prices as canmakers face supply constraints.

An interview argues that improved sorting technology (x-ray/sensor, LIBS) helps recycle aluminium with high purity and better alloy separation, enabling smelters to use more secondary metal. It claims large energy and CO2 savings versus primary production, and notes rising regulatory and automotive demand for lightweight recycled aluminium. | Swisstek Aluminium installed a 3,340-panel rooftop solar system under Sri Lanka’s net-plus scheme to cover up to 30% of its factory power use. The company says rising electricity rates drove the shift, lowering operating costs and emissions, and positioning its aluminium extrusions as greener for exports and sustainability-driven buyers. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium falling over 3% and spot A00 dropping sharply. East China remains in discounts as spot supply is adequate, while inventory rises by 10,000 mt. Declining coal prices have not offset high alumina/anode costs, shrinking profits; likely over 25% of aluminium capacity is loss-making. | Novelis announced Generation II of an aluminum intensive EV battery enclosure, using advanced roll-formed aluminum alloys and cell-to-pack architecture to cut weight and cost versus steel or extrusions. It claims improved energy density and lists aluminum’s thermal and corrosion benefits, supporting potential incremental demand for aluminum sheet and alloys. | Chalco reported third-quarter net income up over five-fold year on year, driven by buoyant aluminium and alumina prices. A live futures update states that LME aluminium fell 3.3% on high trading volume. The article also mentions Fastmarkets/Metal Bulletin database corrections and procedural delays for certain aluminium differential and billet premium assessments. Overall, the immediate market signal is negative for aluminium prices, while no clear copper or nickel impact is indicated. | A Reuters poll expects copper to keep falling as China’s growth slows and mine supply ramps up, shifting the market to a surplus. Shanghai aluminum hit a three-month low as thermal coal prices tumbled, easing concerns about rising production costs for the energy-intensive metal. Novelis will invest $375 million to expand its Zhenjiang aluminium recycling and production site in China for automotive sheet products, aiming for completion by mid-2024. The US and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs on EU steel and aluminum with a tariff-rate quota system, allowing historically based volumes to enter duty-free. The EU will suspend retaliatory tariffs and both sides will pause related WTO disputes, aiming to ease costs and shortages for downstream users. | The U.S. and EU agreed to resolve their Section 232 steel and aluminum tariff dispute by replacing existing EU aluminum tariffs with a tariff-rate quota that allows historically based volumes into the U.S. without those tariffs. The US will keep Section 232 tariffs at 25% for steel and 10% for aluminium, but will allow limited EU-produced metals into the US duty-free. The deal avoids a potential spike in EU retaliatory tariffs. | The US and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs with a tariff-rate quota for EU steel and aluminum entering the US market. Certain EU aluminium imports into the US will face no tariffs, though Section 232 remains partially in place. Peru copper supply risk should pressure near-term prices. | London copper rebounded after two weeks of losses, driven by tight LME refined inventories and worsening expectations for mined supply. Jefferies cited underappreciated supply constraints despite projects ramping. China’s factory signals were mixed, supporting volatility. Aluminium edged down and nickel fell on the day. | The U.S. Department of Commerce said it is consulting with Japan on bilateral and multilateral steel and aluminum issues. The statement highlights concerns about global overcapacity in steel and aluminum markets and the need for collective action, including restoring market orientation and addressing carbon emissions. | Biden and the EU agreed to lift U.S. tariffs on EU steel and aluminium, including a carbon-based arrangement on trade. The deal is expected to ease costs for manufacturers and consumers. Specific copper impacts are not described; aluminium effects are more direct. | Harley-Davidson shares jumped after the US and EU agreed to settle a dispute over steel and aluminum tariffs, which had exposed the company to a 56% tariff in Europe. The removal of tariffs should ease costs and supply-chain pressures, with supportive implications for aluminum trade expectations. | Biden and von der Leyen announce a U.S.-EU agreement to suspend tariffs on steel and aluminum and begin work on a Global Sustainable Steel Arrangement. The U.S. Department of Commerce said it is consulting with Japan on steel and aluminium trade issues, especially the effect of global overcapacity on aluminium markets. This “secular shift to higher costs” implies tighter profitability and potential supply strain, which could support higher aluminium prices over the coming days to weeks. | The U.S. and EU agreed to ease trade tensions: the U.S. will lift tariffs on some EU steel and aluminum imports starting Dec. 1 via a duty-free quota, while the EU removes retaliatory tariffs on U.S. goods. Biden and the EU announced a pause and revised approach to US-EU Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum. The deal uses tariff-rate quotas allowing most EU steel duty-free up to quotas, removes retaliation, and ends WTO disputes, aiming to address excess capacity and carbon policies. | Amcor temporarily reduced shipments to some customers after shortages in specialist resins, PET plastic and aluminium constrained production in the September quarter. LME three-month copper rose 0.8% and Shanghai’s December contract climbed 0.6% after two weeks of declines. Century Aluminum subsidiary Norðurál started construction of a new low-carbon billet casthouse at its Grundartangi, Iceland smelter, targeting 150,000 tonnes of billet capacity and doubling primary foundry alloy output. Production is expected to begin in Q1 2024, funded with green financing and renewable power, aiming for higher premiums. | Japan’s trade minister urged the U.S. to abolish extra tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from Japan in a meeting with the U.S. trade representative. Removing tariffs would likely ease aluminium supply constraints facing U.S. buyers, supporting prices in the near term, while steel is not directly tracked here. | The US and EU agreed to ease Section 232 tariffs on EU-origin steel and aluminum using tariff-rate quotas, with duty-free treatment for imports within quota levels and tariffs above. The deal should reduce trade friction and improve near-term aluminum supply expectations, while steel talks on overcapacity proceed separately. | SMM says alumina prices are turning downward, with the weighted index down 2.3% and Western Australia FOB down 9.4% on weaker freight and wider import window. Falling coal and caustic soda reduce production costs by 200-300 yuan/mt, implying further cost pressure and alumina (and likely aluminium) weakness. | Japan is asking the U.S. to abolish additional steel and aluminium tariffs. It also notes LME aluminium and nickel gaining modestly, reinforcing a broad supportive macro backdrop for industrial metals. | Aluminium prices are sliding, with the market expecting Russia to remove its export tax. Rusal shares on Hong Kong’s exchange fell over 5% as world aluminum prices declined. Alcoa plans to restart long-idled capacity at an Australian aluminium smelter. The restart would raise aluminium production/supply expectations in the near term, affecting market tightness. Traders may adjust positions based on the timing and pace of ramp-up, influencing aluminium price sentiment over coming days to weeks. | Alcoa plans to restart aluminum lines in Australia that have been idle since 2009. SMM data show China’s social aluminium inventories across eight consumption areas fell by 6,000 mt week-on-week to about 1.007 million mt as of Nov 11, with declines mainly in Nanhai, Shanghai and Gongyi. London copper eased slightly as the dollar firmed on expectations of an earlier U.S. rate hike after strong inflation data, which can slow global recovery and raise dollar-priced metals. LME aluminium fell while nickel rose; losses were partly offset by tight exchange inventories. | At SMM’s 2021 Nonferrous Metals Industry Chain conference, speakers said China faces a prominent copper resource shortage, with copper processing capacity exceeding smelting and smelting exceeding concentrate supply. Japan and U.S. ministers agreed to begin negotiations to resolve a trade dispute over higher U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum imports imposed under the Trump administration. Talks raise the prospect of reduced tariff pressure on aluminum shipments, improving near-term expectations for aluminum import flows and pricing. | Japan and the U.S. agreed to begin talks on additional U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminium imports from Japan. This may support aluminium prices in the near term. | The headline says the U.S. and Japan have agreed to hold talks on steel and aluminum tariffs. Any move toward higher tariff barriers could reduce or reroute aluminum imports, tightening expected supply and supporting near-term aluminium price sentiment over the coming days to weeks. | The piece argues China’s energy-saving and carbon-reduction policies affect electrolytic aluminum output by driving “double control” limits tied to electricity and coal tightness, citing significant shutdown capacity. It also notes aluminum demand/inventories softened after coal price falls, but overall policy-driven supply pressure should be supportive. | Japan and the U.S. agreed to start talks in Tokyo to address potential additional U.S. tariffs on Japanese steel and aluminum imports. The tariff focus directly targets aluminum trade, which can pressure near-term aluminum prices. | Aleastur will launch the GCC’s first aluminium grain refiners and master alloys hub in Bahrain, investing $15m and exporting about 85% of output to regional smelters and cast-houses. The facility aims to shorten lead times and strengthen downstream aluminium supply chains as construction activity resumes. | Copper prices fell as China’s property sector showed weakness, reducing expectations for metals demand. Coal prices dropped, easing smelter energy costs, but a stronger U.S. dollar lifted metal prices for non-dollar buyers. The LME warehouse squeeze eased. LME aluminium and nickel also fell on the same risk backdrop.

Copper prices rose 0.5% on the LME and edged up on the SHFE after improved risk sentiment following a Biden-Xi summit. The meeting supported wider market confidence, with the yuan strengthening. In the same snapshot, nickel was up while aluminium and zinc fell, showing mixed effects across metals. | The US and EU agreed to modify Section 232 tariffs on EU steel and aluminum imports by replacing tariff requirements with tariff-rate quotas (including 18,000 metric tons for aluminum) starting January 1, 2022 and halting EU retaliation. SHFE aluminium has dropped for four straight days as SMM social inventories rose, short positions expanded, and weakening coal and alumina prices reduced cost support. Spot prices in East China widened to large discounts, reinforcing expectations of more declines. | SMM describes several overseas aluminium capacity additions entering November, including Alcoa’s restart in Australia (35,000 mt/yr), Alcoa/South32’s Brazil Alumar restart, and EGA’s Al Taweelah smelter expansion (78,000 mt/yr). Rio Tinto will invest US$87 million to expand low-carbon aluminium production at its AP60 smelter in Quebec by adding 16 new smelting cells. The project should raise output by about 45% to 86,500 tonnes/year, with work starting in spring 2022 and completion by end-2023. | SMM says the alumina market is temporarily unlisted, with spot price discovery driven by supply tightness and sentiment. The update highlights a new copper-zinc mineralization style but is unlikely to materially shift near-term metals prices beyond copper expectations.

Evtec will take over Liberty Aluminium Technologies’ plants in Kidderminster and Coventry after Liberty Steel’s sale process, saving over 170 jobs. The intervention follows Jaguar Land Rover backing of its supply-chain role. A separate Witham site will still close, with about 64 redundancies, reflecting ongoing restructuring pressures. | Mosaic Minerals reports drilling intersected a 70-meter anomalous nickel zone (Pike Nickel East) and nearly 80 meters of nickeliferous mineralization, extending a nickel corridor by about 3.4 km and prompting plans for further drilling. China’s aluminium extrusion exports fell in October on the National Day holiday, but November is expected to rise as the SHFE/LME aluminium price ratio drops (to ~7.07) and export profits improve. Higher profitability and active trader interest suggest export volumes may reach or exceed prior highs in 2018. | SMM says Shanghai bonded-area aluminium ingot inventory has increased for four straight weeks and is expected to keep rising, widening import losses. Yangshan bonded premiums have fallen and holders have moved spots into bonded areas. Meanwhile aluminium imports keep falling while downstream restocks. Spot discount versus SHFE 2112 shrank to about 60 yuan/mt. | The article says the approved U.S. infrastructure bill will fund electrification, transit and EV charging stations, expected to increase demand for industrial metals. It links this to copper and mentions battery materials such as nickel. It also highlights Kutcho Copper’s feasibility study as supportive for future copper supply.  | The U.S. and EU plan to lower tariffs on steel and aluminum. Copper is not directly addressed. | The piece says global aluminum is in deficit and that inventories are running down sharply, with LME depots in places like Detroit and Vlissingen virtually empty and total China stocks around about two weeks of demand. It cites shortages and rising risk of consumers competing for limited supply.

China imported 86,265.32 tons of waste aluminum scrap in October, down month-on-month but up 82.4% versus a year earlier. Malaysia remained the top supplier at 23,969.92 tons. The larger scrap inflow suggests more recycling feedstock, potentially pressuring aluminium prices near term. | The U.S. and EU agreed to replace Section 232 tariffs on EU steel (25%) and aluminum (10%) with tariff-rate quotas effective Jan. 1, 2022. Eligible EU products enter the U.S. duty-free up to quota volumes; above-quota shipments face remaining tariffs. Quota administration and exclusions rules add some uncertainty. | Indonesia’s president reiterated plans to halt exports of commodity raw materials, including suspending bauxite exports in 2022 and copper in 2023. The article argues short-term bauxite supply impact is limited because imports from other regions and large domestic port stocks provide about three months of feed.
