The explicit nickel restriction is the immediate driver, while copper export shutdown is mentioned as a next-step possibility; aluminium is not addressed.

SMM cites customs data showing China’s aluminium wheel exports fell 25.8% m/m to 65,400 mt in October, with most regions and key destinations declining. SMM attributes weakness to a late-August silicon price surge, higher processing fees, reduced alloy (A356) output, and lower customer purchases. | Showa Aluminum Can (Apollo Funds’ portfolio company) has agreed to acquire Mitsubishi Aluminum’s aluminum rolled & extruded products business and shares of Universal Can. The deal aims to create a more integrated aluminum packaging and engineering group, potentially supporting aluminum downstream demand and production utilization as closing nears. | SMM says China’s secondary aluminium production is concentrated and increasingly makes silicon-aluminium cast alloys such as ADC12 and A356.2. It links higher silicon prices to higher aluminium alloy/ingot values versus primary aluminium and describes expanding secondary aluminium applications in autos and communications, supporting growing demand for silicon-aluminium feedstock and alloys.

Nickel and other industrial metals rose as optimism about China’s property sector returned. The headline implies improved demand expectations for construction-related materials, providing near-term support to prices. By extension, the move is likely to affect copper and aluminium alongside nickel. | Indonesia may ban bauxite exports from 2022 to boost domestic processing, but China’s Antaike says the impact on China’s aluminium industry should be limited. It argues Guinea’s large bauxite capacity surplus, mostly shipped to China for alumina production, can replace Indonesia’s shortfall, with room to increase imports further. | Indonesia may restrict bauxite exports from 2022, threatening China’s aluminium ore supply. Antaike says Guinea’s bauxite capacity surplus—mainly shipped to China for alumina production—could fully replace Indonesian volumes. China’s bauxite imports remain heavily reliant on Guinea and Indonesia, supporting expectations of minimal disruption to aluminium supply.

Craft breweries say they’re struggling to obtain aluminum beverage cans as shortages persist since COVID-era demand increases. The problem worsens because Ball Corporation requires large minimum orders of pre-printed cans and storage capacity for bulk deliveries. Brewers respond with smaller batches, rotating can usage, and sticker label printing. | Russia’s plan to abolish aluminium export tariffs on alloys for Rusal is expected to raise global aluminium supply. The article links the announcement to a sell-off in European and US spot markets, lower premiums, and falling LME/Shanghai prices. It also notes ongoing regional inventory pressure. | Norilsk Nickel raised its 2021-2030 investment forecast by $6 billion to $35 billion due to higher construction costs and added spending on energy and production infrastructure upgrades. The firm expects its capex peak to shift from 2026 to 2028, supporting improved nickel production plans after recent accidents. | Copper prices fell 0.9% as Omicron uncertainties raised concerns about near-term demand and growth. China manufacturing data was stronger, and LME copper stocks are falling, limiting losses. Nickel also eased with low stocks and high cash premiums, while aluminium slipped slightly amid broader logistics and supply tightness worries. | Copper fell to a two-week low as Omicron uncertainty hurt demand and growth expectations and Powell’s comments suggested faster Fed tapering. LME copper stocks are declining, but further selling followed the U.S. open. The piece also highlights tight nickel inventories and easing aluminium prices amid the same macro backdrop.

WRAL reports Ball Corp. will raise the minimum can-order quantities and increase prices for some customers, starting Jan. 1. Brewers say the changes could raise costs and force smaller breweries to find alternative suppliers due to higher cash outlays and logistics constraints. This implies firmer near-term aluminum can demand/pricing. | Ball Corporation is raising minimum orders for non-contract customers to about five truckloads (~1 million cans) per beverage, from one truckload. It also stops warehousing those customers’ cans and increases price-per-can by nearly 50% for some. Utah breweries report margin pressure and potential retail price hikes. | Copper rose about 1% on the LME as Omicron demand concerns eased and investors regained risk appetite. The piece cites low warehouse inventories as extra support. It also flags Chile royalty bill progress, nickel mine depletion, and aluminium’s tight nearby supply via 14-year-low stocks and a cash premium. | Craft brewery owners report aluminum can prices are skyrocketing due to inflation and higher energy costs, making cans harder to source and forcing some businesses to raise prices. Ball Corp. increased can pricing and minimum orders, indicating supply tightening and supportive near-term demand/cost pressures for aluminum. | Rio Tinto plans a $110 million expansion of its Quebec AP60 aluminum smelter, adding 16 smelting cells and using Quebec hydro power to cut emissions versus industry norms. The company frames it as a bet against coal-based smelting and expects growing aluminium demand, amid market tightness mainly linked to China. | China, the world’s largest aluminium producer, continues to face a shortage of primary metal. It imported an additional 140,000 tonnes in October, lifting year-to-date imports to 1.27 million tonnes. The headline indicates aluminium supply tightness responding to previously booming prices, supporting near-term price expectations.

SMM reports aluminium billet stocks in five major consumption areas fell 7,200 mt (down 9.14%) to 71,700 mt, extending seven straight weeks of decline. The drop is attributed to weaker billet arrivals, led by Foshan. North-west transport remains disrupted, so inventories may fall but more slowly, with possible week-to-week reversal. | Fujifilm said it will raise by 0.39 GBP per sqm the surcharge for aluminum offset printing plates from Jan 1, 2022. It attributes the move to unprecedented aluminum price increases plus higher energy, logistics, chemicals, and packaging costs, and adds a UK order charge to offset local logistics expenses. | Copper prices slipped as Omicron fears and slowing China growth reduced demand outlooks. LME copper was down 0.3% on the day and around 4% on the week, despite strong year-to-date performance. China growth and property guidance stayed cautious, while aluminium inventories rose 16% and other base metals moved mixed. | The US Commerce Department is delaying removal of Trump-era tariffs on UK steel and aluminum, citing UK threats to trigger Article 16 of the Northern Ireland protocol. The 25% steel and 10% aluminum tariffs remain, while negotiations with the UK stall, adding uncertainty over future trade terms despite UK denial of protocol linkage. | Vietnam’s customs holds about 1.8 million tonnes of seized aluminium worth roughly $5bn, but it’s unlikely to be released soon amid an ongoing investigation. With China curbing supply and global inventories drawn down rapidly, the hoard’s inaccessibility reinforces a tight aluminium market and supports higher prices.

Montana craft brewers report severe shortages and much higher prices for aluminum beverage cans. Supply-chain clogs, increased can-maker minimum order requirements, and longer lead times force breweries to use intermediaries, raising can costs. The story cites aluminum rising to a decade high and says beer price increases may follow for customers soon. | The piece notes that nickel is edging above $20,000/t, implying improving near-term sentiment for nickel. It also references aluminium supply staying intact despite output curtailments and mentions severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, factors that can affect aluminium transport and pricing.

Supply chain problems have caused an aluminum can shortage, and Ball Corp plans to raise minimum order sizes, forcing distributors to revise order quantities. Arizona craft breweries report difficulty obtaining normal can volumes, predicting higher costs, price increases, and scaled-back releases into spring, supporting higher aluminum-related prices. | A report says aluminum can demand is high, but shortages of cans available for shipment are affecting Central Alabama breweries. Ball Corporation links the issue to recycling input quality and supply chain constraints, forcing larger orders and higher costs that may be passed to consumers, potentially supporting near-term aluminum sentiment. | The article cites initial producer offers for Q1 MJP aluminium at $195/t and discusses Fastmarkets’ corrections/delays to European low-carbon aluminium differential and 6063 extrusion billet premium assessments. While it is directly linked to aluminium price benchmarks, it does not state a clear increase or decrease versus earlier offers.

China’s aluminium exports reached record levels in November, totaling 509,300 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis (+6.2% month-on-month, +20.1% year-on-year). Total exports in January–November rose 14.9% to 5.06 million mt, per customs data, suggesting increased outward supply. | The UK Trade Secretary says she will urge the U.S. to remove Trump-era tariffs on British steel and aluminum. The move follows an October U.S.-EU deal to drop their levies, which would likely lower aluminum trade barriers and improve supply conditions, supporting near-term aluminum sentiment. | China exported 509,300 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis in November, a record high since March 2020, with exports up 6.2% month-on-month and 20.1% year-on-year. Rumours of reduced export rebates and strong overseas demand boosted shipments as export profits rose and SHFE/LME ratios stayed low. | Trade experts and the World Bank forecast higher aluminium prices, citing strong demand and ongoing supply concerns. Aluminium output was affected this year by power crises in China, India and other locations, while alumina (needed for refining) output dynamics are highlighted. Prices are expected to rise before easing as energy constraints fade. | U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai met the UK trade minister and reiterated U.S. focus on tackling non-market excess capacity in steel and aluminum, including cooperation with partners on trade and carbon intensity. The U.S. also maintains Section 232 tariffs—25% on steel and 10% on aluminum—supportive for near-term aluminum market sentiment.

SMM data show China’s aluminium output fell 3% month-on-month to 3.07 Mt in November, with declines linked to power rationing in Guangxi/Chongqing and an accident at a plant in Yunnan. Some plants resumed, but December output is projected steady. Downstream restocking and lower social inventories may support prices into late December. | Inner Mongolia’s new materials development plan (2021-2025) calls for expanding downstream capacity for high-performance aluminium (over 2 million tons by 2025) and high-performance copper products (e.g., copper poles, conductors, foil). The policy supports industry chain buildout that may boost supply expectations for both metals. | Inner Mongolia’s new materials development plan (2021-2025) calls for expanding downstream non-ferrous metal industries, targeting high-performance aluminum (including alloys, foil, rods, cables) and high-performance copper (copper poles, conductors, foil). Planned capacity additions and industrial-chain extension can lift near-term demand expectations for aluminium and copper prices. | SMM data show China’s aluminium output fell to 3.07mn mt in November (-3% YoY), with monthly production affected by power rationing in Guangxi/Chongqing and an accident at a plant in Yunnan. Some plants resumed, but operating rates remain ~85.6%. Downstream restocking and expected lower end-December inventories are supportive. | South Korea’s antitrust regulator fined eight aluminum alloy makers a combined 20.7 billion won for colluding to fix bidding prices and split Hyundai Motor and Kia orders between 2011 and 2021. The FTC also ordered corrective steps and Hyundai/Kia will tighten bidding to prevent price collusion next year. | Alinta plans a $4bn offshore windfarm near Victoria to connect through Alcoa’s Portland smelter, enabling the large energy user to run on 100% renewable power. The move supports the smelter’s long-term viability and green aluminium demand, though the project is only at early planning stages and would take years to build. | Britain’s trade minister will press the U.S. Commerce Secretary to start talks on cutting U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum. The UK is concerned about rising pressure from retaliatory tariffs and faces changing trade flows after the U.S.-EU metals deal, which keeps broader U.S. aluminum tariffs in place. | General Motors says it will invest more than $51 million to add equipment at its Bedford, Indiana aluminum die casting foundry to produce drive unit castings for the Chevrolet Silverado EV and other applications. The expansion signals incremental demand for aluminum cast products, likely offering mild upward pressure on aluminum. | Australian energy group Alinta is evaluating a gigawatt-scale offshore wind project to decarbonise electricity for a large aluminium smelter in Victoria, which is described as the state’s biggest power user. The proposal targets green power procurement to support ongoing aluminium output and reduce power-related transition risk. | Copper fell for a third day as Omicron concerns offset China’s stimulus effects. A stronger yuan reduced costs for buyers, but investors cut Comex longs and remained cautious. Tight Chinese stockpiles and strong imports were supportive, while rising LME availability and weak deliveries tempered gains. | The EU’s CBAM remains largely unpriced in European spot aluminium premiums due to a long implementation timeline and an emissions scope not yet fully reflected. Still, decarbonisation policy raises production costs and keeps widening low-carbon aluminium differentials, supporting a modest bullish tone for aluminium prices and low-carbon product premiums. | The piece reports weak/volatile Shanghai aluminium trading despite a modest night-market rebound and a reserve-rate cut that has not lifted sentiment meaningfully. It cites solid November export data and limited short-term supply additions, but also notes mild long-short tension, improving yet soft terminal demand, and ongoing sensitivity to energy and Winter Olympic-related policy. | GM plans to invest $51 million in its Bedford, Indiana aluminum die-casting plant to install new equipment supporting EV drive unit castings for the upcoming Chevrolet Silverado EV and other applications. The expansion begins immediately, adding incremental aluminum-based demand tied to GM’s all-electric production ramp.

SMM reports aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas rose 13,800 mt to 85,500 mt versus the prior week, reversing a seven-week decline. The build was driven by higher billet arrivals and relatively weak downstream demand, and inventories are expected to stay on an upward trajectory. | South Korea’s Fair Trade Commission fined eight aluminium alloy product makers a combined KRW20.7bn for colluding to fix quotation and bidding prices and divide order volumes for carmakers including Hyundai and Kia from 2011-2021. The FTC expects a revised bidding system from next year to prevent future price fixing. | Rockridge completed a helicopter-borne VTEM EM and magnetic survey at its Knife Lake copper project in Saskatchewan and reports encouraging identification of conductors, while field analytical results are still pending. The update supports future drilling targeting for a Cu-Zn-Co VMS deposit, with no immediate production or supply change indicated. | Copper prices fell as Omicron-related concerns eased and the dollar firmed, adding pressure despite still-low LME inventories. LME on-warrant stocks rose to a one-month high, signaling easing nearby tightness. Nickel declined as well, while aluminium rose slightly, showing mixed pressure across industrial metals. | Fathom Nickel announced updated 2021 soil geochemistry at its Albert Lake property near the historic Rottenstone mine, identifying extensive Ni-Cu-Co anomalies consistent with magmatic nickel signatures and planning winter drilling. Nickel and copper exploration potential is increased, but no immediate production changes are stated, limiting near-term price impact.

SMM expects China’s waste aluminium supply to become relatively surplus as recycled-aluminium consumption slows with weak real estate and auto end-demand. Import volumes of unwrought aluminium alloys are forecast to fall further as overseas supply/imports tighten. Higher waste treatment costs may support some grades, but overall pressure remains negative. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) signed a three-year bauxite supply deal with China’s Bosai Group, providing several million tonnes annually from Guinea, with the first shipment expected in January 2022. It also cites rising Chinese bauxite imports and alumina capacity additions, supporting feedstock availability for aluminium production. | SMM reports aluminium billet conversion margins have stayed high but inventories across China have been declining for seven weeks amid sluggish downstream purchases and falling shipments. The inventory is expected to drop more slowly and possibly turn to accumulation in 1–2 weeks, with conversion margins peaking. Downstream extrusion operating rates may also fall. | Fastmarkets announced a consultation outcome affecting the publication times/holiday schedule of some base metal assessments in Asia, plus delays and corrections to European low-carbon aluminium differential assessments and aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premium midpoints. It also discusses methodology compliance for steelmaking raw-material indices, without citing new aluminium fundamentals. | SMM expects aluminium scrap supply to turn surplus as recycling output rises while downstream consumption in real estate and automobiles stays weak. China’s scrap import limits and logistics frictions reduce unwrought alloy imports. Cost and silicon-price effects may tighten some scrap spreads, but overall scrap surplus implies downward pressure on aluminium-linked pricing. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) will supply several million tonnes of Guinean bauxite ore per year to China’s Bosai Minerals Group under a three-year agreement. EGA already delivered over a million tonnes this year. Bauxite is the key input to alumina and aluminium, supporting feedstock reliability for Chinese output. | The article includes a Dow Jones news item noting that a military coup in Guinea has sent aluminum futures to a 10-year high, with Alcoa shares up about 2.3% in premarket. Because Guinea is central to bauxite supply, expectations for aluminum supply tightness should be bullish.

SMM cites China Business Industrial Research Institute data showing China imported 2.572 million tons of unwrought aluminium and aluminium products from January to October 2021, up 14.4% year-on-year. Import value rose to $7.518 billion, up 46%, indicating stronger supply inflows that may weigh on aluminium prices. | SMM reports China’s domestic aluminium social inventory fell to 91,500 mt, supporting a brief upside in SHFE trading. However, costs are shrinking as alumina and electricity prices fall. With heating-season production restrictions and colder weather likely weakening demand, SMM expects SHFE aluminium to remain rangebound with slight declines into month-end. | The headline indicates the U.S. is pitching a truce to Japan regarding Trump-era steel and aluminum tariffs. If negotiations lead to reduced or avoided tariff pressure, it would improve expectations for aluminum trade flows and pricing near term, likely providing support to aluminum markets over the coming days.

SMM says China’s aluminium scrap supply tightened further, pushing scrap prices up ~200 yuan/mt as recycling fell and processors reduced output after annual targets. Die-casting plants resumed buying and may stockpile ahead of end-month. Secondary aluminium alloy prices stabilised but edged higher despite weak demand and closed import windows. | SMM reports China’s electrolytic aluminium inventory fell to 915,000 tons (-75,000). Spot prices in Guangdong slipped slightly but aluminum discounts narrowed. The piece argues macro support for stable growth and faster inventory movement “into the warehouse” boosts sentiment, while falling alumina prices temper gains. | Shanghai aluminium futures rose to a more-than-two-week peak, up 1.7%, driven by low inventories and improving demand as China eases energy consumption curbs. Reported lack of imminent large-scale output restarts and potential production cuts in northern China add support, lifting both ShFE and LME earlier. | LME prices are described as mostly lower while the report highlights a near 75,000-tonne inflow of aluminium into Asian warehouses. Such warehouse additions increase readily available supply in the region, which can weaken nearby pricing and contribute to the overall downside move in aluminium. | LME aluminium prices fell after LME-registered warehouse inventories jumped by 67,750 tonnes to 977,400 tonnes. Expectations of solid demand and still-tight Chinese supply limited losses, but cash/3-month premiums narrowed and the market reversed earlier gains. Aluminium has dropped 18% since October highs. | Norsk Hydro will invest about US$230mn to convert a Brazil alumina plant to natural gas. The change is intended to alter the plant’s energy inputs, potentially lowering operating costs and improving production economics. Better near-term alumina affordability can support expectations for aluminium output. | The US is offering Japan a deal to resolve disputes over 2018-era tariffs on steel and aluminum. The proposed arrangement would allow a quota of imports to enter the US free of duties, similar to the US-EU suspension. Japan seeks full abolition, but talks are ongoing.

LME three-month aluminium fell 0.96% after LME warehouse inventories jumped by 67,750 tonnes, though declines were capped as demand stayed strong and China supply was expected to remain tight. The article also says copper dropped 0.38% with LME registered stocks rising for a fourth day. | China’s aluminium production in November fell 1.8% year-on-year to 3.1 million tons, with electrolytic operating capacity still constrained in some regions due to power reasons. Several areas reduced energy-intensive output amid electricity pressure, and a tank leakage accident occurred at a plant in Yunnan, keeping supply tight. | SMM says alumina prices have plunged, reversing prior gains, and bauxite prices in Shanxi, Henan, and south-west regions are under pressure or expected to fall further. Weaker alumina profitability, low acceptance of high-priced bauxite, increased north China mining and discounts, and planned procurement cuts support bearish near-term aluminium sentiment. | SMM says aluminium fluoride prices rose in November, driven by largely unchanged supply and restocking by some aluminium smelters for winter. Major firms lifted purchasing quotations, with Chinalco’s price up to 12,500 yuan/mt, improving producer profits and operating rates. Fluorite powder gains and expectations point to further December increases. | London copper was flat as investors awaited a key U.S. Fed decision on timing of tapering and potential earlier rate hikes. The dollar’s strength and possible liquidity tightening were a focus. LME aluminium eased, nickel was steady, and Shanghai contracts were slightly lower, indicating limited net near-term momentum.

SMM said aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas fell 10,000 mt WoW to 75,500 mt on Dec 16, led by sharp declines in Wuxi and Nanchang. Warehouses reported low arrivals because billet makers were less willing to ship amid low conversion margins. Next week inventory is expected to be largely unchanged.

SMM reports the inner metal market trading higher. Shanghai nickel rises over 2.7% and Shanghai aluminium over 2.7% (around 2%+). Copper also stays high and volatile, with spot prices up for two days, though downstream buying weakens after replenishment. Overall sentiment remains supported by a weaker USD. | SMM reports a broad metal rally in China as the dollar moved higher then the market stayed bid. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.75% and Shanghai aluminium about 2.7% on supportive spot dynamics. Copper remained high and volatile for two straight days, despite weaker downstream replenishment. | Rusal said its previously long-delayed Taishet aluminum smelter in Siberia has begun production, with the first phase completed and testing starting. The plant’s planned first phase capacity is 428,500 tons annually. Restarting output increases Russian aluminum supply expectations, potentially weighing on prices.

SMM notes early mixed trading: Shanghai aluminium rose ~1.6% on low inventories and tight supply, while nickel rose slightly as tight absolute inventories eased via limited imports, though fundamentals remain weak. Copper was pressured by a stronger dollar amid BoE/ECB tightening and Covid cases, keeping moves mixed overall. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) signed a three-year deal to supply China’s Bosai Minerals with several million tonnes of Guinea bauxite annually, starting with shipments in January 2022. EGA highlights its growing role as a bauxite supplier, with mining, rail transport to Kamsar port, and transshipment capacity. | RUSAL announced the opening of a new low-carbon aluminium smelter in Taishet. The start of production and potential lower emissions/cost competitiveness can influence market expectations for aluminium supply and operating costs, which may provide near-term support to aluminium prices, subject to ramp-up timing. | The article argues aluminium should stay supported near term: electrolytic aluminium demand is improving, social inventories have fallen for eight consecutive weeks, and supply remains tighter due to limited electricity production. It also points to stronger auto/NEV output and aluminium-profile exports. Despite Fed debt reduction/interest-rate expectations, prices are expected to oscillate on the upside. | China’s aluminium imports hit an annual record within 11 months, with November unwrought and product imports jumping to 397,915 tons (+34% vs October; +109% y/y). Electricity restrictions at domestic smelters reduced supply, enabling arbitrage into cheaper overseas metal inflows.

China’s bauxite (and concentrates) imports fell 19% month-on-month to 7.7 million tons in November, and were also down year-on-year. Guinea remained the largest supplier but exports to China declined as well. Reduced bauxite inflows can pressure alumina supply expectations and bearishly affect aluminium. | China’s imports of raw (primary) aluminium rose to 229,400 tons in November, up 64% from October and 309% from the same month last year, per China customs data cited by SMM. Imports from India also surged, suggesting stronger near-term physical demand that could support aluminium prices.

Alro Slatina, Romania’s major vertically integrated aluminum producer, is launching a total activity shutdown process due to soaring energy prices. As Romania’s largest energy consumer, the firm faces cost pressure that may curb smelting output in the near term, influencing European aluminum supply expectations. | LME and SHFE aluminium prices rose sharply as traders worried European producers in countries such as Montenegro and Germany could restrict output at year-end. The concern stems from energy shortages and surging electricity prices. With Western Europe’s aluminium capacity and operating rates already relatively low, bearish supply risk supports long positions. | China’s aluminium extrusion exports rose to 88,100 mt in November (+18% MoM, +5% YoY), with EU shipments up. The SHFE/LME ratio fell to 7.07, improving export margins and encouraging traders. Exports are expected to correct slightly in December around Christmas/New Year despite lower LME ratio. | China’s raw aluminium imports rose 64% month-on-month in November to 229,000 tonnes, amid production constraints and tighter energy controls affecting smelters. Market participants expect imports to fall in December because domestic aluminium prices have been declining and import buyers face losses. Alumina imports also dropped on oversupply. | The EU plans to impose additional tariffs on imports of Chinese aluminum foil, citing subsidy concerns. The measure is expected to alter trade flows and effective supply into the European market, potentially tightening availability for aluminum foil and supporting near-term aluminum pricing versus a tariff-free outlook. | The article reports that Europe’s largest aluminum smelter has reduced output following a sharp surge in electricity power prices. Higher energy costs can force curtailments in aluminum production, tightening short-term supply and likely providing upward support to aluminum prices in the near term.

Romanian aluminum producer Alro Slatina plans to shut down and has started reducing output by 60%, citing high energy prices and energy scarcity. The union chairman said the company cannot profitably produce aluminum at current price levels of $2,300–$2,600/ton, prompting closure of production lines. | Norsk Hydro will invest NOK 60 million to upgrade electrolysis cells at its Årdal, Norway aluminum plant starting spring 2022. The project targets improved worker safety and reduced energy use (about 860 MWh) and lower CO2 emissions via next-generation cell control systems, with rollout expected late 2022. | SMM says aluminium billet stocks across five major consumption areas fell 900 mt to 74,600 mt week-on-week. Weekly arrivals remained low as manufacturers were less willing to ship amid falling RCs, with some planning production cuts. Demand is muted into year-end, so inventories may rise next week. | Copper and alumina operations in northern China have been ordered to reduce output to curb emissions. Such curtailments are typically seen as near-term supply tightening for copper and for alumina feedstock into aluminium refining, supporting price expectations over the coming days and weeks. | The news says copper and alumina operations in northern China were ordered to reduce output to curb emissions. Such production curtailments can constrain near-term supply expectations. That typically adds upward risk to prices, but in the short run may also pressure metals if demand expectations weaken alongside compliance-related disruptions. | Aluminium stays near two-month highs after rising over 5% in two days. Record European power prices raise fears of higher smelting costs and potential shutdowns, including a reported halt of 3% capacity at Dunkerque. LME aluminium stocks on-warrant fell sharply, signaling tighter supply. | Brazil has set an aluminium import quota of 350,000 tonnes for 2022. By limiting allowable imports, the policy can reduce near-term supply into Brazil, tightening availability for local buyers and supporting expectations for higher aluminium prices over the coming days to weeks. | Adaro Aluminium Indonesia signed an LOI to invest $728 million in a new aluminium smelter in North Kalimantan’s Green Industrial Park. The project would use hydropower from an 11,000 MW Kayan river plant and is positioned as a ‘green’ supply push targeting growing demand for aluminium products.

China’s aluminium wheel exports were 67,300 mt in November, down 25% m/m, with the weakness blamed on suppressed September production caused by volatile silicon and magnesium prices. As silicon and magnesium stabilized in Oct–Nov, aluminium alloy and wheel output recovered and overseas buyers accepted higher margins, suggesting exports may rebound in December. | SMM reports Shanghai aluminium rising amid European energy and electricity price pressure. Alro (Romania) plans to cut aluminium output by 60% due to high power costs, with broader risk of further smelter curtailments. LME and domestic inventories fall while spot discounts fluctuate, implying tight near-term supply. | Adaro Energy signed a letter of intent to invest $728m in a new aluminum smelter in North Kalimantan, Indonesia, targeting production of aluminum ingots or sheets. While it signals potential future supply growth, construction is expected to take around two years, limiting immediate price impact.

More MJP aluminium Q1 deals have emerged around $177/t, but buyers continue to push back. The piece also notes Fastmarkets’ administrative corrections and delayed low-carbon aluminium differential assessments, while the deal coverage implies ongoing resistance to higher aluminium prices. | Copper rallied to a one-month high after the LME reopened following a two-day holiday break, rising 1.2% to about $9,679/t, with prices supported by COMEX-LME arbitrage. The piece also notes mixed price action in aluminium and nickel on LME and Shanghai, but no metal-specific drivers beyond trading conditions. | Alcoa plans to reduce aluminum production in Spain after energy costs surged. The move signals margin pressure for smelters and implies lower near-term primary output, which can tighten aluminum supply conditions and lend support to spot and short-dated aluminum prices in coming days.

Alcoa will stop primary aluminium production at its Spanish plant for two years, citing soaring European energy prices. The move adds to recent production cuts by other major smelters in the region, including Aluminium Dunkerque. With supply restrained by high power costs, expectations for aluminium prices move higher. | Alcoa said it agreed with workers to curtail its San Ciprián aluminum smelter in Spain by two years because European energy prices are high. Output reduction covers 228,000 mt/year capacity starting Jan. 1, 2022, while the casthouse and alumina refinery keep running. Alcoa expects restructuring charges. | Alcoa will suspend aluminium production at its Spanish plant for two years, citing soaring European energy prices. The move follows an earlier production cut by Aluminium Dunkerque Industries France after energy costs hit record highs, worsening financial pressure on European smelters. | The US White House announced adjustments to aluminum imports under Section 232, including a 10% tariff on aluminum products from most countries and a quota-based exemption framework for EU member states. Imports exceeding quotas face duties; qualifying EU products are excluded within set limits until end-2023. | Yunnan Aluminum announced a RMB 3 billion non-public share offering to fund and replace capital for its Wenshan green low-carbon hydropower and aluminium integration project. The project has been completed and is in production, with proceeds intended for project use, working capital and loan repayment, potentially improving future profitability and output confidence. | Copper traded in a narrow range ahead of the New Year holiday, slightly lower on the LME and Shanghai. Supportive factors include falling inventories and expectations of expanding demand. Copper risk comes from uncertainty over Las Bambas’ restart. Aluminium rose on supply concerns and Alcoa’s temporary Spain shutdown; nickel also ticked up. | SMM’s review argues aluminum prices face support in 2022 because energy-consumption “double control” limits new electrolytic aluminium capacity and production recovery, keeping supply tight. Demand is relatively stable, with weaker real-estate aluminum in 2022 and faster but smaller photovoltaic growth. Fed/dollar strength suppresses commodities broadly. | Norsk Hydro said its Slovalco aluminium plant in Slovakia will cut output further because power costs remain high. Production will fall to about 60% of capacity from roughly 80% currently, removing an additional 35,000 tonnes annually. The supply reduction is supportive for aluminium pricing. | SMM reports aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas rose 5,700 mt to 80,300 mt on Dec 30, a 7.64% weekly increase. Most markets saw gains, notably Huzhou and Foshan. Year-end cash-flow management and weaker orders kept the market subdued, and sluggish demand around New Year may drive further inventory growth. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed. Aluminium rose sharply as European aluminium output is cut due to surging electricity prices, with falling social inventories. Nickel also gained on tight supply and lower SHFE/LME inventories, despite future Philippine ore pickup. Copper edged down slightly amid a quiet spot market. | The report describes early-session mixed moves across metals: Shanghai aluminium up ~1.56% and Shanghai nickel up ~1.27%, while copper fluctuates around 70,000 yuan/ton. It attributes aluminium strength to European energy price shocks and Alcoa planning a two-year Spanish electrolytic shutdown, but overall trading remains range-bound.

Alcoa says it will curb primary aluminium production at its Spain plant for two years, citing soaring European energy prices and fresh record power costs. The move follows earlier output reductions by other regional aluminium smelters, highlighting growing supply risk for aluminium in Europe. | Biden issued proclamations replacing some Section 232 tariffs with TRQs for EU steel and aluminum. Aluminum faces in-quota treatment only up to specified TRQ volumes (and certain exclusions may count against quota), while out-of-quota volumes remain subject to tariffs. The U.S. will monitor implementation through Dec. 2023. | SMM cites Sinolink Securities forecasting a 110,000 mt aluminium shortage in 2022, with supply-demand kept in a tight balance. Production capacity in Yunnan and Guangxi may rise as energy-consumption problems ease, while demand from real estate and manufacturing rebounds, but overall the market stays constrained into 2023. | Soaring natural gas prices have increased European power costs, driving cuts in primary aluminium production at Aluminium Dunkerque, Alcoa’s Spain plant shutdown, and Alro’s 60% reduction. Delivery premiums are rising again while LME inventories remain under 1 million tons, tightening supply and supporting aluminium prices.

SMM reports aluminium prices in China rebounded toward 20,000 yuan/ton as inventories fell and European electrolytic capacity was reduced. Alumina costs also declined, improving per-ton margins. With demand from new energy vehicles rising and policy carbon-reduction support expected, aluminium prices are expected to stay high and profits improve. | SMM reports aluminum profits per ton have surged, supported by falling inventories to under 1 million tons and easing end-of-demand power cuts with order resumption. Costs for electricity, alumina, and pre-baked anodes have declined since late October. Despite energy-consumption limits keeping supply tight, improving demand points to bullish aluminum price pressure. | Copper fell about 1% on the LME as the dollar strengthened and Treasury yields rose, with traders pricing an early U.S. Fed hike. Rising Omicron cases also weighed on sentiment. The same macro pressure pulled down LME aluminium and nickel, alongside other base metals. | Copper prices fell as a firmer U.S. dollar and uncertainty about Omicron’s impact weighed on industrial-metals sentiment. Three-month copper on the LME dropped 1%. The article also notes declines in LME aluminum and nickel on the day, reflecting broader macro-driven bearish pressure. | SMM reviews China alumina price moves in 2021: a surplus early-year, rising prices from late July to a 10-year high in early November driven by higher costs, production cuts, and political disruption, then a Q4 decline on falling costs, surplus, and cheaper imports. Aluminium prices trended down in Q4. | Aluminium prices rose as market participants worried that prolonged high European power costs could trigger further supply disruptions; LME aluminium inventories are down over 60% since mid-March. The same inventory-tightness theme and cancelled warrants point to potential further draws on nickel supply, supporting a broader bullish tone. | China’s decarbonization and emissions compliance have reduced aluminium output and tightened supplies, contributing to Chinese and global deficits. Tonnage has shifted from western stockpiles toward Asia, shrinking LME warehouse levels, widening contango/backwardation dynamics, and lifting global aluminium premiums and prices to records in 2021. | Aluminum prices rose about 1% on Tuesday as traders flagged supply disruptions linked to prolonged high electricity and gas costs, with low LME inventories and expectations of additional warehouse departures. Industrial metals broadly gained on a weaker dollar amid uncertainty in Europe’s energy market. Nickel also increased alongside the move.

London copper fell 0.4% from a six-week high as the U.S. dollar held firm on expectations for early Federal Reserve rate hikes, potentially reducing market liquidity. LME aluminium dipped 0.1% and nickel slipped 0.7% in tandem, reflecting broad macro pressure. Separate data points were also cited. | Vedanta Aluminium will deploy 23 lithium-ion electric forklifts at its Jharsuguda aluminium smelter, replacing diesel units. The move is aimed at reducing diesel use and emissions and improving productivity and safety through smart fleet management and IoT monitoring. It is an operational decarbonization initiative, not a supply change. | LME aluminum rose after traders cited supply concerns from long-term high European electricity prices and low inventories, with more metal expected to leave warehouses. Copper also increased alongside a weaker US dollar, which can spur demand for dollar-priced commodities. Energy/gas uncertainty in Europe remains a key risk factor. | Aluminium prices surged to a two-month peak as high European power prices reinforced expectations of large market deficits. Prompt European power forecasts (e.g., weaker German wind supply and stronger French demand) raise smelting costs, while LME aluminium stocks have fallen sharply and cancelled warrants suggest continued outflows. | CBP set out how tariff-rate quotas for EU steel and aluminum—negotiated to replace Section 232 tariffs—are administered by HTS group and by EU country, split by quarter on a first-come basis. After quarterly quota closure, remaining EU aluminum shipments face Section 232 tariffs unless exclusions apply.

LME aluminium surged to a two-month high amid high European electricity prices and sharply lower LME inventories, with large cancellations of warehouse receipts suggesting more metal could leave. Aluminium spot prices rose sharply, reinforcing tight European supply fears. LME copper meanwhile settled lower on the day, limiting net cross-metal bullishness. | LME aluminium jumped to a two-month high as high European electricity prices and sharply lower LME stocks/warehouse receipt cancellations raised expectations of severe shortages. Aluminium’s supply tightness is reinforced by soaring spot prices and estimates of European capacity shutdown. LME copper settled lower during the same recovery session. | South32 and Alcoa plan to invest $175m to restart the Alumar aluminium smelter in Brazil, previously on care and maintenance since 2015. Using 100% renewable power, the project targets production in the June quarter of 2022 and up to 447,000 tpa capacity by March 2023. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) said it produced an all-time record 1,561,222 tonnes in 2021, beating its 2020 output and marking more than 20 million safe working hours without any lost-time injuries. The release highlights stronger smelting performance at a major ex-China supplier. | US Customs and Border Protection published guidance and Federal Register proclamations for EU steel and aluminum TRQs under Section 232. The aluminum quota begins processing Jan 3, 2022, with quota allocation rules and renewed exclusions through 2023. Reduced effective tariff burden and managed import volumes may weigh on aluminum prices near term.

LME copper futures closed down after Federal Reserve minutes signaled interest rates could rise faster, weighing on risk appetite and liquidity. The stronger dollar reduced demand for dollar assets. Yangshan copper premium fell, suggesting softer Chinese import demand, while aluminium also eased as base metals fell despite tight warehouses. | Japan buyers agreed liquid aluminium shipment premiums of $177/mt for Jan–Mar, down 20% from the prior quarter and below $220/mt in Oct–Dec. The cut reflects weaker Japanese spot premiums and overseas prices, signaling reduced demand pressure. Japan is Asia’s largest aluminium importer, with bids above LME. | Aluminium rebounded toward $3,000/t as LME-registered warehouse stocks dropped sharply, with traders fearing Europe’s and Asia’s energy crunch will force additional smelter cutbacks. Analysts cite energy as ~40% of operating costs and warn significant capacity is at risk, reinforcing a worsening supply deficit. | Kentucky’s legislature introduced a bill ordering the state to recover a $15 million investment (with interest/fees) from a failed aluminum mill deal near Ashland, connected to Braidy Industries/Unity Aluminum. The project’s financing fell short amid allegations of fraud. The bill sets deadlines and limits further time for private funding. | Market participants expect European aluminium scrap and secondary ingot prices to stay robust into 2022 despite a sharp recent decline in DIN226. They cite bullish sentiment from tight raw-material supply, rising automotive order inquiries for early 2022, and continued support from record scrap prices and growing demand for low-CO2 recycled aluminium.

Europe’s aluminium smelters face a winter power crunch, with operators announcing curtailments totaling over half a million tonnes of annual capacity. Others are flexing output to manage power-load price spikes. The resulting supply squeeze is likely to affect aluminium market expectations over the coming days to weeks. | SMM’s daily review says most metals rose, led by stainless steel, with Shanghai aluminium up over 1% for a fourth straight session and aluminium inventories decreasing. Shanghai nickel rose about 2.5% as spot supply remained tight and premiums increased. Copper also edged higher, supporting a bullish near-term tone. | The news says the aluminium queue at Port Klang has fallen to just under 60 days, suggesting congestion is easing and shipments should move more smoothly. With improved logistics and less near-term delivery tightness, market expectations for aluminium availability may turn more favourable over the coming days. | Indonesia’s President said the country will halt bauxite ore exports in 2022 and copper ore exports in 2023 to drive downstream investment and shift exports toward higher-value semi-finished and finished products. Indonesia is a major bauxite and copper exporter, implying reduced global ore supply. | Indonesia’s president said Jakarta plans to stop exporting bauxite ore in 2022 and copper ore in 2023 to accelerate downstream investment and shift toward higher-value products. Indonesia is a major exporter, and the move is expected to affect near-term supply expectations for copper concentrates and aluminium feedstock. | SMM’s afternoon review notes Shanghai nickel rising about 2.56% and Shanghai aluminium gaining roughly 1.1% by midday, while copper also moved higher. For copper, Tianjin epidemic-related disruption left spot trading deserted, with electrolytic copper discounts narrowing. Overall moves appear intraday and mixed across the strip. | Indonesia’s President said the country will stop exporting bauxite ore from 2022 and copper ore in 2023 to accelerate downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. Indonesia already banned nickel ore exports in 2020, signaling a broader shift that could tighten traded ore supplies supporting bearish price pressure. | Indonesia plans to halt bauxite ore exports in 2022 and copper ore exports in 2023 to boost downstream processing and exports of higher-value products. It already banned nickel ore exports from Jan 1, 2020. These actions tighten supply of key raw inputs for aluminum and copper markets.

Huafeng Aluminum reported a 2021 pre-increase, with profit for shareholders expected at RMB 430–510 million, up on year due to recovering national demand and rapid new-energy vehicle growth. The company links improved output and sales to demand for aluminum foil and heat-transfer aluminum materials. | SMM reports China electrolytic aluminum inventory falling to 752,000 tons and spot prices for aluminum rising across Guangdong and the Yangtze River. It attributes strength to continued destocking of domestic aluminum ingots and expected supply tightness in Europe from energy-related production disruptions, despite some macro pressure from Fed hawkishness. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed around Jan 11. Nickel led with a 3.45% gain, supported by falling SHFE inventories, LME nickel and tight spot pure-nickel supply. Aluminium rose 1.97% with inventories down, despite weakening demand. Copper was essentially flat, though LME copper inventory fell. | SMM’s daily review reports mixed metal moves in Asia: Shanghai nickel rose more than 3% and stainless steel also jumped, while Shanghai aluminium climbed nearly 2%. Copper edged down slightly (~-0.01%). The piece attributes some nickel support to low inventories and improved spot tightness. | Novelis will invest about $365 million to expand in Todd County, Kentucky, building a new recycling center with 240,000 tons annual capacity and 140 jobs. The project adds sheet ingot casting and shredding and increases recycled aluminium input for automotive markets, implying supportive aluminium supply/demand dynamics.

SMM reports sharp rises in aluminium prices, citing higher Guangdong and Yangtze River spot ingot prices. It notes China’s electrolytic aluminium inventories fell to 752,000 tons. The piece attributes support to Europe’s energy-driven production disruptions and ongoing domestic ingot stock build, reinforcing bullish near-term aluminium sentiment. | Nickel prices jumped to a 10-year peak as investors focused on dwindling LME/ShFE inventories, tight nearby supplies (cash premiums), and forecasts of a widening market deficit for EV batteries. The broader industrial metals complex also rose, with copper and aluminium moving higher as supply worries and China stimulus expectations improved sentiment. | Kentucky lawmakers are considering clawing back $15 million of taxpayer funds from a stalled aluminum rolling mill project in northeastern Kentucky. The plant, financed since 2017, still hasn’t broken ground. A bill would require repayment by year-end unless investors are lined up, adding pressure and uncertainty for aluminum capacity timelines. | Kentucky lawmakers are advancing Senate Bill 48 to recoup $15 million of taxpayer funds from a stalled aluminum mill project by Unity Aluminum (formerly Braidy Industries) unless the company repays voluntarily or secures financing. The project has not broken ground after five years, raising production/capacity uncertainty for aluminum.

Huayou Cobalt forecast a 2021 net profit jump, citing strong demand and higher prices for new-energy battery materials. It also announced an acquisition related to a lithium mine in Zimbabwe and highlights integrated nickel/cobalt-to-lithium production capacity growth. The news is company-specific and offers no clear near-term market dislocation for traded metals. | China’s stocks turned positive after a four-day losing streak, helped by upbeat global sentiment and a jump in crude oil prices. Resource and energy shares supported the index, including gains in Jiangxi Copper and Aluminum Corporation of China (Chalco), implying mild near-term support for copper and aluminium. | Nickel prices surged to their highest in a decade, driven by continued LME and Shanghai inventory declines, strong backwardation, and tightening warehouse stocks that signal a potential squeeze. Copper also rose to fresh peaks on renewed supply fears. Aluminium edged higher amid broader industrial metals strength and improving China sentiment. | SMM expects 2022 LME and SHFE aluminium to trade within broad ranges and form an inverted-V pattern, citing expanding alumina capacity, recovering aluminium output, earlier gains tied to cost/energy dynamics, and a later drop after coal-price regulation. Demand is seen improving via autos, new energy vehicles and PV, though Fed hikes pressure metals overall. | Rio Tinto’s Tomago Aluminium faces shipping delays and chronic shortages, forcing the plant to create back-up plans to keep operating. CEO Matt Howell says container deliveries are being delayed, slowing inbound specialist materials and components needed for the NSW smelter. The disruption increases near-term aluminium supply risk. | Vedanta Aluminium says its Jharsuguda smelter became India’s biggest industrial consumer of renewable energy in 2021, procuring about 2 billion units of RE power and large volumes of renewable energy certificates. The move is aimed at lowering emissions intensity, not reported as changing output or shipments. | SMM says copper prices rose slightly, with improving demand expectations and sharply lower inventories. Aluminium also rebounded, aided by falling inventories, downward domestic operating capacity, and reduced imports. It attributes supply disruption to European smelters cutting output from power shortages and notes profit recovery in electrolytic aluminium. | The SMM review says copper prices rose slightly as demand expectations improved and inventories fell, citing Zijin Mining. Aluminium rebounded with lower inventories and weaker domestic output, plus supply disruption from European smelter power shortages. Overall, price action is supported by both demand and supply-side factors. | French aluminium smelter Dunkerque will curtail an additional 5% of output, bringing total cuts to 15%, citing high energy costs. Similar curtailments by Alcoa, Norsk Hydro and Alro tightened European supply, pushing aluminium premiums higher (Rotterdam P1020A up) and keeping forward spreads in contango. | Awalé Resources announced follow-up soil and termitaria sampling at its Sceptre IOCG copper-gold target in Odienné, Côte d’Ivoire, defining new copper–gold anomalies and planning geophysics and drilling in 2022. The release highlights elevated Cu/Au footprints and compares the geology to major IOCG provinces, but provides no production or supply changes.

Saudi Arabia plans two more copper mine license auctions after the Al-Khunayqiyah tender, aiming to broaden exploration and meet domestic needs. It also promotes bauxite-to-aluminum value-chain development for EV-related aluminum sheets. However, no production volumes or near-term output changes are specified. | SMM’s midday review shows downside pressure across most non-ferrous markets. Shanghai aluminium fell about 2.18% at midday, while Shanghai copper dropped around 0.78%. Shanghai nickel ultimately recovered and closed up about 0.97%. The US dollar eased slightly, but demand/spot activity remained soft, weighing on prices.

SMM notes that aluminium prices in China have fallen further, with Guangdong Nanzhu and Yangtze River spot ingot prices down and discounts easing. It attributes near-term pressure to risk-off macro factors and concerns about slowing demand into Spring Festival, while inventory conditions keep some downside limited. | A Middle East aluminium dross processing plant bought magnetic and electrostatic separation equipment (drum magnet, eddy current separator, electrostatic separator) to recover residual aluminium. Controlled tests showed about 15% recovery from dross. The production system targets ~3 tonnes/hour throughput, increasing secondary supply marginally. | Cinda’s nonferrous weekly links improving China and trade data with supportive demand for industrial metals. It highlights bullish factors for aluminium—declining ingot inventories, ongoing French energy-driven production cuts, and rising industry profits. For copper, it notes inflation and financial indicators lifting prices, with low inventories and tight short-term supply/demand keeping prices firm. | China’s 2021 aluminium production rose 4.8% to a record 38.5 million tonnes, even with energy-consumption curbs at smelters. December output was slightly up month-on-month but down year-on-year. China also produced more of a non-ferrous basket including copper and nickel, but overall the aluminium supply outlook is bearish. | The piece describes a split: copper fell sharply as USD disturbances pressured prices, though Europe’s energy problems/outbreaks and low inventories keep fundamentals supported. Aluminum closed slightly higher on supply concerns from potential European smelter suspensions, but China UHV line proposals failed to boost prices meaningfully, suggesting limited directional follow-through. | The article reports copper fell sharply, attributing the move to USD fluctuations, though it notes low global inventories and ongoing supply disruption from European outbreaks and energy problems. Aluminium closed slightly higher on concerns about possible European smelter pauses, while China UHV-line plans reportedly failed to meaningfully boost prices. | China’s raw aluminum output rose 4.8% in 2021 to a record 38.5 million tons, despite energy-consumption limits. December primary aluminum production was near November but still high versus the prior year. The article also notes overall non-ferrous output, including copper and nickel, strengthening near-term supply expectations. | China’s official data shows 2021 primary aluminium output rose 4.8% to a record 38.5 million tonnes, even with factory power consumption curbs. December output edged down versus November, but remains high. The report also tracks other non-ferrous output (including copper and nickel) lower year-on-year, suggesting pressure from ample supply. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium may retreat as pre–Chinese New Year risk aversion prompts long exits. While USD weakness supported LME, expected slightly higher January supply in key regions and ending downstream stocking could weaken demand further. Technical indicators warn of short-term correction, with expected weekly trading ranges.

Zijin Mining reported a major 2021 profit jump and said it expects 2022 copper output to rise sharply, supported by higher copper prices and contributions from several copper mines. The update reinforces near-term copper production and price optimism, though gold underperformance in 2021 is noted as a partial offset. | Nickel prices are supported by falling exchange and warehouse inventories, with LME stockpiles dropping for a record stretch and China’s official stocks near a low. The market runs a sizable deficit, while EV and stainless demand growth expectations keep buyers engaged. Aluminum inventories are also described as in deficit.

Europe’s energy shortage worsened after Russia cut natural-gas flows to zero via the Yamal-Europe pipeline, raising electricity costs for Western European aluminium smelters. Production has been reduced and global primary supply faces further contraction risk. Meanwhile, China’s off-season social aluminium inventory keeps falling, supporting prices despite softer near-term demand. | Antofagasta reported full-year group copper production of 721,500 tonnes, within guidance but 1.7% below last year. Q4 copper was down 1.2% due to reduced throughput at Los Pelambres from Chile drought. The company cites higher costs and guidance for 2022, with copper expected to rise quarter on quarter. | A commentary on fluorspar demand highlights uses in steelmaking and aluminum refining, but the most price-relevant section concerns Alcoa’s San Ciprián aluminum smelter. Workers and the company agreed to curtail the smelter’s 228,000 tpa capacity for two years from early January 2022 due to high energy costs. | The article reports nickel breaking above $23,000/t on the LME, signaling near-term bullish momentum. It also provides context on aluminium market tightness, stating output curtailments in parts of the Gulf and severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, though it characterizes aluminium as “hard to move.” | The U.S. and UK will begin talks aimed at resolving their steel and aluminum tariff dispute, including U.S. tariffs on British metals and UK retaliatory tariffs. The announcement is likely to influence near-term aluminum price expectations by shifting the market toward potential tariff relief and higher import supply risk.

Alcoa warned that the Russia-Ukraine conflict could disrupt aluminum supply. Any interruption to regional production, shipping, or sourcing raises short-term expectations for tighter aluminum availability, which usually pressures prices upward over the following days as traders price in supply risk. | Nickel prices surged to the highest since 2011 as a supply shortage cut LME and Shanghai warehouse stocks, pushing tom/next and cash premiums to multi-year highs. The piece also links higher power prices to reduced smelter output, tightening aluminium, while copper rises as well. | SMM says base metals rose overnight, with copper up ~1.5%, aluminium up ~1.1%, and nickel surging over 5% on tighter overseas inventories and supportive demand/infrastructure expectations. Copper gains were linked to stronger oil prices/IEA demand outlook, a weaker US dollar, and potential changes in premiums as market conditions evolve. | The US and UK have started formal talks regarding Trump’s proposed steel and aluminum tariffs. Such tariff negotiations can quickly change expectations for aluminum import costs and availability, which often supports prices in the near term. Steel is mentioned but aluminium is the directly implicated metal in the title. | The U.S. and UK agreed to begin talks to resolve their tariff dispute on steel and aluminum, covering global excess capacity and the U.S. tariffs on imports from Britain. The UK will maintain retaliatory measures until a deal is reached. U.S. producers warn negotiations could increase import volumes and pressure prices. | The U.S. and UK agreed to launch talks to resolve their trade dispute over U.S. steel and aluminum tariffs. Officials cited risks from global excess capacity, largely driven by China, and discussed potential trade measures to preserve domestic steel and aluminium industries amid excess supply pressures. | SMM reports aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas jumped 36,500 mt to 176,900 mt (+25.95%) week-on-week, extending a four-week rise. Foshan and Wuxi drove most gains. Downstream orders weakened, leaving sluggish billet demand and expectations of further inventory increases next week.

Nickel surged to a 2011 peak on very low exchange inventories, with Russia-related sanctions threats adding supply concerns. Markets also anticipate stronger EV battery demand. LME three-month nickel rose sharply, and traders linked the broader rally to deficits ahead; aluminium was also cited as exposed to supply risk. | China’s primary aluminium imports fell to 82,265 mt in December, down 64.13% MoM and 36.98% YoY, according to customs data. Imports from India also dropped sharply. Reduced primary aluminium inflows to China may tighten available material and provide near-term support for aluminium prices. | Nickel prices surged, helped by LME inventory drawdowns to multi-year lows and expectations of higher EV battery demand. The LME three-month contract hit the highest since 2011, while traders also flagged supply concerns, including a larger projected nickel deficit. Sanctions fears over Russia are also expected to widen aluminium supply gaps. | Jiangsu Haiguang Metal commissioned workshop No. 6 to process aluminum slag/aluminum ash into calcium aluminate, aiming to advance recycling and safe utilization of hazardous waste. The company states large annual processing capacities and output of various aluminum-related materials. The development is supportive for aluminium recycling but not clearly linked to primary metal pricing. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed amid US inflation pressure. SHFE copper rose 0.64% as copper inventories fell on the week; aluminium fell 0.28% due to resumed capacity in Yunnan/Qinghai and weaker pre-CNY demand; nickel surged 4.21% on low inventories and backwardation.

A Nature Sustainability study suggests scaling photovoltaics to net-zero targets by 2050 could require up to 486 Mt of aluminium, potentially amounting to nearly half of current world production. It highlights energy-intensive aluminium smelting emissions and notes mitigation via recycled aluminium and renewable-powered smelters.

SMM reports easing aluminium spot prices in China (Guangdong Nanzhu and Yangtze River ingots down) alongside a small increase in Chinese electrolytic aluminium inventories. Despite some profit-taking and the pullback, Spring Festival-era domestic stocks remain low and supply expectations are tight, keeping an overall upward aluminium bias. | SMM says China’s alumina refinery full cost fell 13.5% QoQ in Q4 2021 as coal and caustic soda prices dropped, keeping margins profitable. In mid-January 2022 alumina prices rose and smelters/traders increased buying amid expectations of northern production cuts, likely supporting aluminium-linked sentiment. | SMM reports base metals fell overnight as investors took profits amid rising risk aversion, with a stronger U.S. dollar weighing on non-ferrous prices. SHFE copper fell 1.74% and nickel plunged 6.88%, pressured by high matte nickel output sentiment. Aluminium was near-flat, with weak/seasonal demand and rising inventories. | Reuters reports global aluminium production slowed in late 2021 as power constraints spread from China to Europe. Smelter curtailments and closures tied to energy efficiency rules and high electricity prices are widening a supply gap, lifting LME prices above $3,000/tonne and driving sharp increases in physical premiums. | Shanghai nickel fell sharply as traders took profits after a blistering rally to record highs; March nickel hit an 8% daily limit lower on profit-taking ahead of the U.S. Fed meeting and China holidays. The move was reinforced by Shanghai exchange fee/margin changes, with nickel also falling at the LME. | India’s aluminium sector expects the upcoming budget to raise customs duty on aluminium scrap from 2.5% to 10% (and possibly higher duties on aluminium). The article cites heavy scrap import dependence and increased scrap inflows due to China’s restrictions, arguing tariffs would protect domestic producers. | The article discusses global bauxite market trends, noting that aluminium production drives bauxite demand. It reports bauxite output rising 1.2% in 2020 and expects further growth to 406.7 Mt by 2025, implying improving raw-material availability and demand conditions that support aluminium prospects. | Nickel led a broad base-metals decline as investors turned cautious ahead of a key Fed meeting and amid rising Ukraine tensions. Nickel futures and spot fell after prior rallies, driven partly by exchange fee/margin adjustments and profit-taking. Copper and aluminium also declined in Shanghai, reflecting risk-off sentiment despite low nickel inventories.

LME three-month aluminum jumped 2.1% as markets focused on potential supply disruptions from Russia/major producer Rusal amid Ukraine-related escalation and possible sanctions. The piece links the move to past sanctions impacts on Rusal and also reports broader base-metal weakness, while nickel remained highly volatile. | Aluminium prices rose overnight on SHFE and LME, with the market increasingly concerned about potential reductions in overseas aluminium supply due to intensifying Ukraine-Russia tensions and evolving US sanctions. LME-listed warehouse stocks fell sharply, while spot activity softened ahead of the CNY holiday. Overall outlook points to high-level fluctuations. | SME Times reports that India’s aluminium industry is pushing for higher customs duty on aluminium scrap, from 2.5% to 10%, to counter rising scrap imports driven by China’s restrictions. The tariff change is expected to reduce scrap inflows, support domestic producers’ margins, and lift aluminium price expectations. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose overall, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly after two losses (+1.30%) and Shanghai aluminium ending a losing streak (+1.87%). Shanghai nickel remained slightly down (-0.57%), though the decline narrowed. LME copper futures also rose in the afternoon. | SMM’s daily review says metals rose generally, with Shanghai copper rebounding strongly (+1.30%) after two losses and LME copper also firmer. Shanghai aluminium ended a losing streak (+1.87%) on supply and Europe-energy worries, while Shanghai nickel fell slightly (−0.57%) but the drop narrowed.

LME three-month copper rose about 1.2% as investors expected the Fed to take a moderate approach to March rate hikes, supporting economic recovery and near-term metal demand. SHFE copper also gained. LME aluminum was slightly higher, lifted by Russia-Ukraine supply-risk concerns. | SMM says aluminium billet inventories in five major consumption areas rose 34,700 mt to 211,600 mt on Jan 27, the fifth straight weekly increase. Foshan and Wuxi drove most of the gains. Demand was sluggish ahead of Chinese New Year, and inventories are expected to rise further during the holiday. | India’s Aluminium Association of India asks the finance minister to cut basic customs duties, fix inverted duty structures on aluminium value-chain raw materials, align scrap and primary duty rates, and eliminate coal cess for power-intensive aluminium producers. The stated goal is lowering production costs and improving competitiveness versus cheaper imports, which could support aluminium prices. | Kootenay Silver reports that its Aztec joint venture completed the first 14 RC drill holes of a planned 5,000 m Phase 2 program at the Cervantes gold-copper property in Sonora, Mexico. Drilling targets oxide gold and deeper copper-gold porphyry sulfides; assay results are pending.

SMM estimates China’s January aluminium output at 3.19 million mt (-2.6% YoY). However, 530,000 mt of capacity was resumed and more is expected to ramp in February, while downstream consumption drops sharply after CNY due to delayed re-openings from environmental rules. Inventories may accumulate to 1.1 million mt by end-Feb. | The commentary argues that U.S.-U.K. talks will likely result in managed-trade tariff-rate quotas that limit steel (and in the broader system, aluminium) import volumes to pre-2018 levels. By constraining imports and keeping volumes fixed, the author expects continued price pressure rather than relief for downstream users. | Report notes no typical pre–Spring Festival buildup in aluminium ingot stocks; inventories have fallen since January. It highlights supply vulnerability from hydropower seasonality and power curtailments in Yunnan, and potential sanctions/energy-cost impacts from Russia-Ukraine conflict. With low inventories and strong spot demand, aluminium prices face bullish pressure. | A Reuters poll expects copper to soften in 2022 as rising interest rates weaken demand and more mine output—mainly from South America—adds supply. Aluminium remains supported by past power curtailments, but tighter conditions are seen as already priced in. Nickel is forecast lower as inventories rise and Indonesia supply increases.

The report projects that 74% of global aluminum market growth will come from APAC, led by China, Japan and India, and expects overall aluminum demand to expand at about 3% CAGR through 2024. It also points to rising aluminum demand in electric vehicles as a growth driver. | Europe’s natural-gas and power costs have surged, forcing aluminum smelters to cut output or close (including Alcoa in Spain and Norsk Hydro in Slovakia). War-related fears around Russian supply and shrinking LME stocks to the lowest level since 2007 point to short-term aluminum tightness and higher prices. | Callinex reported additional drilling results expanding the Rainbow VMS copper-gold-zinc deposit at its Pine Bay Project, including deeper intersections and a newly defined Red Zone with high copper-equivalent grades. Assays support potential continuation at depth, but there is no near-term production or supply change, limiting immediate price impact. | Sohar Port invites investors to develop downstream metals processing, highlighting copper concentration from Oman’s mined ore (with copper ore exports curtailed to support in-country value) and aluminium downstream uses from Sohar Aluminium’s smelter feedstock. The piece is promotional and doesn’t specify near-term output increases affecting prices. | ALRO S.A. said management is working on solutions to bring electrolytic aluminium production back to the 2021 level starting in 2023, contingent on stabilization of energy prices. The announcement links production volumes to power-market conditions, influencing market expectations for aluminium supply.

Norilsk Nickel’s Global Palladium Fund launched a physically-backed EV metals ETC. The tracked index weights include Copper (40%) and Nickel (18.65%) and stores metal in LME/LPPM warehouses. The news is primarily an investment product launch, without clear immediate supply-demand impact, and Aluminium is not referenced. | A deal is being discussed to restart Alcoa’s idled Intalco aluminum smelter in Washington, using $10 million in state funds to improve efficiency and cut pollution, plus a still-uncertain low-cost electricity contract. Power negotiations with Bonneville Power Administration are ongoing, making timing and market impact uncertain. | Washington state and stakeholders are seeking to restart Alcoa’s idled Intalco Works aluminium smelter near Ferndale using about $10 million in upgrades to cut pollution and improve efficiency. A buyer sale is being negotiated alongside early-stage talks for affordable wholesale power, crucial for the power-hungry restart.

Aluminium producers in India say coal rakes for captive power plants remain critically low, with stocks around 3–4 days versus a 15-day target. They warn production disruptions are looming because smelting requires continuous power. The group asks for 25–30 coal rakes per day and cites cancellations of coal auctions for non-regulated sectors. | Copper prices rose 1.5% on a softer U.S. dollar and buying driven by concerns over low LME inventories, though gains were tempered by slowing China activity around the new year holiday. China’s smelter shutdowns for pollution controls supported supply tightness, aiding the broader base-metals tone. Aluminium shortages also remain stressed. | A growing “candemic” is causing aluminum beverage container shortages. Small craft breweries are struggling to keep selling because can supply is tight, leading them to stock up on unbranded cans or shift to bottled beer. The aluminum can bottleneck can tighten short-term demand/supply dynamics for aluminium packaging.

The piece weighs a potential Western Australia supply shock (prompted by an omicron spread) and its possible impact on WA-linked commodities, including nickel and alumina. It also notes Oxford Economics expects improved metal supply and new copper projects coming online, while nickel production rises in Indonesia/Philippines, offsetting disruption risk. | Aluminium futures rose as LME warehouses drew down quickly, with stocks hitting a 15-year low. The move is supported by geopolitical uncertainty from Russia-Ukraine and elevated power costs in Europe, both of which can pressure supply availability and increase production economics for aluminium producers.

Brazil’s ministry says alumina exports declined 14% in January.
