It describes longer-term market expansion but provides no concrete near-term supply disruptions, inventory changes, tariffs, or contract price impacts for copper, nickel, or aluminium. | SMM reports aluminium plate/sheet and strip producers ran at 71.97% in August, down MoM/YoY, due to seasonal low demand and power rationing amid an energy shortage. SMM expects September operating rates to recover as rationing eases and overseas energy pressure may boost Chinese exports, though a worsening pandemic could offset gains. | SMM reports aluminium ingot social inventories across eight China markets at 675,000 mt on Sep 15, slightly up week-on-week but down sharply versus Tuesday and year ago. Aluminium billet social inventory fell to 114,900 mt. Reduced arrivals from Sichuan/Yunnan and potential further Yunnan smelter cuts may support higher spot prices. | Europe’s aluminium buyers face power shortages and high electricity costs, but some still plan to purchase Russian Rusal metal despite war-related moral concerns. Others refuse Rusal supply for next year due to reputational risk. With Rusal reportedly offering $100-$150/tonne discounts amid threatened supply, pricing pressure intensifies for aluminium contracts. | Aluminium prices rose as Europe’s power crisis forced about half of EU aluminium capacity offline and analysts warned shortages could worsen in winter. Copper fell amid recession demand fears and a sharp jump in LME on-warrant stocks; nickel dropped even more with broader risk-off sentiment and tighter economic prospects. | The Aluminum Association’s Aluminum Transportation Group hosted an automotive-electrification panel linking automakers with aluminum producers. Speakers discussed joint work on manufacturing processes, advanced alloys, and joining technologies, along with aluminum-clad vehicles displayed at NAIAS and related industry outreach aimed at accelerating aluminium penetration in transportation.

Constellium’s executive said Rusal accounts for under 5% of its aluminium purchases and that, with no sanctions currently in place, the company sees no reason to avoid buying. The article contrasts other buyers turning away from Russian metal and mentions discounts tied to potential export-sanction risk. | Shanghai aluminium rose up to 2% on fears of wider and longer China production cuts as power restrictions in Yunnan could expand and remain in place amid low rainfall and hydropower supply. Gains were capped by record August output and global macro worries, which also weighed on copper and nickel. | CME Group said aluminum futures trading has hit record volume, with average daily volume up sharply and growing open interest and accounts. Participants, including Bonnell Aluminum, are using the contracts for hedging and referencing prices in downstream contracts. The release highlights market liquidity rather than physical aluminum fundamentals. | SMM says aluminium prices have recently rebounded, but alumina remains vulnerable: regional alumina prices are weak and demand risk is rising as hydropower shortages in Yunnan cause smelters to reduce output. Although Sichuan curbs eased and some capacity resumes, Yunnan’s production risk keeps downside pressure on alumina. | Alcoa’s Harvey says short-term aluminium markets face volatility from high European electricity prices, with some smelters offline and Alcoa cutting output at its Lista facility. Despite tighter energy conditions, demand remains weak and recession fears weigh, reducing European aluminium premiums (P1020A) versus earlier peaks. | Copper is set for a weekly decline as a weaker yuan and stronger dollar make dollar-priced metals costlier for Chinese buyers. Investors also fear slowing growth amid a deepening China property slump and broader recession risks. Aluminium on the LME is also down, consistent with risk-off macro pressure.

SMM reports domestic alumina prices fell across most provinces on weaker demand and thin trading, despite firmer overseas prices. It highlights widening domestic/overseas differentials supporting exports, but warns Yunnan power rationing may reduce aluminium output by 20–30%, putting alumina demand at risk and possibly pressuring prices further. | SMM reports volatility in aluminium prices: SHFE fell on the week while LME rose. Macro factors like hotter US CPI, higher Fed hike probabilities, and weaker RMB are seen pressuring aluminium. Offsetting supports include Yunnan smelter production cuts (10% possibly to 20%), slow Sichuan ramp-up, and domestic demand stimulus. | SMM reports China’s aluminium ingot social inventories at 685,000 mt, still around a low level despite minor week-to-week uptick. Warehouse withdrawals fell sharply to 113,800 mt amid holiday disruptions and weaker billet demand after imported inflows. With National Day stocking and Yunnan production reductions ahead, inventories are unlikely to build in September. | Copper prices fell as markets turned more pessimistic about global growth, raising concerns of weaker metals demand; the weak U.S. dollar limited declines. Fitch Solutions cut its copper forecast. Aluminium also weakened on lower Chinese import volumes tied to strong domestic production, while nickel moved higher amid mixed signals.

SMM reports aluminium prices are supported by expected power-related production cuts in Yunnan and rising electricity costs, while actual restriction levels remain uncertain. Sichuan smelters are resuming, slightly easing supply. Demand remains sluggish and inventories are low, leaving aluminium pressured by weak transactions but structurally supported. | East China aluminium scrap throughput in Linyi rose 43% WoW to 14,200 mt, while spot A00 stayed volatile and fell to 18,770 yuan/mt on Sept 19. Traders shipped more amid Fed rate-hike worries, social ingot inventories eased slightly, and the scrap market is expected to remain oversupplied, bearish for aluminium prices. | China’s customs data show primary aluminium net imports of 43,200 mt in August 2022, down 0.43% MoM and sharply lower versus a year earlier (-58.9% YoY). Total imports fell to 49,300 mt (down 53.4% YoY) and exports rose to 6,100 mt (+1,365% YoY). | Fastmarkets’ International Aluminium Conference in Barcelona highlighted bearish sentiment and demand worries for the rest of the year. European aluminium P1020 premiums fell to $400–440/t, with additional downside expected. High energy costs have led to smelter cuts (including Hydro and Alcoa) and may squeeze margins and downstream demand. | Bloomberg reports that Rusal is considering selling aluminium directly on the LME by shipping some metal from Vladivostok to LME warehouses in Asia. Rusal is cautious about large inventory inflows that could depress prices, implying near-term bearish pressure on aluminium if deliveries proceed.

SMM reports IAI data showing ex-China aluminium production increased to 2.22 million tonnes in August. Daily average output outside China rebounded from a 20-month low to 71,480 tonnes. Higher ex-China supply points to potential short-term pressure on aluminium prices as availability rises. | Fastmarkets conference takeaways were that high European energy costs are driving some aluminium smelter curtailments and weighing on end-user demand, keeping the supply-demand balance fragile. European aluminium premiums have fallen to new 2022 lows, while spot liquidity remains weak, though low warehouse stocks cap downside.

China imported 10.75 million tonnes of bauxite in August, with Guinea the largest source. Indonesia export-ban uncertainty and some mines receiving extra quotas sustain demand, while supply is not expanding. Bauxite prices have surged (Guinea +28% YTD, Indonesia +21%), implying higher alumina feedstock costs. | SMM said China’s aluminium ingot social inventory fell to 666,000 mt, with declines concentrated in Wuxi, Nanhai and Hangzhou. Arrivals were limited by pandemic-related rail restrictions, and Nanhai dropped on Yunnan production cuts. Billet inventory also declined, supported by pre-holiday restocking, likely keeping supply tight next week. | China’s aluminium extrusion exports fell for the third straight month in August to 81,800 mt, down 12.4% MoM and 3.4% YoY. Higher domestic prices, exchange-rate-driven transaction costs, and weak overseas demand hurt orders. Separately, EU smelters cut output due to high energy costs, but the overall export momentum remained weak. | The article headline suggests aluminium supply problems are intensifying, implying tighter near-term availability that can push aluminium prices higher. It also references China ordering output related to copper, pointing to potential demand support for copper. However, the excerpt contains no further specifics on volumes or timing. | Rising European gas and electricity prices tied to the energy crisis are forcing aluminum smelters to cut output or shut down (including Slovalco, Aldel and prior line closures). China’s aluminum imports have fallen sharply while domestic output hits records, and demand weakens from COVID and heatwaves, weighing on LME aluminum prices.

After the US Fed’s 75 bp rate hike on Sept 21 (in line with expectations), aluminium prices rebounded sharply on Sept 22, with downstream buyers returning and stocking ahead of the National Day holiday. Traders cite support from possible production cuts, but note accepted Yunnan supply reductions and import pressure may weigh on prices.

SMM shows alumina prices eased week-on-week across Chinese regions, while overseas prices stayed high. Despite refinery cutbacks in Shanxi/Chongqing, aluminium smelters reduced output and southwest demand declined, with Guangxi facing excess supply. Market sentiment is bearish and alumina is expected to keep trending down short term. | London copper slid to a two-month low, falling about 1.1% to $7,352/t, as the U.S. dollar hit a two-decade peak and the Fed outlook turned more hawkish. Weak growth expectations pressured industrial metal demand. LME aluminium also hit an 18-month low, while ShFE nickel dropped 3.1%. | SMM reports SHFE 2210 aluminium eased to 18,630 yuan/mt (down 0.56% weekly) and LME aluminium to about $2,208/mt (down 4.5%). Output cuts in Yunnan are expanding (up to 18%) and stocks decline, but global rate-hike fears remain bearish, limiting rebounds. | SMM reports aluminium smelter costs fell last week as alumina prices dropped on oversupply. Real-time smelter cost eased to 17,888.8 yuan/mt and profits narrowed as A00 aluminium prices dropped. Electricity costs may keep total costs high due to rising coal prices, so smelter profits are expected to fluctuate within a tight range. | SMM says Foshan aluminium A00 prices were stable in September, but aluminium billet conversion margins came under pressure. Downstream extruder operating rates did not improve, small producers suffered losses and cut output, and market transactions were weak. Billet inventory fell and arrivals decreased, yet outlook depends on limited downstream recovery. | SMM reports China aluminium ingot social inventories in eight major markets fell to 652,000 mt on Sep 26, hitting a year-to-date low. Inventories declined in key regions such as Wuxi, Nanhai and Gongyi as warehouse shipments rose and arrivals weakened. Downstream stockpiling for National Day may prolong the drawdown this week. | The piece says aluminum supply is shrinking due to smelter shutdowns tied to energy shortages, while demand remains weak, especially in China’s construction. It notes aluminum prices have broken below short-term support after a brief rebound from the Fed’s rate hike. Lead times for cans are long, but prices are still trending down.

SMM says aluminium scrap throughput in Linyi (Sep 19–25) rose 20% WoW as retailers shipped in panic to mitigate falling prices. A00 aluminium stayed volatile and declined WoW; downstream scrap users bought less. Inventory fell, but oversupply of scrap is expected to persist this week, weighing on aluminium prices. Copper scrap throughput also increased. | SMM says a signing ceremony took place in Jiayuguan, Gansu for a 6 million mt/year alumina and materials project. The planned upstream alumina refining is intended to extend and integrate the local aluminium chain, covering alumina refining, aluminium smelting, semis and deep processing, potentially improving supply expectations for aluminium. | SMM’s summit takeaway argues China’s dual-carbon policies will accelerate low-carbon secondary aluminium growth faster than primary in the long run. It highlights expanding low-carbon supply in Yunnan into 2025, switching to green power, and carbon-related cost pressures (carbon trading and EU CBAM) that influence aluminium pricing. | Copper prices rose Tuesday as a softer U.S. dollar made the dollar-priced metal cheaper, but growth and demand concerns from aggressive global rate hikes limited upside. LME copper added 1.2%. The piece also notes small declines in LME aluminium and ShFE nickel amid generally mixed moves across metals inventories. | Element 29 reported an initial inferred mineral resource for its Elida porphyry copper-molybdenum deposit in Peru, including 321.7 million tonnes grading 0.32% copper. The update informs future drilling and potential project economics but does not indicate near-term production changes, limiting immediate price impact mainly to copper. | Norsk Hydro said it is responding to reduced aluminium demand by partially curtailing production. The move can tighten near-term aluminium supply, offering some support to prices. However, the decision is driven by softer demand, which may cap gains and keep price support moderate.

Analysts cite macro slowdown and power/energy issues to explain large aluminium declines and potential transitory spikes from Russian delivery and potential smelter curbs. Citi notes a recent nickel rally but expects Europe-led demand weakness to dominate. Copper is seen flat but forecast down on recession risk in Europe. | The piece reviews broker outlooks: it attributes aluminium’s sharp decline to recession and energy-cost-driven production cuts, while noting potential short-lived spikes. For nickel it remains bearish despite a September rally, citing demand slowdown. Copper is expected to stay weak under Europe recession fears, with no bullish near-term catalyst. | JP Morgan and others cut iron ore forecasts, while Longview and Citi discuss aluminium, nickel and copper. Aluminium is pressured by recession and power-cost-driven smelter cuts, with potential Russian delivery and Yunnan hydropower outages. Nickel is bearish despite a Sept rally, while copper remains range-bound with recession-driven downside risks. | SMM reports discussion at an automotive materials summit that rising policy pressure toward carbon reduction and fuel efficiency is accelerating substitution of steel wheels with lightweight cast aluminium wheels. The piece argues this boosts aluminium billet and casting demand as commercial and exported vehicles adopt aluminium wheels for energy savings and recyclability. | Norsk Hydro says weaker European demand for aluminium billets and uncertainty in the energy market have led to stock buildup. It will partially curtail output at Hydro Karmøy and Hydro Husnes in Norway, following the Slovalco plant closure in Slovakia. The curtailment is expected to start shortly and reduce power consumption. | Aluminium Bahrain (ALBA) held its Q3 2022 board meeting, reviewing safety performance, productivity and financial results, sales amid global logistics challenges, and developments on a proposed Line 7 project. It also discussed Power Station 5 construction linked to decarbonization and noted financing closure with Sinosure for the project.

SMM reports China’s aluminium ingot social inventories fell to 619,000 mt as of Sept 29, a weekly drop, hitting a four-year low as downstream buyers stockpiled ahead of the National Day holiday. Aluminium billet inventories also declined to 80,100 mt, helped by weak shipments due to Xinjiang disruptions and holiday demand. | Aluminum surged 8% on the London Metal Exchange after a Bloomberg report said the LME may move toward banning Russian metal supplies to its warehouses. The market reacted quickly to tighter supply prospects, while nickel also rose and Alcoa shares jumped 7% in premarket trading. | Aluminum jumped to an all-time high as the LME weighed a potential ban related to Russian aluminum. Traders appear to be pricing in tighter near-term availability and higher costs if Russian supply is disrupted. The move points to immediate bullish pressure for aluminium prices. | Alcoa and Century Aluminum shares rise after reports the London Metal Exchange could ban Russian aluminum. The market reaction suggests traders expect tighter available supply for the LME market if Russian metal is restricted, supporting a bullish near-term outlook for aluminum prices. | Aluminum surged 8.5% on the LME after Bloomberg reported the exchange may discuss blocking new Russian metal deliveries to its warehouses. Russia’s role as a major supplier raises supply-risk concerns. Nickel also jumped about 5%, while copper was cited as potentially affected if Russian flows tighten. | Kentucky says it has recouped its $15 million investment tied to a proposed Braidy/Unity Aluminum aluminum mill that never reached completion and required financing delays. The site is being donated and sold for future development, but there’s no clear near-term change to broader aluminum supply markets. | Kentucky says it has recovered its $15 million stake after Braidy Industries/Unity Aluminum failed to build a promised aluminum rolling mill near Ashland. The state also reacquired the land. Unity later merged with Steel Dynamics (Aluminum Dynamics), indicating continued plans for an aluminum plant elsewhere.

Kentucky recovered $15 million invested in Braidy Industries for an aluminum rolling mill planned near Ashland, after the project failed to materialize. Gov. Andy Beshear said the land will be donated for future economic development. Braidy was later rebranded as Unity Aluminum and merged with Steel Dynamics. | SMM survey indicates many Chinese aluminium downstream processors—especially extruders and secondary alloy plants—will take longer breaks due to weak orders and bearish post-holiday sentiment. While pre-holiday restocking was limited, aluminium ingot and billet inventories are declining pre-holiday but are expected to accumulate sharply after the National Day holiday as output and inventory cycles normalize. | Aluminium prices extend gains after a record spike, with the move linked to fears over Russia supply. The headline suggests market participants are pricing in tighter near-term aluminium availability, supporting higher aluminium quotations in the immediate period following the shock. | Kentucky’s governor announced efforts to revitalize an aluminum mill project. The move is likely to improve the aluminum supply outlook by supporting or restarting smelting/processing capacity, which can strengthen expectations for near-term availability and potentially lift aluminum prices in the coming days to weeks. | Rusal denied plans to offload aluminium into LME warehouses amid LME consideration of a consultation over whether Russian aluminium, nickel and copper should remain tradable and storable. The uncertainty sparked a buying frenzy, with benchmark aluminium jumping 8.5% to $2,305/t, as traders feared delivery constraints. | Aluminium prices declined after a record spike attributed to fears over Russian supply. The move suggests the market’s immediate concern about availability from Russia has eased or been reassessed, reducing support for aluminium and applying downward pressure to spot and near-term expectations.

Mitsubishi Power signed a long-term service agreement with Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) for a new 680.9 MW combined-cycle gas turbine power block. The deal covers parts, repairs and services to improve reliability and efficiency, supporting Alba’s aluminium production and uninterrupted operations as commercial use begins in Q4 2024. | The report says aluminium prices are falling on concerns about Russian supply. The implication is that market participants expect supply-related uncertainty tied to Russia to worsen aluminium availability or refine short-term supply expectations, creating immediate downward price pressure in the metal. | Vedanta said its aluminium production rose 2% to 584,000 tonnes in the second quarter of FY23. The filing also noted higher mined output at Zinc India and increased power sales. The aluminium uptick points to slightly improved near-term supply visibility for the metal.

London copper rose 0.8% and aluminium gained 1.1% as a softer U.S. dollar followed weak U.S. manufacturing data and lower Treasury yields. Trading stayed thin due to holidays in China and reduced Asian participation, with analysts expecting low demand and sideways price action. | Alcoa urged the LME to avoid accepting Russian metal into the exchange, warning of a potential “crisis.” The argument implies reduced inflows of Russian supply to LME warehouses and tighter availability expectations, which would likely support aluminium prices over the coming days.

Rusal said there is “no basis” for any LME ban on Russian aluminum. The headline implies less likelihood of an immediate exchange-related disruption to Russian metal supply flows, which can ease bearish expectations and provide support for aluminium prices over the next days to weeks. | Nickel is poised for another rebound as the LME considers options to manage Russian metal trading, including possible bans on new Russian nickel deliveries. With Russia supplying about 20% of global nickel, a change could mirror March-style Western supply fears, while aluminium could be affected too via similar LME action.

ARENA will fund a Spinifex offshore wind farm resource assessment aimed at integrating variable renewable power with the Portland, Victoria aluminium smelter. The smelter can produce 358,000 tonnes per year and is a major power user. The project is positioned to support low-emissions aluminium production via steady electricity supply.

Copper prices fell on Friday, extending losses, as a firmer U.S. dollar and hawkish Fed commentary ahead of jobs data increased recession and demand-damping fears. Reuters links the move to tighter monetary expectations. Aluminium was up modestly, indicating less consistent pressure across industrial metals. | Almost all aluminium premiums fell as discounting widened and demand weakened across regions. US, Rotterdam and other European premiums hit multi-month lows amid “freefall” discounting and liquidity competition. Brazil’s cif premium dropped to a 2022 low amid weak spot interest and uncertainty over import quotas. Japan and South Korea premiums also declined.

Japanese buyers agreed to price Q4 2022 aluminum in Japan at a $99/mt premium over the LME cash price, down 33% from Q3. The continuing decline reflects weak demand, high domestic inventories, slower auto recovery tied to semiconductors, and oversupply concerns amid recession worries. | SMM reports LME is discussing conditions for a potential ban on new deliveries of Russian metals, which could rebalance global aluminium supply and ease price suppression if Russian output can’t reach LME. It also points to continued regional smelter curbs from high energy costs and further cuts, expecting aluminium prices to rise. | Gravita India’s Senegal subsidiary has started commercial production at a new aluminium recycling plant with ~4,000 MTPA capacity. The firm expects additional annual revenue of about Rs. 60 crores and ~20% gross margins. The new recycling output increases availability of aluminium from secondary sources in West Africa. | An aluminium industry CEO says rising electricity and gas prices—exacerbated by Europe’s gas shortages and the merit-order power pricing system—are the toughest test in the sector’s history. He notes some firms are already cutting production and urges rapid EU/Germany energy price caps to prevent closures and carbon leakage. | Copper prices rose, supported by renewed concerns over copper availability in LME warehouses (higher cancelled warrants) and a wider cash premium. However, Chinese demand worries, expectations of further U.S. rate hikes, a stronger dollar, and rising inventories weighed on sentiment. Aluminium and nickel fell on the day amid the same macro backdrop.

SMM reports disturbances in aluminium markets driven by production cuts (Hydro in Norway, European curtailments, and China’s Yunnan/Sichuan reductions) and an LME discussion on potentially banning new Russian metals supply. Despite weaker demand and higher LME/Chinese inventories, supply tightening and rising costs are expected to support aluminium prices near term. | LME copper slipped 0.2% and aluminium fell 0.4% as a stronger U.S. dollar, supported by robust labour data and sticky inflation expectations, increased costs for non-dollar buyers. Nickel rose on China’s SHFE, showing mixed momentum across venues rather than a uniform market move. | Rusal, a major Russian aluminium producer, reshuffled its sales team as sanctions and market access concerns grow. Some consumers previously indicated they would not buy Rusal aluminium for 2023, while the LME discussed a possible ban of Russian aluminium from trading. Rusal says supplies remain stable. | Base metals dropped as recession fears and rising COVID-19 cases in China raised concerns about weaker demand. A stronger dollar also pressured USD-priced commodities. LME aluminium fell, while copper initially slipped but later edged up; Comex copper rose. Nickel declined, reflecting the overall risk-off sentiment.

Russian-origin copper has accumulated in LME warehouses in Germany, the Netherlands and Taiwan since mid-September, with stocks rising sharply. Some consumers reportedly reject Russian copper, turning LME storage into a last-resort outlet. LME also discussed possible bans on Russian aluminium, nickel and copper. | The article reports that the White House is weighing a ban on Russian aluminum in response to Ukraine-war escalation. Such a restriction could limit Russia’s access to export markets and reduce available supply, increasing expectations of tighter global aluminum availability and supporting prices in the near term. | The Biden administration is weighing an outright ban, punitive tariffs, or sanctions targeting Russian aluminum producer Rusal to respond to Moscow’s escalation in Ukraine. If implemented, reduced Russian supply would likely tighten global availability and boost aluminum prices in the coming days and weeks. | The article headline says aluminium prices are surging after the White House weighs a ban on Russian metal. Such a policy move would likely reduce near-term Russian aluminium availability and increase supply risk, supporting firmer spot pricing over the coming days. | Rusal says it will meet with the LME to oppose a possible metal ban. Because Rusal is a leading aluminium producer, an LME restriction could reduce deliverability or participation in the aluminium market, tightening supply expectations and supporting aluminium prices in the coming days. | The Biden administration is considering a total ban or equivalent punitive tariffs/sanctions targeting Russian aluminum after renewed missile strikes on Ukraine. Russia supplies about 10% of U.S. aluminum imports and Rusal is a major producer. Any restriction would likely tighten supply and support higher aluminum prices near term. | Bloomberg reports the US is considering a complete ban on Russian aluminum, alongside potential tariff increases and sanctions on Rusal. Discussions have been ongoing for weeks. Blocking Russian supply would reduce availability in the near term and likely support higher aluminium prices. | Reuters reports the Biden administration is considering restricting Russian aluminum imports, including a possible ban, punitive tariffs, or sanctions on Rusal. The measure is expected to boost global aluminum prices by tightening supply, with no finalized decision yet. Alcoa shares rose and related stocks moved on the news.

The report states the US may block Russian aluminum imports. Such a measure would cut Russian-origin supply entering the US, potentially tightening availability for downstream consumers and helping support aluminum prices in the near term. Copper and nickel are not indicated as directly affected. | SMM reports aluminium production has been constrained as soaring energy prices and strikes led to 1.67 million mt of capacity cuts since August in Europe and North America. The cuts include 1.36 million mt in Europe and 304,000 mt in North America. The market is also concerned about a possible LME ban on Russian metals. | SMM said China’s social inventories of aluminium ingots fell to 655,000 mt by Oct 13, down from Oct 8, while billet inventories dropped to 97,600 mt. Declines were linked to delayed shipments from Xinjiang and pandemic-related transport disruptions, though rail recovery may raise arrivals later in October. | OpenGate Capital is combining Extol and Aluminium France Extrusion into Aluminium Solutions Group SAS, a major European aluminum extruder, and plans to improve its ESG profile with investments to facilitate recycled materials. The deal increases extrusion scale, but offers no disclosed terms or immediate capacity figures affecting global aluminum balances. | Reports say the Biden administration is considering an outright ban, tariff increases, or Rusal-specific sanctions on Russian aluminium imports. U.S. aluminium producers’ stocks jumped on expectations of more domestic business, while officials warn sanctions could raise prices; aluminium reportedly spiked about 7% after the news. | Alcoa urged the White House to halt U.S. imports of Russian aluminum and support sanctions on Russian aluminum, citing Russia’s intensified attacks on Ukraine. The request follows reports that Washington is weighing limits on Rusal’s ability to sell aluminum in the U.S., which could reduce near-term supply.

Alcoa urged the White House to block U.S. imports of Russian aluminum after Russia’s escalation in Ukraine. Biden’s administration reportedly considered options including restricting Rusal. Alcoa also asked the LME to delist Russian aluminum, arguing it distorts price discovery and supply-demand signals. | The piece links an OPEC-driven recession risk to weaker demand across commodities, but focuses on aluminium. It says US sanctions on Russia’s Rusal (via sanctions, tariffs, or a ban) could disrupt supply, citing a prior 2018 sanction-induced 25% aluminium jump and increased upside for prices. Lead and iron ore are discussed but not tracked here. | The US is considering a blanket ban or sanctions on Russian aluminium, including Rusal, potentially blocking imports or restricting warehouse deliveries. Such actions would tighten Western supply amid existing disruptions, with analysts warning of sharp aluminium price increases as Russian volumes reroute to China and other exporters. | European copper buyers face record term premiums for 2023 as they self-sanction against Russian metal following UK actions affecting UMMC-linked brands. Aurubis, Codelco and Montanwerke Brixlegg quote much higher premiums amid low visible stocks and potential benchmark concerns. The piece warns Russian copper could redirect to China, affecting regional pricing.

SMM reports firm electricity costs keeping aluminium smelter costs high. Hydropower costs in Sichuan and Yunnan are expected to rise with the dry season, while prebaked anode prices increased. Smelter full costs edged up and profits expanded as aluminium prices stayed firm, limiting short-term downside. | SMM reports the LME is considering limiting or banning new Russian aluminium deliveries after UK sanctions related to UMMC’s co-founder. Russia supplies a meaningful share of global and LME flows, with heavy exports to Europe. If a delivery ban occurs, overseas circulation could tighten and prompt supplier price/output adjustments. | SMM reports alumina prices fell across Chinese regions and overseas in mid-October, with the weighted average index down 16 yuan/mt. Higher costs did not support prices, and weak profitability plus new capacity reduced bargaining power. Pandemic-related transport disruptions and refinery curtailments could further pressure near-term aluminium-linked costs. | SMM says both SHFE and LME aluminium rose after the holiday as US consideration of a ban on Russian aluminium imports raised supply-shock expectations, while Chinese fundamentals improved. Domestic operating capacity declined, downstream processing rates recovered, orders improved, and social inventories stayed low, supporting further gains in the coming week. | A weekly broker-rating roundup highlights Citi’s view of short-term headwinds for base-metals demand from sluggish China, higher US interest rates and a stronger USD. Citi trims near-term price forecasts for aluminium, copper and nickel (while being comparatively more bearish on copper). Some company ratings are upgraded on valuation, but metal-price expectations lean weaker. | Hindalco says it has launched India’s first all-aluminium freight rail rakes for Indian Railways. The move supports freight transport modernization and is positioned to deliver carbon savings. By increasing aluminium railcar demand through Hindalco’s deliveries, the news could add modest near-term bullish pressure to aluminium prices. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty from China’s continued zero-COVID curbs and global recession fears offset worries over tight supply. LME and Shanghai copper prices gave back gains. Aluminium and nickel also declined as risk sentiment weakened, despite lower Chile output and tight inventories. | Copper rose on tightening global supplies: LME/COMEX/ShFE stocks fell and Chilean production dropped sharply. China demand improved at the margin via premiums, but gains were capped by ongoing zero-COVID uncertainty and global recession fears. Aluminium and nickel fell in the same session, showing mixed risk appetite. | Nelson Mandela Bay’s electricity department is replacing copper cables with cheaper aluminium to deter cable theft and vandalism. Officials report repairing hundreds of street lights daily and arresting cable thieves, while also targeting scrapyards. The substitution is demand-shifting at the city level for copper versus aluminium. | Nelson Mandela Bay’s electricity department is replacing copper cables with cheaper aluminium to reduce cable theft and vandalism. The city reports ongoing streetlight repairs and arrests of cable thieves, and says aluminium cables are installed higher to deter removal. This may modestly shift demand from copper to aluminium locally. | SMM reports Linyi East China scrap market throughput surged in Oct 10-16, especially aluminium scrap, reaching 19,200 mt (+83.6% WoW). This coincided with rising A00 aluminium prices and improved downstream recycler buying after the holiday. Aluminium supply fell on trader risk concerns, while inventories declined; prices were expected stable this week. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty outweighed supply woes. LME copper gave up gains and hovered around $7,535/t while inventories fell and Chile production dropped sharply. China’s reiterated zero-COVID stance and recession fears limited upside, with aluminium and nickel also slipping. | India’s Railways Minister inaugurated the first domestically built aluminium freight rake made with Hindalco collaboration. The lighter, corrosion-resistant design is intended to improve payload and reduce maintenance while lowering emissions. The new rake supports incremental aluminium demand through fabrication and purchases, potentially modestly bullish for aluminium prices. | Benchmark LME aluminium fell about 2.3% as warehouse stocks surged by roughly 65,825 tonnes to 433,025. Traders cited concern that Rusal-linked Russian metal could be delivered into the LME system, distorting prices. Despite ongoing sanctions on some Russian finance, buying Russian metal remains unrestricted.

Aluminum prices jumped over the last week as markets priced the possibility that the Biden administration may fully ban Russian aluminum. The U.S. is considering options including an outright ban, higher tariffs, or renewed sanctions targeting Rusal, which previously caused supply-market disruption when sanctioned. | Biden is reportedly considering an executive order banning imports of Russian aluminium, either via a direct ban, expanded tariffs, or sanctions targeting Rusal. While the US would receive less Russian metal, the article warns Russian supply could be redirected into global markets, potentially increasing dumping and suppressing aluminium prices. | The piece says Iran’s aluminum ingot production rose 23% in the first part of the year, indicating increased domestic output. Higher production can expand near-term aluminum supply and influence short-term market expectations for inventory tightness and pricing, especially if volumes affect regional export or availability.

Copper prices fell further as soaring inflation, recession worries and expectations of more rate hikes pressured global demand sentiment, while a stronger dollar weighed on metal prices. LME aluminium also slipped amid additional inflows into approved warehouses and rising inventories. Nickel rose slightly, but the overall tone remains bearish. | Kaiser Aluminum reported Q3 earnings of $2.5 million (EPS $0.16), versus a loss of -$2.3 million and -$0.14 EPS in the prior-year quarter. Adjusted earnings were $9.6 million or $0.60 per share. Revenue was $749 million, slightly below last year.  | BNN Bloomberg highlights Alcoa posting a surprise loss as “aluminum woes” deepen. The framing implies continued pressure on aluminium economics—likely reflecting weak pricing or demand—making near-term aluminium sentiment more negative for prices over the coming days. | The piece covers trade and logistics updates. It notes that U.S. shipping costs and port backups have eased, but highlights a key risk: the Biden administration may block Russian aluminum imports, which could boost global aluminium prices and potentially reverse an earlier stance on sanctions’ market effects.

Alcoa urged the US and allies to sanction “cheating” Russian metal. Such measures would likely disrupt Russia-linked metal supply or trading, particularly in aluminium markets given Alcoa’s exposure, and therefore could lift near-term aluminium price expectations over the coming days to weeks. | SMM reports China aluminium ingot social inventories at 636,000 mt as of Oct 20, down week-on-week, with transportation disruptions limiting cargo arrivals and Gongyi Railway Station suspending loading/unloading. Aluminium billet inventories also fell to 71,900 mt. Smelter output cuts and unstable logistics tightened supply. | India’s railways put its first rake of aluminium-bodied wagons (61 bottom-discharge cars) into service to improve freight efficiency. The wagons were produced with Hindalco supplying high-strength alloy plates and extrusions. The lighter design and expected CO2 savings support ongoing domestic aluminium demand and modernization of freight transport. | Gravita India’s share price rose after it started an expanded aluminium recycling plant in Senegal. The project cost about Rs3.5 crore, expected to add ~Rs60 crore annual revenue and improve the group’s aluminium sales mix. The plant will process local scrap for die-casting component demand in China, Japan, and Vietnam. | Iran’s state metals firm IMIDRO says Iranian aluminum smelters produced 318,091 mt of aluminum ingot in six months to late September, up 22.5% from the prior year. Salco accounted for over 43% of output. Alumina rose only 2% y/y, while bauxite fell. | Speculation that the U.S. may ban Russian aluminium helped trigger a brief price spike last week. Some buyers have already voluntarily avoided Russian metal, prompting the LME to consider ways to prevent trading distortions from possible stockpiles in its warehouses. Tightened effective supply supports near-term aluminium upside.

The piece says sanctions and alleged dumping of Russian aluminum are pressuring the market. It notes LME warehouse stocks jumped, raising concerns about more Russian-origin supply, while global demand is weakening and operational costs are rising, contributing to aluminium price declines this quarter. | Environmental inspections in Beijing and its surrounding areas and pandemic-related disruptions in Shanxi have reduced raw-material spot purchases for aluminium processors, causing intermittent production suspensions and order-delivery delays. Transport restrictions limit supply inflows, while aluminium ingot and billet social inventories fell, tightening availability and supporting prices. | DTE won a $1.85m contract to install real-time chemical composition analysis on Nordural’s new low-carbon aluminium billet casthouse in Iceland. The system monitors molten aluminium quality to optimize furnace throughput and reduce emissions/energy use. Nordural’s expanded capacity is expected to start in 1Q 2024.

China exported 496,000 mt of unwrought aluminium and aluminium semis in September, down 8.22% month-on-month but slightly up 0.8% year-on-year. Cumulative exports in January–September rose 27.8% versus the prior period, according to customs data, implying stronger outward supply flows. | SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium prices fell on the week, with SHFE at 18,535 yuan/mt and LME near $2,212.5/mt. Supply recovered slightly, but Yunnan power risks persist and COVID disruptions reduced arrivals and social inventories. Consumption was hit by COVID and downstream plant shutdowns, while hawkish Fed views weigh sentiment. | Alcoa is reportedly lobbying the Biden administration to curb Russian aluminium imports from Rusal, with options including a full ban, higher tariffs and sanctions. The piece notes that prior Rusal sanctions lifted LME aluminium and increased China aluminium exports. A new ban could tighten supply and support aluminium prices amid mixed demand outlook. | Rusal filed a London lawsuit alleging Norilsk Nickel CEO Vladimir Potanin breached a decade-old shareholder pact that was meant to protect Nornickel’s dividend formula. The dispute renews tension between Rusal and Potanin-controlled Interros, with the agreement set to expire and merger talks postponed. | China’s primary aluminium imports rose 31.13% month-on-month to 64,685.05 mt in September, though they fell year-on-year. Russia supplied the most, with imports of 45,666.12 mt, up 8.79% month-on-month and 447.23% year-on-year, indicating stronger near-term inbound supply. | China’s September bauxite imports fell to 8.21 million mt, down 23.57% month-on-month. Guinea remained the top supplier, with volumes from Guinea at 4.15 million mt, down 33.7% MoM. The decline suggests reduced upstream raw material inflows for China’s alumina/aluminium sector. | China’s primary aluminium output rose 9.3% y/y in September to 3.42 million tonnes after authorities relaxed power restrictions for smelters. Production was still down 2.5% month-on-month because Yunnan cut power from Sept. 10 due to tight hydropower. Overall supply trends point to bearish near-term price pressure for aluminium.

A resurgence of COVID in Henan is disrupting secondary aluminium (remelted billets/ADC12) producers. Lockdowns and transport restrictions delay shipments, increase freight costs, and limit access to aluminium scrap/raw materials, narrowing margins and reducing production or output. Firms are generally bearish on near-term downstream order recovery. | CME Group and Marex will expand clients’ access to CME’s base metals markets, starting with CME COMEX Aluminum futures, offered on Marex’s Neon Metals platform. CME cites rising aluminum futures volumes and record open interest. The move is aimed at improving market access and risk management rather than changing fundamentals. | Johor Customs in Malaysia stopped a shipping company from exporting about 73,696kg of aluminium scrap in three containers. The shipment, valued at RM819,000, included export duties. The seizure removes a small but immediate amount of aluminium scrap from outgoing trade flows, which can modestly support prices. | Aldel’s aluminium smelter in Delfzijl has been granted creditor protection while seeking new financing after shutting down due to high energy prices and lack of government support. It previously stopped processing imported aluminium, and since then has remained in care-and-maintenance, prompting curators to find a buyer. | SMM reports aluminium scrap throughput in Linyi, Shandong fell 11.8% WoW to about 16,900 mt as pandemic controls disrupted logistics. Secondary producer operating rates rose only slightly and Linyi market was shut temporarily. Spot A00 aluminium prices dipped and SHFE aluminium (2211) weakened, indicating bearish near-term aluminium pressure. | SMM reports falling aluminium spot prices with low south China arrivals, firm spot premiums, and improving inventory only slightly after a recent LME surge. Demand is weak as PMI signals deteriorate and processing rates decline. Yunnan power shortage could reduce output; alumina prices fall, weakening cost support, while high energy costs keep aluminium rangebound.

Traders are reportedly placing large bets in aluminum options as the LME considers imposing a Russia-related ban. The market implication is tighter near-term aluminum availability and increased risk of supply disruption, which typically pushes expectations for higher aluminum prices in the following days.

SMM reports China aluminium extrusion imports rose 12.4% YoY in Jan–Sep 2022, driven by higher inflows from Taiwan, while exports rose slightly 2.57% despite anti-dumping actions. Europe’s energy crisis and a low SHFE/LME ratio supported exports, but later rate hikes and weaker demand pressured shipments. | SMM said China’s aluminium ingot social inventories were 621,000 mt, slightly down WoW, but sluggish transactions and transport disruptions led to local accumulation, with more arrivals expected after Gongyi railway restores. Aluminium billet inventories fell WoW to 55,800 mt due to reduced arrivals from logistics issues and Yunnan smelter cuts, but SMM expects inventory growth once controls ease. | A study for the Beer Institute says Section 232 aluminum tariffs since March 2018 have cost the U.S. beverage industry $1.714 billion on 8.203 million metric tons of aluminum, largely charged through end-user pricing by mills and smelters. Brewers use aluminum heavily for cans, and the report urges tariff relief. | Research for the Beer Institute estimates U.S. beverage producers paid $1.714 billion in Section 232 aluminum tariffs between 2018 and August 2022. It argues rolling mills and smelters pass through tariff-burdened costs to brewers, raising production costs even when specific shipments are not tariffed.

SMM reports China’s tightening aluminium capacity-approval regime has encouraged smelters to secure or trade quotas to commission new projects. It highlights Shanxi Zhongrun’s 65,000 mt/year capacity replacement project scheduled for December. Overall, 228,000 mt/year of aluminium capacity is expected to be commissioned in the year through replacements. | DutchNews.nl reports that the Aldel aluminium smelter in Delfzijl was declared bankrupt shortly after court protection. Operations had already been curtailed, citing high energy prices and insufficient government support. The closure leaves about 120 workers without jobs and previously stopped processing imported aluminium, reducing supply. | Citibank deposited bulk aluminium into LME warehouses in early October as higher interest rates reduced profitability of financing trades and some deals expired. LME aluminium stocks more than doubled, backwardation/premiums eased, and analysts cited slowing demand—together pointing to increased near-term supply and bearish sentiment for aluminium.

SMM reports SHFE and LME aluminium fell on the week, with bearish sentiment driven by ECB/Fed tightening expectations, fears of global recession, COVID disruption to downstream demand and logistics, and weaker cost support from lower alumina and thermal coal. EU gas strength may boost smelter output, but overall downside risk persists. | SMM says the weighted alumina index fell to 2,792 yuan/mt, with most regions down on the week. Some losses and shutdowns capped declines, but import windows remain difficult and more capacity is set to release in Shandong, implying a north China surplus. Southwest output cuts could pressure further, keeping the market pessimistic. | SMM says alumina prices inched lower, and thermal coal also fell slightly, while smelter electricity prices were broadly stable. Lower feedstock costs reduced total aluminium smelting costs by 52 yuan/mt and narrowed profit declines, with A00 at 18,300 yuan/mt. Electricity remains the key cost driver, limiting longer-term easing. | Crisil Ratings expects Indian aluminium producers’ operating margins to fall by over 1,200 bps to 22–24% in 2022-23, down from 36% last fiscal. The deterioration is attributed to lower realizations and higher production costs, mainly power, which may weigh on supply conditions and sentiment for aluminium prices. | Kuusakoski is investing in a new Finnish copper centre to recycle copper- and aluminium-containing cables, radiators and transformers. The plant will crush and separate material and produce cleaner copper and aluminium fractions, claiming ~40 tons of copper per week capacity. Completion is targeted for 2023, enhancing recycled supply locally.

EU sectors call for cautious implementation of the CBAM/ETS as part of “Fit for 55”. CBAM would charge importers for emissions certificates from 2026, initially for aluminium and other high-carbon sectors, while companies warn soaring carbon costs and energy prices have already driven unprecedented aluminium capacity idling.

Mississippi lawmakers are debating Gov. Tate Reeves’ plan to provide about $240 million in taxpayer incentives for a company to build a new aluminum plant in the Golden Triangle. Democrats and Republicans criticize the lack of detail and vetting, but the deal is unlikely to be stopped, supporting near-term aluminum production expectations. | Copper prices edged lower as bleak near-term manufacturing and metals demand outweighed hopes that China may ease COVID-19 controls next year. Global risk sentiment weakened ahead of a large U.S. rate hike, likely stifling activity. Tight supply and low inventories were cited as support, leaving overall bias mixed. | The report says Ghana’s sole aluminum smelter has shut down as a pay strike threatens safety. With one of the key local primary production sources offline, short-term aluminum supply availability may tighten, likely supporting near-term aluminium prices on expectations of reduced output and higher costs. | Mississippi lawmakers are reportedly considering incentives to encourage development of an aluminum plant. Production incentives typically strengthen the case for new or expanded capacity, supporting near-term expectations for aluminum demand from construction/start-up activity and potential supply outlook. | Steel Dynamics selected Columbus, Mississippi for a greenfield aluminum flat-rolled mill using recycled scrap, targeting 650,000 tonnes of output and product lines for packaging and automotive. The project is expected to start mid-2025, and is positioned as a decarbonized expansion to meet customer co-location needs and supply-chain advantages. | Mississippi lawmakers are seeking approval of nearly $247 million in state incentives for an aluminum plant tied to Steel Dynamics. The project is expected to create around 1,000 jobs by 2029. While company details are undisclosed, the policy push is likely supportive for aluminum investment sentiment. | Base metals prices rose on Tuesday after widespread rumors that China might change/ease its zero-Covid policy. Three-month futures gained for copper (+2.7%), aluminium (+0.9%), and nickel (+8.2%). Analysts said reopening hopes lifted China risk assets and triggered short covering; nickel additionally benefited from strong EV sales.

Mississippi lawmakers finalized incentive funding to attract Steel Dynamics’ $2.5 billion aluminum mill complex in Lowndes County. The project is expected to create about 1,000 jobs in seven years and includes multiple facilities. Officials cite workforce training and potential co-located industries, boosting near-term confidence for aluminium-related demand. | SMM expects China’s aluminium extrusion output in 2022 to fall 3.86% to 20.11 million mt, mainly due to a sharp drop in construction aluminium extrusion from pandemic-related disruptions and a real estate downturn. Industrial extrusion grows on PV and new energy vehicle demand, but overall output reverses upward trends. | Copper prices fell as markets digested a hawkish Fed stance signaling prolonged policy tightening, weakening risk sentiment and near-term demand expectations. China’s elevated COVID cases added pressure on industrial and construction activity. The report also notes lower tin and lead, with aluminium dipping and nickel edging up. | Balco (Vedanta) conducted successful trial runs using biodiesel at its aluminium smelter to preheat molten-metal ladles by reducing moisture before casting. The company frames this as part of its decarbonization plan, including higher renewables/biomass use. The report provides no production impact or timelines. | Mississippi lawmakers approved nearly $247 million in incentives for an aluminum mill project by Steel Dynamics, including a flat-rolled aluminium production facility and related industrial units. The state says the project is a major job creator with $2.5 billion investment and includes roads, land help, and income tax rebates.

Mississippi lawmakers approved funding to support site preparation for a planned $2.5 billion aluminum mill near Lowndes County, including $81 million upfront for development needs such as land, environmental/engineering work, and some road/bridge repairs. Developers expect ground to break in December, with operations targeted for 2026. | SHFE aluminium gained after the Fed’s hike was largely digested, while aluminium prices were supported by easing supply pressure—rumored production cuts at Henan following other provinces—and continued destocking. Social inventories fell sharply and spot premiums stayed elevated despite weaker downstream buying, keeping aluminium near range highs. | SMM estimates China’s aluminium plate/sheet and strip output in 2022 at 12.9 million mt (+2% YoY), with aluminium foil output at 4.75 million mt (+3%). Food packaging and NEV-driven battery/auto semis support growth, while some foil types fall due to weak margins and demand. | Constellium said it is joining the First Movers Coalition to decarbonize the aluminium industry, aiming that by 2030 10% of its primary aluminium purchases are near-zero emissions and at least 50% of its aluminium inputs are recycled. It highlights sustainability targets but provides no short-term pricing impact.

Novelis broke ground on a $50m aluminum recycling center in South Korea with 100,000 tons/year capacity, expected online in 2024. The project expands recycling capacity by over 20% to 440 kt in Korea and increases processing of different scrap types, aiming to supply low-carbon aluminum sheet using more recycled content. | SMM reports that alumina prices fell, but aluminium smelter costs rose due to higher electricity/thermal power costs, with weighted average power price climbing to 0.463 yuan/kWh. Although full costs increased to 17,890 yuan/mt, A00-based real-time profits fell to 390 yuan/mt as aluminium prices dropped, suggesting bearish near-term sentiment. | SMM reports tight aluminium ingot inventories reaching new lows and widening spot premiums. Several aluminium smelters in Henan and Shandong plan heating-season capacity cuts (~200,000 mt) due to cost pressure and environmental requirements. With demand improving and profitability weak, near-term supply tightness is expected to support aluminium prices. | China’s aluminium ingot social inventories in eight major markets fell to 571,000 mt on Nov 7, the lowest level in 2022, down from last Thursday and a month ago. Some areas rose on arrivals, but Wuxi and south China declined; transport restrictions and ongoing rigid downstream restocking underpin the drawdown. | Steel Dynamics plans to build a next-generation recycled aluminium flat rolled mill in Columbus, Mississippi with 650,000 tonnes annual capacity. The project is supported by state financial assistance for site work and workforce training, positioning increased recycled aluminium supply for beverage packaging and automotive use. | Lowndes County, Mississippi approved resolutions supporting an $81 million upfront state investment and $245 million in bonds to enable a $2 billion aluminium mill project with Steel Dynamics/Aluminum Dynamics. The facility is expected to add about 1,000 jobs, but the timing of output is not specified, implying limited near-term price pressure.

Novelis broke ground on a $53 million aluminum recycling center in Ulsan, aiming to reduce carbon emissions and produce 100,000 tons of low-carbon sheet ingot annually from recycled content. Built by a Novelis-Kobe Steel JV, it is expected to begin operating in 2024, implying limited immediate price pressure.

SMM reports aluminium prices softened in Sept-Oct but remain supported by weak supply and falling inventories. Overseas smelters cut output due to high natural gas costs, while Yunnan smelters reduced production for power shortages. With low social inventories around 500,000-600,000 mt in November, prices are expected to find support. | SMM reports China’s metallurgical-grade alumina output rose but supply surplus is expanding, while alumina prices remain weak and raw material costs increased. Profits have fallen below 50 yuan/mt, and many plants are near break-even. With high operating pressure, further production reductions or stoppages are possible in November. | Copper prices fell after China’s weaker economic indicators and a fresh rise in COVID-19 cases increased concerns about industrial demand. Despite some support from tight physical supplies, three-month LME copper slipped around 0.5% and SHFE copper diverged. Market focus now shifts to US inflation and politics.

European Aluminium argues that CBAM should not include indirect emissions until an adequate methodology is developed for aluminium’s indirect carbon costs. The group warns inclusion could increase carbon leakage and hurt EU aluminium’s transition to carbon neutrality, citing lost capacity and high electricity costs since 2021.
