Stocks remained low due to resilient domestic demand, especially from solar and power sectors, supporting near-term pricing. | Copper prices rose ahead of China’s upcoming GDP data, driven by a brighter demand outlook and concerns about longer-term supply amid energy-transition demand. Reuters cites robust consumption from renewables and infrastructure and Glencore planning to close Mount Isa copper operations by 2025. LME aluminium and nickel also edged higher. | SMM says orange heavy-air-pollution alerts in Shandong led alumina refineries to cut roaster operation by about 50% for Oct. 14–22. With the heating season, emission-control restrictions may persist into Q4, keeping domestic alumina supplies tight and potentially supportive for aluminium prices. | Glencore announced it will cease copper mining at Mount Isa from 2025, with underground operations and the concentrator closing in the second half of 2025. Over two years, activity will transition toward zinc assets and copper smelting/refining. Local leaders and politicians urge government action to mitigate impacts, highlighting potential copper supply risk. | NioCorp announced successful pilot-scale production of a 1 kg aluminum-scandium (Al-Sc) ingot and progress toward aluminum-scandium master alloy, using a process said to improve yields and reduce emissions. The company expects eventual commercial production, but the current milestone is small and unlikely to affect near-term aluminum prices. | Copper and other base metals rose in London after China posted better-than-expected GDP growth, supporting expectations for higher demand from the world’s top consumer. The piece cites improving consumption/industrial activity and robust non-ferrous output alongside relatively low Chinese stockpiles, driving gains in copper, aluminium and nickel. | A letter from major manufacturers and buyers urges the U.S. Department of Energy to heavily invest in American-made “clean aluminum” using Inflation Reduction Act funds. It cites curtailments from high fossil-fuel energy costs and argues DOE support could secure low-cost power and expand primary aluminium supply, helping prices.

Copper prices edged up as China’s better-than-expected GDP and signs of improving consumption/industrial activity supported demand expectations. Gains were modest due to a firmer U.S. dollar and higher Treasury yields as markets expected rates higher for longer. LME zinc, tin, lead fell while aluminium rose. Nickel was down on SHFE. | Romanian aluminium producer Alro announced a renewed multi-year contract with Airbus to supply aluminium flat rolled products used in aircraft manufacturing. The agreement renews the prior 2016 deal and highlights Alro’s capacity and ongoing investments. The new orders provide clearer demand visibility for aluminium. | Copper rose 0.6% on LME support from stronger-than-expected Chinese growth, consumption and industrial activity, plus hopes for additional stimulus. Gains were capped by a firmer dollar as U.S. yields approached 5%. Aluminium’s cash discount narrowed, indicating tighter LME inventories; nickel slipped slightly. | Ghana’s GIADEC aims to reduce reliance on imported aluminium products by expanding local downstream production. The piece cites Ghana’s large bauxite resources, a concession awarded to Rocksure, and existing use of VALCO output by local manufacturers for cables, hollowware and roofing sheets, supporting higher aluminium processing demand.

Copper prices edged lower as the U.S. dollar strengthened following a surge in the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield. Three-month LME copper fell 0.1%. Aluminium was also slightly down, while nickel gained modestly, reflecting mixed but generally softer pricing linked to currency pressure and tempered China stimulus hopes. | Glencore plans to wind up Mount Isa copper operations in 2025, and politicians dispute the company’s claim the mine is not economically viable. The Queensland government is funding worker support and past smelter maintenance, while calls grow for state intervention to keep copper mining—and smelting—operating. | Oman’s Green Tech Mining & Services, backed by OMCO, plans to move from pilots to commercial production by reprocessing OMCO legacy sites. It targets copper output from 15 million tonnes of tailings and contaminated groundwater in Suhar starting from late 2023, with further ramp and gold production in 2024. Invested in a copper tailings remediation plant. | The piece argues aluminum demand is being supported by a growing aircraft backlog as air travel rebounds and production lags. Aerospace manufacturers use large shares of aluminum in aircraft components, and sanctions and supply-chain disruptions raise sourcing challenges, supporting firmer aluminum demand and potentially higher near-term prices. | Aluminum prices are described as sideways after an end-September spike and October pullback. The article links price behavior to U.S.-EU discussions on Section 232 aluminum exemptions and possible future tariff action, while noting Russian-origin discounts are being absorbed by buyers and overall price moves remain driven by broader trends.

Copper prices opened lower on Monday as Middle East conflict concerns intensified after fears Israel-Hamas could broaden, while LME/SHFE copper stocks rose. Three-month LME copper fell 0.4% and Shanghai December copper dropped 0.8%. Aluminium also dipped slightly; nickel was slightly higher. | SMM reports SHFE aluminium seen stabilising as China’s supply stays steady, with new Inner Mongolia capacity expected to start and no major smelter cuts anticipated. Downstream demand was average, while real-estate-linked caution weighed on construction extrusion. However, social aluminium ingot inventory fell 10,000 mt, supporting prices. | Copper prices fell as investors worried that the Israel-Hamas war could broaden in the Middle East, while LME inventories rose and Chinese import demand improved. Softer copper demand indicators were also noted. Aluminium also dipped alongside the broader metals move, reflecting risk-off sentiment. | India’s government issued mandatory BIS quality norms for copper products (and also drums/tins) under Quality Control Orders, effective six months after publication. Products cannot be produced, sold, imported, or stocked without a BIS mark. The stated aim is to curb imports of substandard goods and boost domestic manufacturing, supporting compliant copper demand. | India’s DPIIT issued mandatory BIS quality norms for copper products (including wire rods, solid drawn copper and copper tubes) and for drums and tin containers, effective six months after notification. Goods without BIS marks cannot be produced, sold, traded, imported or stocked, aimed at curbing substandard imports and boosting compliant domestic manufacturing. | South32 said Q1 alumina production rose to 1.29 million tonnes from 1.26 million tonnes a year earlier, with sequential growth of 3%. Quarterly aluminium output increased to 288,000 tonnes from 279,000 tonnes, also rising sequentially. The company maintained annual guidance amid continued low-carbon aluminium growth. | Copper prices fell to 11-month lows as China’s property crisis and fading hopes for stimulus reduced demand expectations, while Middle East conflict risk hurt sentiment. Traders also pointed to rising SHFE and LME warehouse inventories weighing on prices. The article notes three-month aluminium was down 0.7%, reflecting broader industrial-metals weakness. | Carolina Rush Corporation begins a 2,000-meter core drilling program at its Brewer Gold-Copper Project. The company aims to expand known copper mineralization and test new zones, citing prior drill results with copper grades near 0.15–0.27%. No aluminium-related developments are mentioned. | Century Aluminum said it reached final approval on a new three-year electricity contract with South Carolina’s Santee Cooper for its Mt. Holly aluminum smelter. The 295MW power deal starts Jan. 1, 2024 and runs through Dec. 2026, helping maintain operations at current capacity and reduce curtailment risk.

Rusal agreed to buy a 30% stake in Chinese alumina producer Hebei Wenfeng New Materials for about $262 million, pending approvals. The investment would secure roughly 1.44 million tons of alumina to offset Russia’s shortage caused by Australia’s export ban, supporting Rusal’s aluminium production. | Norwegian aluminium producer Hydro said third-quarter adjusted core profit fell more than expected and warned that aluminium markets are challenging and uncertain. Reuters notes LME aluminium cash was slightly higher at the time of reporting, but the earnings miss and cautious outlook can weigh on near-term sentiment around aluminium prices. | China’s primary aluminium net imports surged in September, with Russia dominating inflows. The import window boosted volumes, but by late September/October prices and inventories weakened, cutting import profits to losses. SMM expects October net imports to stay elevated (~150,000 mt) but warns November could see sharp drops if Rusal curbs unprofitable smelters. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) marked the arrival of Mitsubishi Power’s M701JAC gas turbine for its PS5 Block 4 expansion. The combined-cycle unit (680.9 MW) is expected to increase PS5 complex capacity from 1,800 MW to 2,481 MW and reduce CO2 intensity, supporting cleaner, more efficient aluminium production. | Rusal, owner of the Shannon Estuary alumina-to-aluminium smelter, agreed to buy a 30% stake in China’s Hebei Wenfeng New Materials for 245m euros to secure alumina supply amid Ukraine-war and sanctions disruptions. The firm cites alumina shortages for its smelters and sanctions threats.

South32’s Worsley Alumina has converted a first boiler from coal to natural gas, starting operations in late September, with a second boiler planned for 2024. South32 says the change reduces operational GHG emissions by up to 208kt CO2-e per year and mitigates local coal supply challenges, supporting alumina production stability. | Zambia, facing large external debt, is pursuing major copper expansion with investments such as KoBold’s $150m Mingomba copper-cobalt project and renewed mining/refining activity. The government aims to triple production by 2032, supported by debt restructuring and improved operational control, which is generally supportive for near-term copper market expectations. | India issued mandatory Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) quality marks for nine categories of copper products, plus quality orders for drums and tin containers, effective six months after notification. Non-compliant goods cannot be produced, sold, imported, or stocked. This likely tightens copper product availability and supports prices near term. | Kaiser Aluminum reported Q3 2023 results, with modestly lower net sales driven by a 7% decline in average selling price per pound and discussion of aluminum-linked hedged alloyed metal costs. Demand was strongest in aerospace, while packaging destocking continued. No major aluminum supply or tariff shock was announced.

SMM’s aluminium morning comment reports SHFE 2312 aluminium slightly lower overnight, while LME aluminium edges up. It cites macro uncertainty (Fed hike expectations, Israel-Gaza conflict) and fundamentals: China operating capacity near a peak, mixed downstream orders, lower social-warehouse pick-ups, and no imminent inventory decline. Overall, prices expected rangebound short term. | HK-listed Rusal is reported to buy a 30% stake in Chinese alumina refinery operator Wenfeng. Because alumina refining underpins aluminium production, the move may strengthen supply confidence and marginally improve near-term expectations for aluminium availability and processing economics.

Glencore said it expects full-year copper and zinc despite lower first-nine-month output, citing Cobar mine sale and weaker by-product production. It lowered full-year nickel guidance after sharp production drops and also adjusted ferrochrome targets. These updates are likely to affect copper and nickel market expectations near term; aluminium is not addressed.

Yunnan aluminium smelters face renewed dry-season output cuts after prior curtailments. Provincial authorities plan to manage industrial electricity demand as the dry season approaches. SMM says four smelters will cut capacity by 9%-40%, totaling ~1.15 million mt, potentially lowering daily output from ~15,000 to ~12,000 mt, affecting domestic supply in early 2024. | Copper fell Tuesday as weak October China manufacturing data renewed demand concerns, prompting selling despite support from a softer dollar and falling LME/cross-exchange stocks. The article notes aluminium also slipped and nickel retreated, indicating broader base-metal sentiment tied to China’s industrial outlook. | Sandbox Royalties announced acquisitions of gold purchase and royalty/stream assets, including a 1.0% GRR on Southern Copper’s El Pilar project and a partial NSR royalty on Artemis Gold’s Blackwater. These transactions may affect expectations for copper-related cash flows, but there is no immediate operational or supply change described for copper or aluminium.

Yunnan’s aluminium smelters began cutting output this week to comply with electricity curbs expected to last until April. The cuts target 9%–40% of capacity, reducing about 1.15 million tonnes, as the dry season and hydropower shortages constrain production. | Verofax was selected in Emirates Global Aluminum’s ramp-up programme. The article links the initiative to the EU’s Carbon Border Tax rules, which can impose up to 35% tariffs on non-compliant high-emission exports such as aluminum. It argues this creates opportunity for low-emission UAE exporters and EGA trade competitiveness. | A U.S. trade remedy petition (antidumping and countervailing duties) was filed on Oct. 4, 2023, targeting alleged dumped and subsidized extrusion aluminum imports from 15 countries, covering more than $3 billion in accused shipments. The update notes recent Commerce initiation and ITC procedural developments.

Sibanye-Stillwater confirmed it will exercise an option to buy Tasmania’s Mt Lyell copper mine and is conducting a feasibility study for reopening. The article highlights copper as key for the clean-energy transition and notes restart cost and potential annual output, supporting expectations of improved copper supply prospects. | SMM’s conference takeaway outlines China’s aluminum capacity reaching near-maximum levels, with operating rates recovering and Yunnan largely resuming production. Despite earlier energy-constraint disruptions, output is now near full capacity, increasing operating availability. The piece highlights persistent southwest supply pressure, which likely limits aluminum price upside. | Verofax was selected for Emirates Global Aluminum’s Ramp-Up Programme. The piece links the initiative to EU Carbon Border Tax rules that impose up to 35% tariffs on non-compliant high-emission commodities, explicitly including aluminum. It suggests better traceability/compliance could boost UAE aluminum exports.

Copper gained for a second straight week as a weaker U.S. dollar supported commodities amid subdued sentiment after poor Chinese economic data. LME aluminium rose modestly and LME nickel edged higher alongside broader risk-driven moves, suggesting macro/FX factors dominated rather than fresh metal-specific fundamentals. | SMM says China’s domestic aluminum ingot stocks jumped to 657,000 mt (highest in nearly six months), with accumulation driven by backlog shipments after transport recovery, continued imported ingot inflows despite the spot import window, and weak downstream demand. Off-season pressure and reduced production reinforce supply overhang in coming weeks. | LME three-month copper rose about 0.3% to $8,164.5/mt as a weaker U.S. dollar after key U.S. data supported prices, offsetting bearish pressure from higher exchange-registered copper inventories. Copper’s year-to-date weakness persists on softer China demand expectations. LME aluminium also gained. | Bank of Central African States data show Cemac export commodity prices up 2% QoQ in Q3 2023, driven by higher iron and manganese. However, aluminum export prices fell 2.5% after a 5.8% drop in Q2, indicating local aluminum pressure. Gold also declined, but only aluminium is tracked here.

Copper prices held in a tight range as investors balanced higher supply and uncertain demand against support from a weaker U.S. dollar. China’s slower winter consumption and uneven recovery added demand uncertainty. The report also shows mild easing in LME aluminium and a decline in LME nickel, alongside small overall moves. | London copper rose 0.4% as a weaker U.S. dollar supported commodity prices and China stimulus improved risk appetite. The move was limited by slower seasonal winter demand in China and slowing destocking/inventory rises. LME aluminium and nickel edged higher, reflecting the same supportive financial backdrop despite mixed demand signals. | Copper rose to one-month highs as a weaker U.S. dollar and China’s commitment to boost imports encouraged fund buying ahead of key Chinese economic data. Aluminium also hit a one-month high on low Shanghai Futures Exchange stocks, falling LME-approved inventories, and China Yunnan output cuts from power curbs. | Fastmarkets assessed its US Midwest low-carbon aluminium differential P1020A at zero on Nov. 3, with demand for low-carbon units in the US described as sluggish and no premium being paid. Some customer inquiries exist, mainly for beverage cans, automotive and construction, but near-term pressure is limited.

U.S. petitioners filed with the Department of Commerce seeking antidumping duties on aluminum extrusions imported from Vietnam. Fourteen Vietnamese firms were accused of dumping, with margins cited around 41.84%. Vietnam’s authorities warned exporters to cooperate with the probe, which could reduce imports and support aluminium prices. | The US Department of Commerce launched an anti-dumping investigation into aluminum extrusions and products from 15 countries, including Vietnam. The alleged dumping margin for Vietnam is 41.84%. Duties would follow only if the ITC finds injury to the US industry. The process may constrain imports over coming weeks. | The US Department of Commerce launched an anti-dumping investigation into aluminum extrusions and products from Vietnam and 14 other countries, prompted by US labor union petitions. Vietnam’s alleged dumping margin is 41.84%. Duties are possible only if the ITC finds injury, affecting near-term aluminum import expectations. | SMM’s conference takeaway highlights persistent domestic aluminum supply fluctuation risk, including an expected 1.15 million mt Yunnan capacity cut. It links fast-growing photovoltaic power and aluminum PV frames/extrusions demand to higher aluminum usage, while noting tight 2023 supply-demand balance and rising spot prices and profits in September. | Pan Global reports high-grade copper intersections at its La Romana copper-tin-silver discovery, including 15.9m at 1.15% CuEq1 (1.04% Cu) from 79m, and says mineralization remains open and extends over more than 1.35 km. It also notes pending results and plans more drilling, but no production impact. | EU-US talks on GASSA collapsed without agreement on measures to limit high-carbon steel and aluminium contributing to overcapacity and to support lower-carbon production. Sources expect negotiations to be stalled and any year-end outcome to be partial, likely focused on the tariff-rate quota framework rather than carbon provisions. | Copper fell 1% from a one-month high as a stronger U.S. dollar pressured dollar-priced commodities and China’s trade data pointed to uneven recovery. The article also notes aluminium down 1% and nickel down 2.5%, indicating broad industrial-metals weakness tied to demand concerns. | Aluminium Deutschland says German primary aluminium production nearly halved in Q3 2023, while raw aluminium output fell and semi-finished volumes declined sharply, citing weak orders and high energy costs. It warns energy-market tension and poor industrial competitiveness, pointing to demand headwinds in construction and automotive sectors. | Parties argue before the U.S. ITC over a preliminary injury investigation into allegedly subsidized/dumped aluminum extrusions. Petitioners cite surging accused imports and urge cumulation and broad coverage, while respondents argue limited injury due to other constraints. The process may ultimately lead to AD/CVD duties affecting aluminum extrusion import flows and pricing. | Suriname’s presidential commission says Indian, Chinese and Middle East firms have informally shown interest in a bauxite concession in the Bakhuis region, with deposits estimated at 324 million tons. The government plans to award the concession by November 2024, with detailed power and logistics proposals due late January.

Morgan Stanley turns bullish on aluminium, citing risks of tighter winter supply in China and declining LME inventories, alongside energy/production constraints. For copper, Citi/others flag near-term demand vulnerability (slower cyclicals, China property and EV uptake) with a widening surplus, though short-covering risks could support dips. | Morgan Stanley expects aluminium to outperform, projecting higher LME prices supported by declining LME inventories and possible winter supply cuts in China amid constrained power availability. It also notes a potential supply deficit re-emerging later. For copper, demand headwinds persist, but short-covering and potential supply downgrades could limit downside. | NALCO reported a Q2 profit rise, driven mainly by its aluminium segment where pre-tax profit increased nearly 14%, supported by strong aluminium demand. The company benefits from India’s ramped-up infrastructure spending ahead of general elections. Revenue fell due to higher raw material costs, while Hindalco is set to report next. | The EPA said South Carolina’s DHEC must seek more public input before the Mt. Holly Century Aluminum plant can raise emissions limits, after environmental groups challenged permit changes processed as “minor modifications.” The changes would have increased particulates by ~50% and sulfur dioxide by ~one-third, but the process is now under scrutiny. | U.S. authorities initiated antidumping and countervailing duty investigations covering aluminum extrusions from 15 countries, expanding prior AD/CVD scope and reducing exclusions, including for further-processed extrusions. Estimated dumping margins for some countries are very high, with preliminary rulings due later in 2023–2024, which could raise near-term aluminum costs. | Century Aluminum’s earnings call discusses Q3 falling LME and regional premiums, low inventory positioning for potential recovery, and China deficits versus smelter curtailment risks. It also highlights higher-than-expected power costs, Jamalco outage recovery, and the IRA Section 45X aluminum production tax credit, pending Treasury guidance.

Hindalco missed Q2 profit expectations as LME aluminium prices fell 5.1% sequentially and alumina declined 4.1%. India’s aluminium factory activity grew, but at the slowest pace in five months. Hindalco’s U.S. unit revenue dropped, while its copper revenue rose on higher shipments. | Villagers in Odisha’s Rayagada protest Vedanta’s planned Sijimali open-pit bauxite mine, alleging police violence, intimidation, arrests, and pressure to halt resistance. Activists also cite recent forest-law changes weakening community consent. The disputed bauxite project could face delays, tightening bauxite supply expectations for aluminium. | Villagers in Banteji, Odisha, say police raids and violence by company officials have occurred as they fight a proposed bauxite mine. The conflict threatens operations and local approvals for bauxite extraction. Any near-term disruption to bauxite supply could tighten alumina inputs and support aluminium prices. | Century Aluminum secured a new three-year electricity deal with state utility Santee Cooper to restart the remaining 25% of its Mount Holly aluminum pots. The contract provides enough power to lift annual capacity to about 225,000 tons, subject to market conditions and permitting steps. Company also plans further investments using sale proceeds. | Paris 2024 will decide by end-November whether to build an aluminium judges’ tower and related infrastructure for surfing events in Teahupo’o, Tahiti. Local residents and a petition oppose it due to potential reef damage, while officials consider alternatives like reusing prior foundations or reverting to a wooden tower.

London copper edged higher to about $8,052/t, supported by firmer equities, but stayed near a two-week low as a stronger U.S. dollar and China demand uncertainty weighed. LME aluminium dipped and nickel rose, reflecting mixed sentiment. Chile’s Codelco production guidance added minor context. | Copper hovered near a two-week low as a firm U.S. dollar and uncertainty about China’s and Europe’s recovery weighed on demand expectations. Investors awaited U.S. inflation for rate outlook clues. China stimulus needs also raised concerns. Aluminium was steady and nickel rose modestly, but copper sentiment remained bearish. | Copper prices rose on Monday after bearish traders covered shorts, lifting three-month LME copper about 1% to $8,115/t. Gains were limited by uncertainty over economic growth, high interest rates, and weaker China demand signals. Aluminium rose slightly; nickel fell, reflecting broader mixed base-metal sentiment ahead of data.

Copper prices edged down as the US dollar firmed, while traders awaited key US inflation data to gauge whether the Fed may raise rates further. Powell’s recent comments have tempered hopes the hiking cycle is ending. LME aluminium and nickel showed only slight moves, suggesting mainly macro-driven effects. | Goldman Sachs expects commodity prices to rise in 2024, citing a less hawkish Fed and lower recession risk. The note forecasts oil around $80–$100/bbl and is optimistic on industrial metals, projecting an average copper price of about $9,200/mt in 2024 and positive expectations for aluminium. | Goldman Sachs advises a long stance on commodities, citing a less hawkish Fed and lower recession risks that should support demand. It expects crude oil to trade within $80–100/bbl in 2024 and is also optimistic on copper and aluminium, projecting average copper around $9,200/mt in 2024. | Si6 Metals announced jagged high-grade copper-silver drill intersections at its Dibete project in Botswana, including 2m at 4.2% copper and 268g/t silver, tied to geophysical anomalies. The results strengthen the perceived copper sulphide potential in early drilling, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Canadian Copper reports trenching and exploration at its Murray Brook West project and updates on court-supervised efforts to restart the former Caribou Mine. The company notes existing toll-milling infrastructure and argues Murray Brook could extend mine life, but no clear near-term production or shipment figures are provided. | U.S. Commerce initiated antidumping and countervailing duty investigations on aluminum extrusions imported from 15 countries. Petitioners allege widespread dumping and subsidization and seek expanded scope, including previously excluded alloys and third-country processed products. Potential affirmative AD/CVD findings could raise import costs and tighten aluminum extrusion supply. | EU Raw Materials Week coverage highlights policy measures to secure strategic raw materials for net-zero technologies. It emphasizes copper’s role and the need to streamline permitting and lower energy costs for electricity-intensive production. It also notes recycling regulation could benefit metals including nickel, supporting medium-term supply expectations. | Environmental groups (Sierra Club and Environmental Integrity Project) intend to sue Alcoa’s Warrick aluminum smelter for Ohio River water pollution permit violations. They cite repeated excesses, including mercury and other discharge limits (including aluminum, copper, nickel). The notice allows 60 days to comply, implying potential compliance costs but no shutdown detail. | Aluminum prices are described as range-bound but facing bearish signals: global premiums have eased sharply, with U.S. Midwest and Europe duty premiums at multi-month lows. Norsk Hydro reported steep drops in aluminum sales, and LME dynamics show ample Russian material in warehouses amid weaker industrial and real-estate demand. | Ghana’s GIADEC and Rocksure International presented updated MRE results for bauxite at Nyinahin Block B, with resources revised upward (at least ~375MMT vs prior ~250MMT). The government-linked initiative aims for local refinery and smelter development, improving investor confidence for the Integrated Aluminium Industry and supporting aluminium sentiment.

A class-action lawsuit was filed against the Mt. Holly Century Aluminum smelting plant over multiple alumina dust emissions incidents. The EPA recently ruled in favor of a petition to stop polluting and require more public input before permit changes. DHEC is investigating and may assess penalties if permit violations are found. | Reuters reports China’s October primary aluminium production rose 6% year-on-year to a record monthly high of 3.62 million tonnes. Smelters benefited from much higher profits, cheaper power and firm demand, but the surge in output lifts near-term supply expectations for aluminium. | Goose Creek residents filed a class-action lawsuit against Century Aluminum, alleging increased alumina/aluminum oxide particulate emissions linked to malfunctions in emissions control systems on Sept. 3, 16, and 30 and earlier in 2023. The suit seeks damages, citing health impacts and property and pet harm. | Copper traded near a five-week high as the dollar weakened following softer U.S. inflation data, boosting dovish Fed rate-cut expectations. LME copper was steady-to-higher and Shanghai copper rose. The report also shows LME aluminium gaining slightly, while nickel fell on the day. | Brantford city council is tightening scrap-yard bylaws to curb copper and metal theft, including limits on accepting certain items, requiring police notifications within 24 hours, and banning cash transactions. Officials cite hundreds of theft reports since 2019, but acknowledge theft may shift to yards elsewhere. | Brantford’s city council plans to tighten scrap-yard bylaws to curb copper and metal thefts, including banning cash transactions, requiring traceable identification, and notifying police within 24 hours of certain purchase offers. The goal is to make stolen copper/metal harder to monetize, though thieves may use yards outside the city. | Hydro and UK EV startup WEVC will explore using Hydro’s renewable-energy, low-carbon and recycled aluminium for WEVC’s electric van. The low-carbon aluminium has a much lower CO2 footprint than the global average, aiming to cut the van’s embedded emissions and enable end-of-life recycling. Testing starts in 2024, with production in late 2025. | Hydro Extrusions and WEVC partnered to supply low-CO2, recycled aluminium extrusions for WEVC’s planned eCV1 electric light commercial vehicle. WEVC targets a >50% reduction in embedded production carbon and plans to explore closed-loop recycling. The deal may support low-carbon aluminium demand, but near-term price impact appears limited. | Hydro Extrusions is partnering with WEVC to supply recycled, low-carbon aluminium for the eCV1 electric light commercial vehicle chassis, aiming to cut embedded carbon by over 50% versus other LCVs. The firms will also explore closed-loop recycling, potentially supporting demand for low-CO2 aluminium, though volumes appear limited near term.

Copper prices eased from near six-week highs as a stronger U.S. dollar—supported by retail sales and cooling inflation data—reduced demand for dollar-priced commodities and reinforced expectations that the Fed will cut rates later. In the same report, LME aluminium and nickel also fell modestly. | GIADEC says mining of about half a million tonnes of bauxite from the Nyinahin range in Ghana will soon begin after completing a Mineral Resource Estimate and social impact analysis. The corporation highlights reserve size and responsible mining measures. The project could improve near-term expectations for aluminium feedstock supply. | Northstar Gold reported preliminary Phase I drilling results at the Cam Copper Mine, a historic VMS copper producer, with high-grade copper intersections (including very high Cu zones) and notes zones remain open for down-plunge/strike expansion. Assays are pending for some holes; no aluminium-linked impacts are mentioned. | Colossus Resources signed an option agreement to acquire 100% interests in Chilean Calvario and Mirador porphyry projects targeting copper (± Mo ± Au), with plans for at least $2.5m of exploration over two years. The news is exploration-stage and does not indicate immediate supply changes for tracked metals. | A caustic spill injured five workers at Alcoa’s Pinjarra alumina refinery in Western Australia. Emergency services responded on 14 November and injured staff were treated and released or expected to be released. The caustic is used to dissolve alumina from bauxite; the incident’s cause is under investigation. | A class action lawsuit was filed against Century Aluminum over dust and alleged harmful emissions of alumina/particulates from the Mount Holly smelter. The company acknowledged a malfunction, completed repairs (descaling and replacing collector bags), and said operations returned to normal. The dispute could raise short-term operating and compliance risk. | Ghana Integrated Aluminium Corporation (GIADEC) says feasibility work and a Mineral Resource Estimate support mining around half a million tonnes of bauxite from the Nyinahin hills in Ashanti Region, alongside environmental and social impact studies. Management also signals potential future refinery development, improving aluminium feedstock expectations. | Abitibi Metals announced a definitive agreement to earn up to 80% of the B26 polymetallic Cu–Zn-Au-Ag deposit in Quebec. The company highlights sizable historical copper resources and an exploration/PEA plan with drilling targeted in 2023–2024, potentially improving copper longer-term supply visibility, though near-term price impact is likely limited.

Ghana’s GIADEC and Rocksure International presented completed Mineral Resource Estimate results for bauxite at Nyinahin Block B, showing an increase to 375MMT from 250MMT historical figures. The project’s next phase includes mining lease and joint venture mine construction with a refinery solution, backed by the Asantehene. | Copper prices inched higher as the weaker U.S. dollar made dollar-priced metals cheaper, but gains were capped by renewed concerns over China demand, with home prices falling again and commentary on rising Chinese refined copper output. LME aluminium also edged up, while nickel rose slightly, indicating mixed near-term pressure. | European Aluminium says EU members are debating a sanctions package that already includes bans on some Russian aluminium product categories and urges the EU to broaden coverage to all major categories. Imports of Russian aluminium into the EU have fallen sharply, and wider bans would further tighten supply, likely supporting aluminium prices. | European Aluminium urges the EU to broaden sanctions targeting Russian aluminium, noting existing phase-out efforts and lower Russian import volumes. A potential 12th sanctions package includes bans on aluminium wires, tubes and pipes, alongside tighter enforcement of Russian oil price caps. Broader coverage could further reduce EU Russian supply and support aluminium prices. | The US International Trade Commission issued an affirmative preliminary determination of injury in trade cases over aluminum extrusions imports, citing reasonable indication of material injury from subject countries including China. The ruling advances antidumping and countervailing duty investigations, with potential provisional duties and later orders that could reduce imports.

London three-month copper rose to an eight-week high on a softer U.S. dollar and hopes that Chinese property support will improve demand. Reuters cites pledges for property financing and debt-risk resolution. LME aluminium also advanced and nickel increased, reflecting broader bullish sentiment across industrial metals. | SMM data on weekly aluminium ingot outflows from warehouses in China’s key consuming regions is presented as an indicator of physical market tightness. Higher ingot departures suggest lower readily available warehouse stocks for downstream buyers, which can reduce short-term supply and support nearby aluminium prices. | Copper prices rose to a two-month peak as Reuters linked gains to hopes of new China stimulus supporting property-related industrial demand, a softer dollar, and declining Shanghai Futures Exchange stocks. Supply concerns also emerged from protests disrupting ore processing at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama mine. Aluminium edged higher on the session. | Bonnell Aluminum said it is pleased with the ITC’s November 17, 2023 initial determination to open an investigation into allegedly unfairly traded imports of aluminum extrusions from 14 countries. The probe could lead to trade remedies that reduce import competition, supporting pricing expectations for aluminum extrusions. | Aleris won a multi-year deal to supply aluminum plates, sheets, and processed wing-skin material for Airbus aircraft manufacturing. Deliveries begin next year and build on Aleris’ existing Airbus qualification, including work for Airbus’ China facility. The announcement points to additional aluminum demand expectations.

Copper prices climbed to two-month highs as supply risks intensified. An indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine and maintenance at First Quantum’s Cobre Panama facility—both tied to protests blocking coal—supported LME and Shanghai copper gains. A weaker U.S. dollar further boosted the base-metals complex, lifting aluminium and nickel too.

Copper prices pulled back from two-month highs as a firmer U.S. dollar and renewed growth concerns weighed on sentiment. People’s Daily profiles Guangxi Yinhai Aluminum’s “green aluminum” carbon-labeled electrolysis, powered by over half clean energy and improved efficiency. Clients reportedly purchase only labeled aluminium alloy plates. The firm also lowers electricity costs and extends recycling in the region, reinforcing near-term support for aluminium demand. | Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) started building a pilot plant to convert caustic bauxite residue from its alumina refinery into manufactured soil (“Turba”). The project follows years of R&D and targets completion in 2024, aiming to reuse UAE bauxite waste locally. It is pilot-scale and mainly environmental/circular-economy focused. | Copper prices rose on the LME as a weaker U.S. dollar and a stronger yuan supported Chinese purchasing demand. The Yangshan premium hit a yearly high and front-running for potential China property stimulus boosted buying. LME nickel and aluminium were also slightly mixed in the session. | Saudi Arabia has already invested at least $12bn in aluminium industries and aims to double investment, targeting growth in mining and large projects. Officials say aluminium is prioritized for renewable energy, EV and aviation demand, and more projects are in the pipeline as part of an ongoing diversification strategy. | Great Southern Copper raised GBP905,000 via a placing to fund further exploration in Chile, including drilling at its Especularita copper-gold project and work at other targets, plus initial activity at its Monti Lithium project in Salar de Atacama. The funding is modest, with limited immediate implications for copper prices. | Copper rose on the LME as a firmer yuan and weaker U.S. dollar boosted Chinese buyers’ purchasing power for the dollar-priced metal. Front-running around potential China property stimulus further supported demand. Nickel was also higher and aluminium edged up, reflecting a broadly supportive base-metal tone.

SMM publishes a trial calculation of an aluminium spot premium range for October 24, 2023, based on publicly available information and its internal model. The statement emphasizes the data are for reference only and not investment advice, providing a premium estimate rather than any clear new market-moving factor. | Zangezur Copper and Molybdenum Combine said a sanctioned beneficial owner (Gleb Trotsenko) no longer holds a stake after selling it in 2023, and that the new large shareholder (Svetlana Ershova) acquired shares legally and avoids sanctions. Media speculate this could be sanctions circumvention, raising currency-market concerns for Armenia’s exports. | Reuters reports London copper on track for a second straight weekly gain, supported by renewed China support for the property sector boosting demand signals (higher Yangshan premium), a weaker U.S. dollar, and improved risk appetite after softer US CPI. Copper holds around $8,409/t, while aluminium and nickel drift lower.

The piece reports that aluminium is climbing as Goldman expects higher prices, citing a larger market deficit. This suggests tightening near-term supply/demand balance for aluminium and supports upward price expectations over the coming days to weeks. No comparable detail is provided for copper or nickel.

US aluminium stakeholders met with the Department of Energy to discuss decarbonizing and maintaining primary aluminium production. They argue IRA and infrastructure-law tools need greater electricity and renewables investment because high energy costs have idled or closed smelters. Calls for low-cost power could improve primary supply outlook, supporting aluminium prices. | Reuters reports copper edged higher as China’s central bank signaled accommodative policy and as a strike began at Peru’s Las Bambas copper mine, boosting supply-risk sentiment. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report raised the prospect of changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly. | Copper edged higher on hopes China’s central bank keeps policy accommodative and on a reported indefinite strike at Peru’s Las Bambas mine. Nickel rebounded from three-year lows after an industry report suggested changes to Indonesia’s nickel pricing. Aluminium slipped modestly as broader metal prices were mixed. | Benjamin Hill Mining announced two inaugural diamond drill holes at its Alotta copper-gold-molybdenum project in Yukon, confirming multiphase porphyry-style mineralization with copper and molybdenite indicators. The program is proposed to continue in May 2024, with more drilling targeting geophysical and soil geochemical anomalies. Assays are pending.

Norsk Hydro’s Capital Markets Day outlines stepped-up investment plans for low-carbon aluminium: expanding recycling (post-consumer scrap capacity) and extrusions, plus scaling renewable power via Hydro Rein/Macquarie. It reiterates decarbonization and targets for greener premiums. The update mainly affects aluminium expectations; copper impact is only generic. | Norwegian aluminium producer Hydro raised its cost-cutting and profit improvement targets, driven by digitalisation and expected growing demand for low-emission metals. Hydro forecast a “greener earnings uplift” of 2 billion Norwegian crowns annually by 2030 and noted a cash boost tied to renewables financing. The update is supportive for aluminium sentiment. | Norway’s Hydro said it aims to cut the carbon footprint of some primary aluminium to near zero by 2030, using renewable electricity and carbon capture storage. The company links the strategy to EU critical raw material status and expected demand from the energy transition, while highlighting lower emissions versus coal-based production in China. | Yunnan’s dry-season power supply has led to energy management and production cuts at local aluminum smelters. Four smelters completed reductions totaling about 1.17 million mt, with larger cuts for higher-inkot alloying ratios to secure aluminum liquid for downstream users. SMM expects near-term operations to hold unless power conditions worsen. | UBS’s base metals outlook for 2024 projects copper deficits widening to a larger shortage than previously expected, supportive for copper prices. It also forecasts stronger nickel oversupply and sustained downward pressure. Aluminium is expected to be broadly balanced but with a tendency to shortages later in 2024. | UBS forecasts base metals broadly rangebound short term. Copper is expected to shift to a slightly larger supply deficit (108k mt in 2023, 73k mt in 2024), which supports higher prices. Nickel faces intensifying oversupply and downside pressure. Aluminum is balanced overall but projected to tighten later in 2024. | Hydro outlined a 2030 plan to grow low-carbon aluminium via expanded recycling (post-consumer scrap capacity), increased extrusions, and renewable power projects, alongside decarbonization at its alumina/aluminium value chain. It expects greener premium earnings and capex updates for 2024–2028. No immediate copper-specific actions were stated. | SMM outlines 2024 copper and aluminum fundamentals, referencing weak global manufacturing PMI and OECD macro outlook, tight copper concentrate with historically low cathode inventories (though premiums pressured in October), and aluminum supply/cost and downstream operating-rate dynamics. It expects a relatively tight 2023-2024 aluminum balance and a supportive medium-term copper supply/demand setup. | The SMM outlook highlights weak global manufacturing PMI and fragile recovery, which can weigh on copper and aluminum demand. For copper, historic-low cathode inventories support premiums but October saw premium pressure from ample supply. For aluminum, China’s capacity is near a ceiling and operating rates rise, keeping balance relatively tight.  | Technavio forecasts aluminum die casting market growth of USD 36.59 billion from 2022 to 2027, driven by automotive substitution of iron/steel with aluminum and rising investment in auto and infrastructure. It is a general industry outlook with no specific immediate supply disruption, inventory change, or policy affecting near-term aluminum pricing.

London copper rose 0.6% as China’s manufacturing slowdown boosted expectations of additional government stimulus, supporting near-term demand. Prices were also supported by supply worries after Panama ordered the First Quantum Cobre Panama mine shut down following a Supreme Court contract ruling. LME aluminium edged up; nickel was slightly lower. | Copper Road Resources announced drill results from the JR Zone, reporting long intervals of copper and molybdenum mineralization in the Jogran Porphyry and extending the vertical extent of Richards Breccia. The company emphasizes continuity and potential for additional discoveries, but the update is exploration-only and doesn’t affect near-term supply.

Copper prices rose after the dollar weakened and China’s Caixin factory activity unexpectedly expanded in November, supporting demand expectations for industrial metals. The article also shows aluminium up modestly and nickel roughly unchanged, suggesting a broader positive metals tone driven mainly by macro and China data. | Antam says it shifted bauxite sales to Indonesia’s domestic market to offset the impact of the June export ban. Bauxite production and sales rose 6% in Q3, after earlier declines. The policy also bans copper exports and aims to boost local downstream processing. | BSE issued a notice that Aluminum Futures Contracts in the commodity derivatives segment will be discontinued effective December 4, 2023, following an earlier introduction notice. Trading members are directed to refer to prior exchange documentation. The change mainly impacts aluminium derivatives trading/hedging rather than underlying metal fundamentals. | Copper rose to a three-month high as Caixin showed stronger-than-expected China factory expansion, while exchange inventories declined and tighter supply signals emerged (Panama issues, less concentrate availability). A softer U.S. dollar and expectations of earlier Fed easing provided additional tailwinds. Nickel and aluminium were also modestly higher. | WEF says low-carbon aluminium production costs could be up to 40% higher than conventional output, driven by electricity switching, storage, and newer smelting/refining technologies. While end-market price increases are estimated at 1–2%, investment needs are large and low-carbon share is still small, supporting tightening supply expectations as demand grows through offtakes.

Russian aluminium producer Rusal is exploring a joint venture in India, citing India’s large bauxite deposits and aiming to study market prospects. The JV is also framed as helping reduce carbon footprint. The plan could affect near-term expectations for aluminium supply and investment sentiment, though specific volumes are not given. | Copper prices retreated Monday as the dollar strengthened, LME warehouse stocks increased, and doubts re-emerged over China’s demand prospects. Copper was down 1.3%. The report also mentions nickel pressured by surplus expectations and aluminium lower, reflecting weaker industrial-metals sentiment. | Rio Tinto and Giampaolo completed a $700m deal to form a JV, Matalco, focused on producing low-carbon recycled aluminium in the US and Canada. The JV includes 900,000 tpa recycling capacity and expands Rio’s aluminium product range. Growing recycled demand could support near-term aluminium sentiment.

Sen. Charles Schumer urged Treasury to finalize the rules for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credit and clarify whether it will apply to U.S. aluminum companies. He said the delay and uncertainty could limit aluminum manufacturers’ planning, and the credit—if extended—could support aluminum operations like Alcoa. | Pacific Ridge reported diamond drilling results at its Kliyul copper-gold project, including an interval of 111.0 m grading 0.45% CuEq. The company says drilling expands the known KMZ mineralized footprint and that mineralization remains open. The release is copper-focused and provides no near-term nickel or aluminium drivers. | Kutcho Copper Corp. reports target generation for its Kutcho copper-zinc project, highlighting high-priority drill areas such as Esso West, with historical intercepts including 7.2m at 2.0% Cu and plans for ground and drill testing in 2024. The release is exploration-focused with no clear near-term market supply impacts.

Rio Tinto outlined USD30 billion of capital investment to 2026 and forecast output growth, including higher mined copper volumes and modestly higher aluminium production in 2024 versus 2023. Investment also includes Simandou. The guidance is supportive but lacks an immediate supply shock, limiting near-term price pressure. | LB Aluminium Bhd reported a 64.6% drop in 2Q net profit to RM12.3m but said it is cautiously optimistic for the next quarter. Management linked its outlook to stabilisation in global aluminium prices, as aluminium billet is the company’s main input raw material.

London copper prices were flat as traders waited for catalysts. A nearly 1% weekly gain in the U.S. dollar index weighed on dollar-priced copper. LME aluminium slipped slightly, while nickel rose sharply; however, overall moves suggest limited new directional impetus. China export growth helped cushion sentiment. | Copper prices in London rose, reversing three sessions of declines, on stronger-than-expected Chinese export data and a weaker U.S. dollar that supports dollar-priced buying. The rally was tempered by concerns over broader economic growth and pending U.S. payrolls, while aluminium fell slightly. | International Aluminium Institute (IAI) launched a greenhouse-gas initiative requiring member firms to transparently track and report decarbonisation targets and emissions. Multiple major primary aluminium producers and downstream firms signed on at COP28, emphasizing net-zero ambitions, interim milestones, and cleaner power investments.

Pampa Metals contracted Eco Minera to conduct a ~2,500m diamond drilling program at its Piuquenes porphyry copper-gold project in Argentina, aimed at testing depth and lateral extension of known mineralization. Drilling starts in January 2024, with assays expected progressively from March. Historical intervals reported included high copper grades. | An EU industry group urges the bloc to expand sanctions on Russian aluminium, arguing current measures mainly cover wire, foil, tubes and pipes while most Russian primary metal imports remain outside scope. The EU is reviewing a 12th sanctions package that could further restrict aluminium supply, supporting price sentiment. | European Aluminium urges the EU to broaden sanctions on Russia beyond products like wire, foil and tubes, calling for bans that also cover Russian primary aluminium. The group says most Russian aluminium exports remain outside current measures and warns against circumvention, as EU reliance has fallen with decoupling efforts. | The International Aluminium Institute launched a transparent decarbonization initiative at COP28, requiring member companies to publicly track and report greenhouse-gas reduction ambitions and progress, including annual industry-wide emissions reporting. It signals decarbonization investment and targets, but does not announce immediate production cuts or procurement changes. | Anglo American said it will cut near-term production and capital spending to lower costs amid weak Chinese demand and macro uncertainty. The firm will reduce output at its Los Bronces copper operation and trim iron ore at Kumba due to logistics constraints. Shares fell sharply following the guidance. | ArcWest and Interra Copper signed an earn-in agreement to explore ArcWest’s Rip porphyry Cu-Mo project in British Columbia, including staged funding, payments, and planned 3D induced polarization surveys. It outlines geology and past drilling but no near-term production, logistics, or inventory impacts for metals. | European Aluminium urges the EU to expand sanctions on Russia beyond bans on certain Russian aluminium products, advocating inclusion of Russian primary aluminium metal. It notes most Russian aluminium exports to the EU remain outside current measures, and highlights the EU’s significant import volumes. Additional restrictions could tighten supply in coming weeks.

Aluminium prices fell to their weakest in over three months as speculative funds increased selling, driven by expectations of rising LME inventories ahead of the Third Wednesday prompt. Weak China data and a strong dollar pressured the wider metals complex, with copper and nickel also declining. | Aluminium prices fell to a three-month low as CTA/speculative funds increased selling and expectations of rising LME inventories grew. Weak China metals data and a strong U.S. dollar weighed on broader base metals markets. Aluminium is down about 10% since early October, with potential warehouse supply concerns next week. Copper also slipped.

Marubeni expects Japanese aluminium premiums to hold at $75-$95/ton in Q1 2024, then rise later in 2024 as Asian supply tightens and domestic demand firms. Premiums are benchmarked to the LME cash price. A producer’s offers reflect slack demand and high inventories, but tighter supply should support prices. | Copper prices rose in London as a weaker dollar improved currency buying power and tighter supply prospects next year were reinforced by recent mine closures/disruptions. The piece also cites China stimulus hopes supporting metals demand. Morgan Stanley notes China’s copper and aluminium output is at record highs but inventories/trade suggest absorption rather than buildup, implying solid domestic demand. It also flags supply-side risks—aluminium production cuts in Yunnan and copper disruptions in Chile/Panama—plus potential China policy easing that could support prices into 2024. | The piece expects China’s copper and aluminium demand to stay solid despite all-time-high output, with data suggesting metals are being absorbed rather than stockpiled. It also flags potential supply disruptions—aluminium cuts in Yunnan and copper disruptions in Chile/Panama—plus possible China growth easing as supportive. | Copper prices fell after China’s Central Economic Work Conference readout disappointed investors by omitting large fiscal stimulus measures, worsening expectations for demand from the world’s top consumer. LME three-month copper and Shanghai January copper both dipped, while nickel also declined and aluminium rose modestly. | The report expects December (Dec 12–Jan 10) aluminium prices to be supported by low ingot/primary aluminium inventories, driven by supply discipline and downstream restocking after low prices. However, a cautious macro backdrop, seasonal demand softness, and abundant East China spot supply limit bullish activity, keeping SHFE downside risk more balanced. | Aluminium prices fell to their lowest in nearly 15 months as oversupply expectations outweighed demand hopes. LME data showed funds’ net short positions at the highest since May 2020. The firmer U.S. dollar also pressured base metals, with aluminium weaker and copper and nickel also down. | European aluminium industry leaders call on the EU to expand sanctions by banning Russian primary aluminium imports, saying the current 12th package covers only a limited set of aluminium products. They estimate most Russian exports, especially primary metal, remain outside measures and argue a broader ban would curtail supply and support resilience in EU downstream chains. | Tennessee’s Air Pollution Control Board issued a proposed order assessing a $7,500 civil penalty on Tennessee Aluminum Processors’ secondary aluminum recycling/smelting facility in Mt. Pleasant. The order follows an April 2023 inspection, cites alleged permit violations, and requires corrective actions within 30 days, with appeal rights.

During a heatwave, NSW’s grid operator warned of significantly reduced electricity reserves. Tomago Aluminium, the state’s largest power user, may cut production on short notice—up to 600 MW off the grid within minutes—to stabilize supply. The plant seeks constant power, and outages risk write-offs and process disruption. | NSW’s power grid is under strain during a heatwave, with AEMO warning of reduced electricity reserves. Tomago Aluminium, a large interruptible power user, is ready to cut production within minutes to stabilize supply. Officials urge demand reduction, and further summer shortfalls are possible, risking smelter outages or modulation curtailment. | Copper rallied sharply, gaining 2.8% to around $8,565/t on Thursday, the biggest daily jump in nearly a year, driven by a weaker dollar and expectations of Fed rate cuts. China’s primary aluminium production fell 2.2% to 3.54 million tonnes in November versus the prior month, according to the National Bureau of Statistics. Through the first 11 months, aluminium output rose 3.9% year on year. The report provides near-term supply context for aluminium markets. | China’s November primary aluminium output rose 4.8% year-on-year to 3.54 million tons as major regions added new capacity to meet solid demand. While some production cuts occurred in Yunnan due to dry seasonal conditions, overall supply was higher, which typically pressures aluminium prices short term. | London copper was steady after a sharp prior-session jump as dovish Fed expectations pressured the dollar near four-month lows. Three-month LME copper was almost flat, while January SHFE copper rose. In the same session, aluminium fell and nickel declined, though the week trend was mixed and copper ended stronger weekly. | The UK announced new sanctions restricting imports of certain Russian-made metals and banning exports of dual-use goods. The restricted list includes copper, nickel and aluminium. Markets reacted with sharp overnight moves: palladium jumped nearly 12% and copper logged its largest intraday gain in 11 months, indicating immediate supply-risk pricing. | Century Aluminum says it welcomes U.S. Treasury interpretive guidance clarifying Section 45X of the Inflation Reduction Act for the U.S. primary aluminum industry. The company expects benefits from the 45X advanced manufacturing tax credit, potentially improving smelter economics, and plans an investor call to discuss the impact on 45X. | Century Aluminum said it welcomes U.S. Treasury interpretive guidance clarifying how Section 45X of the Inflation Reduction Act applies to the U.S. primary aluminum industry. The company, a major domestic aluminum producer, expects benefits from the advanced manufacturing tax credit and will discuss the guidance’s impact on an investor call. | Century Aluminum praised U.S. Treasury guidance clarifying how Section 45X of the Inflation Reduction Act applies to the U.S. primary aluminum industry. As the largest domestic primary aluminum producer, it expects benefits from the advanced manufacturing tax credit tied to smelters, potentially supporting aluminum economics and near-term sentiment.

China’s aluminium imports rose 34.2% year-on-year in November to 343,109 tons, driven by strong domestic demand and expectations of lower output. Thin inventories and Yunnan’s hydropower-driven output curbs are expected to reduce production into early next year, supporting aluminium prices despite weaker US/EU demand. | Kings Aluminium Industries has begun construction of a $204m Abu Dhabi manufacturing and recycling plant at Kezad. The facility will process molten aluminium, billets and aluminium waste, using raw material sourced from Emirates Global Aluminium. The project expands downstream aluminium capacity and recycling activity, supporting regional aluminium demand. | Reuters reports copper eased on worries about weak Chinese demand, but sentiment was supported by mine closures/disruptions suggesting tighter supplies and supply concerns around Panama’s Cobre mine. It also notes aluminium fell after LME warehouse stock jumps, then rebounded. Nickel dropped as other base metals moved with risk appetite. | Fastmarkets reported aluminium 6063 extrusion billet premiums easing in Europe, Spain, Thailand and Brazil, citing reduced liquidity, weak end-demand, and producers trying to clear inventories with discounted offers ahead of holidays. Copper prices were largely flat as traders stayed cautious ahead of a Bank of Japan decision and assessed US rate-cut prospects. Strong Chinese refined copper production and rising imports weighed on the Yangshan premium. Aluminium weakened on rising LME inventories and nickel also declined. | The article forecasts growth in the global aluminium powder market from about $1.84B in 2023 to $2.70B by 2030, citing rising demand from construction (brick production), solar cells, automotive, and aerospace/defense. It notes production methods, capacity expansions by players, and COVID-related supply chain disruptions. | Silver One Resources announced additional staking at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona to cover areas tied to magnetic anomalies along a 30+ km porphyry copper belt. Traders also cited uncertainty around First Quantum’s Cobre Panama and production guidance cuts from Anglo American. | The European Commission said the EU and US extended the suspension of EU “rebalancing” tariffs on certain US products, including aluminium, until 31 March 2025. The piece notes that aluminum has pulled back from a six-week high, with attention on LME inventory levels. A retreat suggests improving near-term supply conditions or reduced tightness expectations, leading traders to price in softer demand/supply balance for the coming days. | LS Cable & System says it developed “CuFlake,” a new raw material for copper foil that uses copper pieces instead of copper wire, potentially reducing processing steps and costs. The company completed sample testing and plans commercialization next year, with goals for higher copper-foil sales. It also targets aluminum EV components. | Marubeni, with Antofagasta, finalized investment in expanding Chile’s Centinela copper mine, including a new concentrator plant to double copper concentrate capacity. Total annual copper output is projected to rise by ~140,000 tonnes, increasing Marubeni’s equity copper volume by ~40,000 tonnes. Production starts in 2027. | Elval, an aluminium canstock/rolling division, highlighted at COP28 its push for higher circularity of aluminium beverage cans. The company supports policies to raise recycling rates, noting targets of up to 80% by 2030 and 100% by 2050, and claims increased recycled content metrics. | Mytilineos said the Hellenic Competition Commission approved its acquisition of Imerys Bauxites Greece, increasing its annual bauxite production to over 1.2 million tonnes and making it the largest EU producer. The company links the added bauxite to ensuring supply for its historic aluminium operations and gallium extraction activities. | Mytilineos plans to merge IMERYS Bauxites activity with its subsidiary Delphi Distomon to vertically integrate and expand bauxite production. The deal will make the group the largest bauxite producer in Europe, boosting output and capacity. More upstream supply from bauxite could reduce near-term aluminium tightness.

Norsk Hydro reportedly took a $571 million loss tied to a weak alumina market. Alumina weakness can indicate deteriorating refining economics and softer near-term aluminium production incentives, which typically weighs on expectations for aluminium prices in the coming days and weeks. | Reuters reports copper prices pulled back as bullish investors reduced positions ahead of the holidays after copper failed to hold above recent highs. Overall, the moves reflect short-term positioning and demand/supply expectations. | Norsk Hydro said it will record a 5.09 billion Norwegian krone ($495 million) impairment in Q4 after “challenging alumina market conditions,” and its shares dipped on the news. Shanghai aluminium (Feb) and zinc rose to November highs as markets priced potential further Chinese rate cuts and expected Red Sea shipping delays. Traders cited delayed refined aluminium shipments via Suez. The same session saw copper at multiweek highs and nickel higher, reflecting broad metal risk-on and logistics supply friction. | Chinese alumina futures on SHFE surged to record highs after an explosion in Guinea, a major bauxite supplier. Stockpiles may limit the short-term squeeze, but sentiment remains bullish for aluminium-related costs. | Reuters reports aluminium prices in London rose to the highest since Nov 6 on Friday, driven by supply concerns after a fuel depot blast in Guinea (key bauxite supplier) raised fears of bauxite shortages for Chinese alumina smelters. The piece highlights conflicting signals for China’s aluminum market: Golden World Innovation cut pay and furloughed staff amid the real estate-driven demand downturn, while other reports show rising primary aluminum production and revised higher output estimates. Shanghai prices were mixed, with copper little changed and aluminium slightly up. | Base metals rose in London on improved Chinese industrial profits. Aluminium hit an eight-month high on technical buying by CTAs and supply concerns after a Dec. 18 blast at Guinea’s main fuel terminal handling bauxite-related inputs. Copper and nickel also increased as the dollar eased and Indonesia-related supply focus strengthened.

Copper prices in London were steady near a five-month high as a softer U.S. dollar—driven by expectations for potential Fed interest rate cuts—supported dollar-priced metals. Three-month LME copper was almost unchanged while SHFE copper edged higher. LME aluminium was flat and nickel slightly lower, reflecting mixed but mostly mild macro effects. | Copper retreated from a five-month high as investors took profits ahead of year-end, with concerns about Chinese growth/property. Aluminium fell after LME inventories built to six-month highs. Nickel was the largest decliner as LME nickel inventories rose to levels not seen since July 2022. | Biden determined that specified volumes of eligible EU steel and aluminium articles imports will no longer threaten U.S. national security. London copper rose as the dollar weakened, while expectations of tighter copper concentrate supply and thin global inventories supported sentiment. London copper edged higher as China manufacturing improved and investors looked for fresh stimulus, while SHFE copper benefited from low warehouse inventories. Copper prices eased as the U.S. dollar firmed on reduced expectations for early, aggressive rate cuts and as Chinese buying slowed with seasonal weak demand and strong domestic output. Alcoa announced it will fully curtail production at its Kwinana alumina refinery in Western Australia starting in the second quarter, ending all alumina output by the third quarter of 2024. Pinjarra and Wagerup output are expected to remain unaffected. | Alcoa is expected to announce cost-driven curtailment at its Kwinana alumina refinery in Western Australia, though it is not a full closure and currently runs around 80% capacity. Reuters also reported LME aluminium and nickel edging higher in the same session. Investors awaited upcoming U.S. inflation data for further base-metals volatility. | Alcoa will fully curtail production at its Kwinana alumina refinery near Perth, starting in Q2 2024, with all alumina production ending by Q3 2024. Supplies are said not to be affected. | Minneapolis is testing five miles of aluminum wiring in about 125 streetlights to prevent copper thieves from stripping light posts. Alcoa announced it will shut its Kwinana alumina refinery in Western Australia, ending alumina production by Q3 and cutting jobs. While the port continues to handle related flows, the closure may tighten near-term alumina availability impacting aluminium expectations. | China’s November 2023 bauxite imports rose MoM and slightly YoY, led by higher shipments from Guinea as the rainy season ended. Copper prices edged higher, nearing the $10,000/ton resistance, supported by investment flows into funds. LME three-month copper was up about 1%, and SHFE June copper gained about 1.5%. GCC aluminium producers expect to gain market share after the US and UK banned Russian aluminium imports and restricted Russian trading on major exchanges, with similar measures for copper and nickel. Virgin Atlantic and easyJet are trialing low-carbon aluminium 5cl bottles, with Diageo launching 70cl aluminium Baileys bottles at airports. Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) hosted the Alumina Quality Workshop in Dubai, focusing on decarbonisation and sustainability. Copper rallied above $10,000/ton on LME as traders revived expectations of U.S. Fed rate cuts after softer labor-market data. Canada says it will not allow diverted Chinese steel and aluminium to enter as a “back door” after the U.S. announced tariff hikes on certain Chinese aluminium and steel products to 25%. China’s primary aluminium production rose 7.2% year-on-year to 3.58 million tonnes in April, according to China’s National Bureau of Statistics. ArcelorMittal claims VinFast lacks a license for VF 8 and other models. The piece provides no immediate ruling or ban.

Rio Tinto said it has declared force majeure on third-party alumina export contracts from Queensland refineries because restricted gas capacity at its Gladstone operations persists after a pipeline incident in early March. A Federal Register notice from BIS ends 12 general approved exclusions from U.S. Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum. The changes take effect July 1, 2024, potentially dampening aluminum demand. | Aluminum prices rose to a 23-month high as ongoing production issues in Australia reduce supply availability. The university says it is over six times stronger than traditional alloys and expects industrial use soon, which may modestly support future aluminum demand, but near-term price impact appears limited. | MX Holdings and Century Aluminum are considering Columbiana County for a $130 million high-tech secondary aluminum billet casting facility. The venture plans to use recycled scrap to produce low-carbon billets and aims for production in Q4 2026. Approval includes intent to reimburse expenditures via tax-exempt bonds. | Resources Minister Madeleine King warned that BHP’s underinvestment in ageing infrastructure could force thousands of workers into hardship, with BHP flagging it may close its Western Australian nickel operations. She linked the situation to Alcoa’s recent alumina refinery shutdown at Kwinana, citing similar consequences for supply. | Vedanta Aluminium, India’s largest aluminium producer, will not add new coal-fired power and is securing 1.3 GW of solar/wind renewables to raise renewable power use. The firm also plans capacity expansion to 3 million tonnes by 2026 and expects a bauxite mine environmental clearance, supporting output growth. | Prospect Ridge Resources announced new high-grade copper-bearing quartz vein showings on its Holy Grail property near the Copper Ridge zone, including samples up to ~14.8% Cu. The company plans to initiate drill holes on Copper Ridge in summer 2024. The release is exploration-focused and does not affect near-term supply materially. | The news notes Glencore will keep its Russian aluminum contract, but physical metal flows are becoming scarce. That suggests delivery disruptions and reduced near-term availability, which can tighten the aluminium market and support prices over the following days to weeks. | ElvalHalcor reported Q1’24 trading results, noting that both aluminium and copper metal prices averaged lower than in Q1’23. The company said revenue and segment profitability were pressured by the metal-price decline and subdued European demand. No new operational or regulatory catalyst for copper or aluminium prices was announced. | ElvalHalcor’s Q1’24 trading update links weaker financial results to lower LME benchmark metal prices and subdued industrial demand. It reports average aluminium and copper prices below the prior-year quarter, with reduced segment revenues and profitability. The company expects ongoing geopolitical and macro headwinds through 2024. | US policy and industry demand are pushing aluminium producers to decarbonize and standardize “low-carbon” definitions. DOE selected Century Aluminum for negotiations on $500m funding for a green smelter to potentially double capacity, while Fastmarkets launched low-carbon differentials. Curtailments from high power costs underscore the market impact. | Glencore and Rusal extended their long-term aluminium supply contract into 2025 despite previously low traded volumes. The extension is enabled because Rusal is not directly targeted by Western sanctions. The report also notes an LME ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel from April 13, reinforcing near-term supply-tight sentiment. | Cameroon exported 3,011 tons of aluminum sheets in 2023, up about 90.8% year-on-year, with revenue rising 80.2% to CFA6.6bn. The case proceeds via ITC preliminary injury review (June 28, 2024) followed by Commerce preliminary AD/CVD decisions, which could raise import costs.

Copper prices fell after hawkish Fed minutes supported a firmer U.S. dollar, making dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The drop was reinforced by weakening Chinese demand as earlier price gains reduced end-user consumption and lifted inventories. LME and SHFE aluminium and nickel also declined, reflecting broad risk-off pressure. | Washington will provide nearly $5 million to Kaiser Aluminum’s Spokane-area plant to replace two aging steam boilers under the state’s Climate Commitment Act. The funding offsets emissions-reduction costs and requires Kaiser to match the amount. The retrofit is expected to cut the plant’s annual greenhouse gases by about 13,000 metric tons. | Copper on the LME fell for a third straight session due to profit-taking and consumers shunning near-record prices. It notes elevated China inventories and a refined market surplus in March. The piece also shows aluminium and nickel easing on the session, alongside caution ahead of U.S. jobs data. | The USTR released a Federal Register notice outlining higher Section 301 tariffs on $18 billion of China-origin imports, with increases effective Aug. 1, 2024 (and later dates for 2025/2026). Copper prices were nearly unchanged as investors reassessed the rally that recently pushed LME prices to record highs, supported by fund buying and a U.S. Comex short squeeze but offset by dampened Chinese physical demand. LME aluminium and nickel fell slightly, indicating limited immediate directional impact beyond copper’s cooling momentum. | Hindalco Industries reported Q4 profit above analyst expectations, saying lower costs outweighed weak aluminium prices. The better earnings from the major India producer of both aluminium and copper supports margin resilience sentiment. The news may help underpin near-term expectations for aluminium pricing and, to a lesser extent, copper demand and production outlook. | Hindalco reported Q4 profit above expectations, saying lower costs helped offset weak aluminium prices. The result suggests current aluminium pricing remains soft, but the article is primarily about company earnings rather than a direct market shock. Copper is mentioned only as part of the producer mix, with no specific copper impact. | SMM says China’s alumina spot prices jumped on tight bauxite availability and rising refinery utilisation constraints. Demand rose as Yunnan restart of aluminium smelting increased alumina needs. Globally, outages at Rio Tinto/NALCO/Alcoa tightened supplies, pushing Western Australia FOB alumina higher and curtailing China imports. | Hindalco said India’s copper and aluminium demand should remain robust in FY25, citing rising electrification-led demand. It reported a 15% YoY increase in domestic refined copper demand in the latest quarter and higher copper and aluminium revenues/EBITDA, alongside favourable input costs, boosting sentiment toward both metals. | Hindalco’s management said Indian demand for refined copper rose 15% year-on-year to 225 KT in the latest quarter, helped by household electrification and other industrial uses. Gold and silver narratives are included, but copper receives direct coverage: prices hit an all-time high and are still supported by supply tightness. The article cites reduced copper surplus forecasts by the ICSG on lower production, plus mine-related output disruptions and planned cuts, supporting bullish expectations. | Gladiator Metals reports assay results from previously unassayed historic drill core at the Cowley Park prospect, extending and confirming high-grade copper skarn continuity (notably several meters grading around ~1–2% Cu) with mineralization open under cover and at depth. A global aluminium producer is seeking Q3 premium offers of $175/ton for July-September primary aluminium shipments to Japanese buyers, up about 18%-21% from the prior quarter.
