Gains were capped by a steady/firm U.S. dollar and higher Chinese inventories. Aluminium and nickel also ticked up on the broader metals sentiment. | Aluminium prices rose to a nearly two-year high on the LME as funds reportedly rotated from copper into aluminium. The move was supported by supply tightness, including alumina shortages from lower Chinese output and Australian export disruptions, alongside stronger physical demand shown by higher Japanese premiums for upcoming quarters. | Copper Lake Resources reported winter drilling results from its Marshall Lake copper-zinc-silver VMS property in Ontario, highlighting high-grade copper and zinc intercepts and a persistent EM conductor with the deposit remaining open at depth. The update supports potential resource expansion but does not indicate near-term production. | Aluminium prices rose to a nearly two-year high on LME, driven by supply tightness and funds switching from copper. Reuters cites tighter alumina availability from weaker China output and Australian export disruptions, alongside higher Japanese booking premiums, indicating firmer physical demand and continued fund inflows. | A new Aluminum Association/ICF roadmap discusses how North American aluminum can cut production emissions via low-carbon primary tech, electrification and clean power, and expanded recycling, enabled by policy such as the Inflation Reduction Act. Shanghai aluminium jumped about 3% to a two-year high, with LME aluminium also near multi-year peaks. The report attributes the rally to strong China demand for aluminium used in solar and EVs, alumina supply disruptions, and higher premiums for primary metal shipments, supporting immediate bullish price pressure. | Aluminium is reported to be at a two-year high as China enforces or tightens capacity limits. The headline suggests these restrictions reduce nearby supply, lifting market expectations and supporting aluminium prices in the near term. | Shanghai aluminium hit a two-year high on early trade, supported by supply constraints linked to western sanctions on Russian metals, tight availability (including higher premiums to Japan), and robust demand from solar and EV sectors. Meanwhile, copper and nickel slipped as a firmer U.S. dollar weighed on base metals priced in dollars. | Norsk Hydro says it has started delivering power from onsite solar panels and battery storage at its aluminium plants in Sweden, with rooftop and ground-mounted PV plus 4.5 MW of batteries. The move may lift expectations for aluminium industry investment. | Aluminum prices reportedly reached a two-year high as Chinese smelters operated under output limits. The restriction on production tightens available supply in the near term, typically lifting spot prices. The move suggests market expectations for aluminium availability have worsened over the next days to weeks. | New Zealand Aluminium Smelters secured 20-year electricity arrangements totaling 572 MW to power the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter, with possible demand-response curtailments of up to 185 MW. Contracts start July 2024, supporting stable, competitive low-carbon aluminium output. Rio Tinto also moves to full ownership of NZAS. | Meridian, Mercury, Contact Energy and New Zealand Aluminium Smelters have signed a new electricity agreement, securing operation of the Tiwai Point aluminium smelter for 20 years after 18 months of talks. Local officials described the decision as major economic support, implying more stable aluminium output. | New Zealand’s Tiwai Point aluminium smelter (NZAS/Rio Tinto) secured a fixed-price power contract with Meridian Energy through 2044, ending uncertainty about shutdown after this year. The deal supports ongoing low-carbon aluminium output and includes demand-response provisions, which can improve power system reliability. | Rio says it will keep its New Zealand aluminum smelter running after power deals secured electricity on workable terms. The update lowers the near-term risk of shutdowns or reduced output tied to power costs, supporting aluminum supply expectations and likely providing modest upward price pressure for the metal. | Rio Tinto said it will keep its New Zealand aluminium plant operating for 20 more years. The extension improves long-term supply certainty for aluminium, but because the horizon is far beyond the next few weeks, near-term price impact is likely limited. | New Zealand’s Tiwai Point aluminium smelter is secured for the next 20 years after Meridian, Mercury, Contact Energy and New Zealand Aluminium Smelters signed a deal following 18 months of negotiations. Rio Tinto says the long-term future of the smelter is secured, and it will buy remaining minority stakes in NZAS after the deal. | Rio Tinto agreed to buy Sumitomo Chemical’s stake to become sole owner of New Zealand Aluminium Smelters, which runs the Tiwai aluminium smelter. Sumitomo Chemical said it will sell its shares in New Zealand Aluminium Smelters and Boyne Smelters to Rio Tinto, after required approvals. The report says base metals traded lower. It also mentions Rio Tinto keeping a New Zealand aluminium smelter running with new electricity supply agreements, but overall the tone is bearish for industrial metals. | Copper rebounded on the LME, but gains were capped by profit-taking after May 20 record highs and by weaker physical demand in China. China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50. Aluminium and nickel also rose on the day, reflecting broader market moves rather than a single new metal-specific shock. | Reuters reports copper under pressure after China’s May manufacturing PMI fell below 50, worsening demand expectations for the top consumer. Despite earlier speculative gains and a modest monthly rise, profit-taking could push copper back below $10,000. LME aluminium eased, nickel edged up slightly.  | City of Bakersfield reports an uptick in copper wire theft from streetlights, leaving areas dark and creating repair costs far exceeding scrap value. Iron ore and limestone also increased, indicating firmer activity in metals-linked user sectors, but aluminium output is the only directly cited tracked-metal driver.

London copper rose 1.1% on supportive China and US data: China’s private manufacturing survey showed the fastest activity growth in about two years, and US inflation stabilization kept the Fed’s planned rate cuts on track. A tighter China copper import discount suggested improved physical demand, lifting copper and supporting aluminium and nickel. | Copper prices rose after stronger-than-expected Chinese factory activity and U.S. inflation data that revived hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts. The project is backed by state tax incentives tied to an $85 million investment and jobs targets. Completion is scheduled for 2026.

Copper prices fell below $10,000 per tonne on the LME, down 1.5%, driven by weak demand expectations from China and persistently high Shanghai physical inventories. Softer U.S. data and rate-cut expectations supported a stronger dollar, adding near-term pressure. Aluminium and nickel also edged lower. | BEAC’s CEMAC report says mineral export prices fell 2.6% QoQ in March 2023, mainly due to weaker iron and manganese prices. Copper prices fell after a strong rally as weak physical demand in China weighed on the market. LME three-month copper slipped below $10,000 and SHFE July copper dropped, while SHFE and LME copper stocks rose. Aluminium and nickel also eased, suggesting broadly bearish sentiment in base metals. | Copper prices near a one-month low as real demand fails to match strong electrification-driven expectations. Aluminium was also down on the day; nickel fell alongside broader metals weakness. | Russian aluminium producer Rusal is in talks with Sierra Leone’s government about securing a bauxite mining concession. High energy costs and weak end-demand (including construction and EV funding cancellations) worsen pressure, suggesting softer near-term aluminium pricing. | Chinese researchers say they developed nanoparticles-enhanced, oxide-dispersion-strengthened aluminum alloys with markedly improved heat resistance beyond typical performance limits. The piece also discusses China’s shift toward greener electricity for aluminum smelting.

Copper futures rebounded above $10,000/t as the dollar softened on expectations of Fed easing and as signs of firmer China demand emerged. LME three-month copper rose 1.6%. LME aluminum and nickel also advanced, with Reuters citing renewed copper buying activity in China. | Silver One says it found additional copper and silver showings at its Phoenix Silver Project in Arizona, with select samples up to 7.79% Cu. Gains were capped by concerns over sluggish demand, especially in China where inventories rose and producer operating rates fell. LME aluminium and nickel also rose.

London copper eased as softer-than-expected physical demand from China weighed on prices. Markets also awaited U.S. jobs data for guidance on when Fed rate cuts may begin, lifting expectations of cuts after recent labor-market cooling signals. Other LME metals, including aluminium and nickel, were modestly higher. | China’s May unwrought copper imports rose 15.8% year-on-year to 514,000 tonnes, beating expectations despite weak physical consumption. Imports were up 17.4% from April. Analysts cited potential additional Russia-origin flows into China following Western sanctions in April, with DRC shipments also improving. | The article argues that proposed EU sanctions on Russian aluminium would damage Europe’s industrial supply chain. The author warns restrictions would raise prices and inflation. | London copper fell after mixed China trade data. Better exports contrasted with slower imports and higher-than-expected unwrought copper imports, raising concern that Chinese inventories will grow; SHFE stocks hit a four-year high. Broader macro cues from an upcoming US jobs report and Fed cut expectations also influenced pricing; aluminium and nickel moved mildly. | Copper on the LME is poised for a third weekly decline, breaking below $10,000/t after mixed Chinese trade data. Better exports but slower import growth and higher unwrought copper imports raise concerns about rising inventories. Traders also await a U.S. jobs report for rate-cut timing, weighing on growth-linked metals. | Guinea signed a term sheet with a subsidiary of Emirates Global Aluminium to build a 2 million tpy alumina refinery, aiming for initial production of about 1.2 million tpy by September 2026. The deal targets local bauxite refining and includes a partnership with Chinalco, but is not near-term supply. | Guinea signed a term sheet with Emirates Global Aluminium’s subsidiary to build a second alumina refinery in Guinea with about 2 million mt capacity by September 2026. Such upgrades can alter near-term aluminium supply via maintenance timing, refurbishment schedules, and potential efficiency improvements. The article lacks specifics on project timing or capacity impact, so the immediate price direction is uncertain.

Copper fell to its lowest in over five weeks on a firm dollar and shifting expectations for Fed rate cuts after stronger U.S. jobs data. Physical demand constraints were noted, including a continued discount in China import premiums. Aluminium declined about 1%; nickel was nearly unchanged. | The piece describes ventilated aluminium wall cladding systems for data centres, emphasizing perforated aluminium façades that aid server cooling and energy savings. Traders expected support from China returning from holiday and cited better China PMI activity, while concerns remain from rising warehouse inventories and weaker Yangshan premium. Aluminium and nickel saw only modest moves. | Guinea Alumina Corporation signed a term sheet with Guinea’s government to develop a 1mt/year alumina refinery near Boké. The project is linked to EGA and involves a government equity stake and board appointments. The refinery would expand refining capacity for bauxite feedstock, supportive for aluminium supply expectations. | Copper rebounded 0.6% after dipping to the lowest since May 2, supported by shifting focus to improving fundamentals and expected Tuesday buying as China returns from holiday. The prior sell-off was triggered by strong US jobs data and a stronger dollar. China stocks remain high and premiums weak. Aluminium and nickel saw modest declines/gains. | Power Nickel begins a 2024 summer drilling program of 20 holes and over 8,000 metres at its Nisk property in Quebec, targeting extensions of high-grade polymetallic sulphide lenses. The program focuses on both the polymetallic Lion Zone (PGE/Pd/Au/Ag/Cu) and nickel-rich Nisk Main. No production or shipments are mentioned. | The article headline says the LME aluminum delivery queue increased to around five months after Trafigura made a delivery. The piece links higher copper demand to electrification and data centers, while noting some aluminum smelters have reduced output due to higher electricity prices, boosting copper’s relative appeal. | A project team under Opaz and other agencies conducted a field visit in Sohar Industrial City to plan an integrated economic cluster and an “Aluminum Industry Complex.” They met Sohar Aluminium management and downstream factories to identify challenges and investment opportunities, aiming to attract new investment and strengthen Oman’s aluminium production hub. | Rio Tinto agreed to buy Mitsubishi’s 11.65% stake in Boyne Smelters Ltd, operator of the Boyne Island aluminium smelter in Australia, and noted it will also raise its stake via a separate Sumitomo purchase. Kaiser Aluminum confirmed it will close its Sherman, Texas facility after 60 years, with production expected to stop by June 30 due to business challenges. Impacted customers will be redirected to other Kaiser sites. The closure affects about 75 employees and may reduce near-term aluminium output. | Base metals fell as the yuan weakened, reducing Chinese buying power. LME/SHFE copper dropped, while aluminium eased amid high inventories, weak physical demand, and a wider LME cash-to-three-month discount reflecting non-tight nearby supply. Nickel moved less consistently, amid broader downside pressure. | Copper prices hit a seven-week low as the weaker yuan reduced purchasing power for China and as LME copper inventories rose after fresh inflows. The piece also describes a contango structure with no imminent shortages, while aluminium and nickel fell amid broader base-metal pressure. | Alcoa reported progress toward completing its acquisition of Australia-based Alumina Limited, including filing the definitive proxy, obtaining Brazil’s CADE approval, and receiving indications from Australia’s ACCC. Shareholder and court hearings are scheduled through late July, targeting closing around Aug. 1, subject to approvals. | The U.S. added three more companies to an entity list tied to the Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act, including electrolytic aluminium producer Xinjiang Shenhuo Coal and Electricity Co. Warehouse arbitrage and surplus conditions are implied by wider contango spreads and ongoing stock churn.

The piece reviews copper’s pullback after a sharp rally, citing prior drivers like better-than-expected growth, short covering, and mine disruption in Panama, while projecting a near-term range and medium-term tightening. It also notes strong ETF inflows and discusses copper ETF options, with brief substitution to aluminium if prices spike. | Copper rebounded as the dollar steadied ahead of U.S. CPI and the Fed decision. Traders cited expectations of a dovish statement and lower currency pressure supporting dollar-priced commodities. LME copper rose 0.8% from recent lows, while aluminium was slightly lower and nickel flat, reflecting broad industrial-metal reaction. | DHS added three PRC entities to the UFLPA enforcement list, effective June 12, under which CBP applies a rebuttable presumption that the listed entities’ goods are barred from U.S. import. One entity produces electrolytic aluminum in Xinjiang, raising forced-labor compliance risk and potential supply tightness for aluminum imports. | Norilsk Nickel says sanctions have cut revenues and disrupted payments, deliveries and pricing, limiting copper plant sulfur-equipment upgrades. No production volumes, inventories, or near-term supply changes are reported. | Alcoa has closed its all-stock acquisition of Alumina, expanding its upstream bauxite and alumina position and gaining full control of the AWAC JV. AWAC holds bauxite mines, alumina refineries and a smelter stake. The deal is framed as value creation and operational flexibility, implying limited immediate pricing impact for aluminium.

London copper edged up 0.2% after prior sell-offs, as some dip-buying emerged, but global demand concerns capped upside. The U.S. jobs report and weaker China activity fueled recession worries while expectations of Fed cuts supported industrial demand. LME aluminium and nickel rose slightly, showing mixed sentiment. | Copper prices tumbled as worsening China and U.S. demand outlooks sparked a sell-off. Weaker U.S. jobs data and technology earnings reinforced recession fears, while rising LME warehouse inventories weighed near term. The risk-off move also pushed aluminium and nickel modestly lower alongside copper. | Copper fell sharply (up to 3.8% on the LME) as global financial contagion triggered a risk-off rush to cash out and turn bearish amid fears of a US recession. The selloff pressured other industrial raw-materials, reflecting weaker growth expectations and tighter financial conditions.

Kalamazoo, Michigan welcomed a new eco-friendly JP Processing Center from Schupan to recycle obsolete aluminum into usable raw material. The company says the process cuts carbon footprint and is more efficient and cost effective. Output is aimed at green aluminum products used in items like pop cans and home siding. | Vedanta reported Q1 profit above estimates, citing higher global prices—especially aluminium and zinc—amid supply worries. The firm said pre-tax profit at its aluminium unit rose 14%, with aluminium contributing about one-third of group profit and revenue. The update reinforces short-term positive sentiment for aluminium prices. | Las Vegas officials are proposing tighter controls on scrap-metal sales and purchases to curb copper theft. The city has already replaced targeted street lighting wiring, including with aluminum-based wiring, but theft persists into wider areas. Penalties include fines and possible jail, with a council discussion scheduled for Aug. 7. | The White House announced that some Mexican aluminium imports into the US will lose Section 232 tariff exemptions unless importers provide proof that first/second smelter and casting are not China, Russia, Belarus or Iran. Affected goods face a 10% tariff (and higher for already sanctioned material), increasing compliance uncertainty.

London copper fell 0.5% to a more than four-and-a-half month low as a firm dollar and looming U.S. recession fears pressured prices, alongside weak China demand concerns. LME aluminium also slipped 0.3% and nickel fell 0.5%, while Shanghai contracts were mixed, reflecting catch-up activity. | Reuters reports LME three-month copper fell 0.5% to a four-and-a-half-month low due to a firm dollar and worries about U.S. and Chinese growth. Shanghai copper rose 0.3% as higher prices spurred Chinese exports and narrowing spreads attracted cargoes. LME aluminium and nickel slipped slightly while SHFE aluminium rose and nickel edged down. | South-west WA residents and groups oppose South32’s Worsley Alumina bauxite project expansion, citing alleged environmental harm, changing hydrology, dust mitigation water use, and inadequate consultation. Over 130 appeals were lodged against the expansion’s approval, creating potential near-term uncertainty around operating and expansion timelines. | ESG-focused coverage includes Macquarie’s notes that several nickel mines are being suspended/closed amid weak pricing, which may tighten near-term supply. It also reports South32 is appealing WA EPA conditions at its Worsley alumina mine, potentially influencing aluminium supply/costs. Other sections are largely macro/energy/ESG themes. | SMM reports China’s aluminium output rose in July as Yunnan smelters resumed full production and additional capacity came on in Inner Mongolia and Sichuan. It expects August operating capacity and output to keep increasing, with aluminium liquid proportion recovering toward 73%, suggesting higher supply despite improving downstream demand. | Alba (Aluminium Bahrain) and Daiki Aluminium sign an MoU to establish a sustainable aluminium dross processing business in Bahrain. The facility will use state-of-the-art technology to recover aluminium metal from dross, lowering waste and supporting circular-economy and ESG goals. The move modestly strengthens near-term aluminium supply sentiment. | AMAG Austria Metall AG expands its AMAG AL4® ever portfolio by adding certified low-carbon primary aluminium from its Alouette smelter in Canada. The smelter uses exclusively renewable hydropower, limiting energy-related CO2 and targeting up to 4.0 t CO2 per tonne of aluminium. A certificate verifies Scope 1–3 footprints. | Alba, Bahrain’s major aluminium smelter, and Japan’s Daiki Aluminium have formed a strategic partnership to set up a sustainable aluminium dross processing business in Bahrain. The initiative focuses on recycling dross into usable materials, potentially improving secondary metal supply and supporting aluminium availability expectations. | Alba and Japan’s Daiki Aluminium Industry have signed an MoU to establish a sustainable aluminium dross processing business in Bahrain. The facility will use state-of-the-art technology to recover aluminium metal, reduce waste, and support ESG and circular-economy goals. This could incrementally tighten aluminium supply expectations.

Copper edged up slightly on a softer U.S. dollar, but broader sentiment is weak as LME inventories jumped and global growth expectations deteriorated. Commentary notes rising TC/RCs suggest improving concentrate supply. Aluminium and nickel prices were also lower in the same session, indicating mixed but generally pressured industrial metals. | Copper prices steadied but remained under pressure as pessimism on global growth and risk-off sentiment weighed on base metals. LME copper inventories surged nearly threefold to 294,750 tons. Aluminium and nickel also fell on the day, reflecting weaker demand expectations outweighing improving spot refining charges. | Golden Arrow reported new step-out drill intercepts at its San Pietro IOCG project in Chile, including long intervals grading about 0.43–0.44% Cu with associated gold and cobalt. The release is an exploration update with no production or reserve change stated, so any near-term effect on copper prices is likely limited. | Traders expect improved Japanese automotive demand to support aluminium in the latter half of 2024 after Toyota certification and recall-related production suspensions. Toyota models resume in September following compliance confirmation, while Japan Aluminium Association data shows aluminium usage in transportation. Fastmarkets also moves the Japan spot premium publication to daily to improve liquidity. | Canadian steel and aluminum producers are urging Ottawa to impose tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum, arguing current measures have not stopped dumping and market overcapacity. The push follows recent U.S. tariff increases on Chinese metals and concerns Canada could become a trade backdoor via Mexico, risking job losses and potential price pressure in aluminum markets. | Aluminum producers are pressing Canada’s Trudeau government to act on China-related tariffs. The move would likely raise the cost of importing Chinese aluminum into Canada, potentially reducing near-term supply and supporting domestic prices as markets price in a stronger tariff-driven import barrier. | Meviy (MISUMI Group) said it will cut the sales price of its CNC milling aluminum products by 30% effective immediately, covering aluminum alloys 6061, 5052, and 2017 with no surface treatment. The move targets lower customer costs and competitive pricing, likely signaling local pricing pressure on aluminium products rather than metal prices broadly.

Mitsui underwrites a capital increase in Nippon Amazon Aluminium (NAAC), raising its stake in the Albras smelter joint venture to about 46% (direct and indirect). The deal also increases annual offtake of primary aluminium ingots from ~80,000 to ~140,000 tons, strengthening green aluminium supply access. | Canadian steel and aluminum CEOs urge Ottawa to impose tariffs on China to prevent North America from becoming a “dumping ground” for Chinese overcapacity. They seek trade protection aligned with the U.S. and Mexico. If implemented, reduced Chinese import pressure could support aluminium pricing in the near term. | Copper rose Friday after U.S. jobless claims came in better than expected, easing recession-demand fears and supporting rate-cut optimism. It still faces a fifth straight weekly drop, with LME inventories at a high since 2019 limiting upside catalysts. Aluminium and nickel also climbed amid the broader rebound. | Mitsui increased its stake in Nippon Amazon Aluminium (NAAC) to expand purchases of low-carbon aluminum ingots from Brazil’s Albras. Offtake rises to 140,000 tonnes/year from 80,000 tonnes/year, mainly for Japanese buyers. NAAC/Albras use renewable power, and Mitsui expects tight low-carbon supply and rising demand. | Ormonde notes Peak Nickel has resumed a 2024 drill programme at its Rodburn Ni-Cu-Co project in Scotland, targeting further definition and potential expansion of mineralization. The announcement includes Ni, Cu and Co grades used in Ni-equivalent assumptions, but provides no new price-driving results or production impacts. | Pierre Poilievre said he would implement tariffs on Chinese exports, including aluminum, alongside electric vehicles and steel, citing protection of Canadian jobs from “economic predation” by Beijing. He made the remarks at the Stelco steel plant, arguing tariffs would encourage new mining and steel investment. | Technavio projects growth in the coal tar pitch market from 2024–2028, citing increasing use in primary aluminum production. Coal tar pitch is used as a binder/anode component in the Hall-Héroult process and electrode demand. Regulations limiting coal tar pitch due to PAHs are noted as a constraint, limiting clear short-term aluminum price impact.

Copper prices opened the week softer as investors weighed ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited data for demand signals. Reuters cites economic worries including recession fears, with copper down on both LME and Shanghai. Nickel also slipped, while aluminium was steadier, reflecting cautious industrial demand sentiment. | Thai police and industrial works officials seized about 14,400 tonnes of hazardous aluminium dross at two smelting-related sites near Bangkok after stopping an unauthorized truck. Authorities found hot aluminium bars and illegal operations, including a prior April closure order that was allegedly ignored. Possible production disruption could support near-term aluminium prices. | Copper prices rose as traders covered shorts and looked to upcoming China and U.S. data for demand and rate-cut timing. LME copper was up about 0.9%, with cancelled warrants increasing and warehouse inventories still elevated. The report also said aluminium and nickel climbed on the same risk-on backdrop. | NALCO reported a 76% jump in Q1 profit, attributing gains to lower costs (coal and bauxite) and higher global aluminium prices. Reuters notes base-metal prices rose sharply on supply concerns and stronger demand, while domestic aluminium demand is expected to stay steady from power and construction sectors. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share in LME-approved warehouses rose to 65% in July (from 50% in June), while Indian-origin fell. Total on-warrant aluminium stocks fell 23% in July. Russian copper and nickel shares declined. The piece also references the LME’s earlier ban on sanctioned Russian output. | LME data show Russian-origin aluminium’s share of on-warrant stocks rose to 65% in July while Indian-origin fell to 33%, alongside a 23% decline in total on-warrant aluminium stocks. The May Russian ban remains cited, with similar share changes for copper and nickel inventories. | Nickel prices continued to plunge through July, with early August stabilizing near early-2024 levels. Stainless steel distributors report weak demand, and lower nickel-driven stainless surcharges reduce buyer incentives. U.S. manufacturing PMI stays in contraction, reinforcing soft end-use conditions. Outokumpu sees growth QoQ but still down YoY.

Reuters reports copper prices fell as LME inventories climbed to near five-year highs and China demand remained sluggish. Traders cited exports of surplus copper into Asian LME warehouses and weaker Chinese macro data. Purchases from some physical consumers and hopes of a BHP Escondida strike limited losses, but overall sentiment stayed bearish. | The U.S. issued proclamations modifying Section 232 trade treatment for certain steel and aluminum products from Mexico. For aluminum, importers must document smelt/cast countries; otherwise an additional 10% duty applies. This raises costs for some Mexico-origin aluminum shipments, likely reducing near-term demand and weighing on aluminum prices.

Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) says it has shipped bauxite from Guinea to China on an LNG-fuelled, dual-fuel capesize vessel, aiming to cut transport emissions versus traditional marine fuel. Bauxite is the feedstock for aluminium, but the news does not indicate changes to bauxite volumes or aluminium production schedules.

Rio Tinto signed an agreement with Queensland to safeguard its Boyne aluminum smelter in Gladstone, backed by the state Energy and Jobs Plan as the plant shifts to renewable power. Queensland support from 2029 and Rio’s investment to keep capacity above 500,000 tonnes, plus more bauxite shipping, bolster aluminum supply expectations. | China’s July primary aluminium output rose 6% to 3.68m tonnes, the highest monthly total in over 20 years, as smelters ramped up due to still-profitable operations. While prices recently fell and demand stayed weak, analysts expect profits to remain steady and output to rise further, weighing on aluminium sentiment. | Nevada’s NDOT restored streetlights on US 95 after theft caused outages; officials said stolen copper wiring was removed and the repairs were bundled into ongoing road work. Crews also added protective lids and security measures. The new LED lighting is more energy efficient and requires less maintenance, but the impact appears localized. | Queensland partnered with Rio Tinto to secure the future of the Boyne/B2L aluminium smelter (BSL) and support renewable energy and grid upgrades. Economic support is set to safeguard the smelter’s financial viability and power contracting for decarbonised, green aluminium production, supporting supply continuity into the coming years.

Copper prices are set for their first weekly gain in six, supported by mine supply worries after a strike at BHP’s Escondida in Chile and stronger-than-feared U.S. retail sales and jobs data easing recession fears. Aluminium and nickel also ticked higher, reflecting improved base-metals sentiment. | Fastmarkets says European automotive aluminium demand is weakening as cost-of-living pressures keep consumers cautious and vehicle output remains depressed. Primary foundry alloy premiums (PFA), mainly used in car parts, have fallen to $500-520/t in Germany. Vehicle sales and production declines and postponements reinforce bearish near-term sentiment. | Flying Nickel Mining completed its merger, acquiring 100% of Nevada Vanadium, creating a wholly owned subsidiary. The company is primarily focused on advancing its Minago nickel project and a vanadium project. The corporate combination may affect longer-term sentiment, but no near-term production or supply changes are stated.

White Cliff Minerals announced additional rock-chip assay results from its Great Bear Project in Northern Canada, including multiple high-grade copper grades (up to 42.2% Cu) alongside gold and silver. The findings suggest expanded IOCG/epithermal copper potential, but they are early exploration data and unlikely to materially affect near-term copper pricing. | Copper prices rose as U.S. recession fears eased after strong U.S. data and China’s copper inventories fell. LME three-month copper climbed 0.9% and SHFE September gained 0.5%. The report also cites easing supply concerns at BHP’s Escondida after a wage deal, alongside gains in aluminium and a modest nickel rise. | Queensland will partner with Rio Tinto to secure the Boyne Island aluminium smelter’s long-term operation, including full-capacity requirements until end-2040 and renewable energy support to protect competitiveness. The deal also includes demand response to reduce electricity demand during tight supply. Overall, it supports aluminium supply stability. | Copper prices climbed to a 2-1/2 week high as signs of improving demand in China encouraged physical buyers and a sliding dollar made dollar-priced metal cheaper. ShFE copper stocks were at five-month lows, while LME inventory overhang remained. Aluminium also rose on declining LME stocks and cancelled warrants. | Australia’s FMIA bill will fund “critical minerals” but excludes bauxite from the Critical Minerals List, though aluminium is treated as a strategic/green material. The Australian Aluminium Council argues this omission may weaken investment in the bauxite–alumina pathway. The piece highlights competitive pressure and energy costs for mining and refining. | Aluminum prices have continued to slide, with monthly declines after a brief mid-year stabilization. The piece highlights bearish factors including rising Chinese primary output, large inventories, wide contango versus spot, tepid Chinese demand, and investment funds cutting longs and adding shorts—together weighing on near-term aluminum sentiment.

London LME three-month copper fell 0.4% as the previous rally was attributed to short-covering rather than new demand. Open interest declined and analysts noted limited contract rollover. Chinese physical demand improved slightly, and an Escondida strike was averted, easing supply fears. LME aluminium and nickel also declined. | Aluminium rose to a five-week high on the LME as fund buying increased and China faced a shortage of raw material (alumina). Three-month aluminium touched about $2,482/ton, the highest since mid-July, and last traded around $2,470, up roughly 1%. | Falcon Gold reports delays and permitting for drill access at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland, with roadwork expected to finish by week-end. The property is contiguous to Benton/Homeland Nickel’s Great Burnt Copper Zone. Reported past and recent drilling shows high copper grades, but no immediate production impact. | Thailand’s Foreign Trade Department is probing and has issued an affirmative preliminary determination in an anti-dumping case against Chinese aluminium extrusions, prompted by complaints of import surges harming domestic producers. If dumping is confirmed, anti-dumping duties could be imposed for five years, potentially tightening supply and supporting prices.

Thailand’s Department of Foreign Trade opened a nine-month anti-dumping investigation into Chinese extruded aluminium after local producers complained of injury from imports. The authorities may impose duties for five years if dumping is confirmed and can extend measures if damage persists, potentially tightening supply.

Hindalco and Novelis announced a $10 billion investment plan to expand aluminium production and the recycling value chain. Hindalco plans a major Aditya smelter and flat-rolled products expansion in Odisha and a new alumina refinery at Rayagada. Novelis will add rolling and recycling capacity in the US and expand UBC recycling in the UK.

DRC authorities have halted or recommended stopping Chemaf Resources’s planned sale of its copper-cobalt mines to China’s Norinco, citing violations of Gecamines’ lease and farm-out contract terms. The dispute may disrupt near-term copper supply expectations from the DRC, though the final outcome is uncertain. | South Pacific Metals reports exceptional surface trench assay results at its Osena gold-copper Osena project in Papua New Guinea, including 79 m averaging ~981 ppm Cu and localized higher copper grades. The findings support potential expansion of a porphyry system, but are early-stage exploration with limited near-term impact on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium. | Chinese government departments criticized Canada’s announcement of additional tariffs and other restrictive measures against Chinese exports, including electric vehicles, steel and aluminium. They called it protectionism and urged Canada to reverse the measures. The tariff threat is likely to disrupt aluminium trade expectations and pressure aluminium prices near term.

The piece argues that despite ample primary aluminium supply signals (declining inventories, rising Chinese production), physical delivery premiums keep climbing. It attributes the move to sanctions-related deliverability issues (Russian supply) and cost/congestion constraints: higher freight rates, tight container capacity, Suez traffic disruptions, and port strikes in Germany delaying shipments into Europe and Japan. | Los Angeles police conducted a crackdown on copper wire thefts, recovering 16,000 pounds of copper and arresting/fining recyclers. Officials also mention reclaimed aluminum cable and batteries. The enforcement targets illegal recyclers and upstream sourcing to prevent street lighting and service disruptions. More arrests are expected. | Anglesey Mining says it has submitted an initial Environmental Impact Assessment Scoping Report to restart underground mining at Parys Mountain, historically a major copper producer. The plan would process concentrate (~30% copper) for export, but requires significant investment and further approvals, making near-term copper impact uncertain. | Glencore plans to end copper mining at Mount Isa in 2025, with the smelter’s operation dependent on government support until 2030. As other nearby projects likely won’t reach production until after 2026, the closure raises near-term concerns about copper supply and smelting throughput. Local workforce and investment risks are emphasized.

Rusal reported a 41.6% jump in first-half profit as it increased production of primary aluminium and alumina and boosted shipments to China to record levels. It also notes the LME broad ban on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel amid U.S./UK sanctions. Improved volumes and rerouting are mildly supportive for prices. | Rusal’s first-half adjusted net profit rose 41.6% as it increased production and shipped record volumes to China. The firm faced demand pressure as Western buyers avoid Russia-made metal after Ukraine. The article notes April LME bans on Russian aluminium, copper and nickel and links improved output to higher prices for aluminium and alumina. | Corella Resources discusses government funding for R&D aimed at improving high-purity alumina (HPA) flowsheeting from kaolin, including reducing high-temperature calcination to lower energy intensity. The work is expected to take about six months, but the update is primarily technical and early-stage, with no near-term supply change stated. | WA EPA has set a deadline for Alcoa to submit information required for bauxite mining approvals in the jarrah forest, warning it could take a cautious approach or impose stricter conditions if information is not provided. Water and environmental agencies opposed the plans, citing risks to drinking water and ecosystems. | Queensland is considering an extra $1.2 billion for the CopperString high-voltage transmission line after costs rose to an estimated $6.2 billion. The project is expected to support mining development and help supply cheaper power, likely boosting near-term expectations for grid metals, especially copper, with possible spillover to aluminium. | An explosion at an aluminium smelter in Inner Mongolia disrupted three aluminium electrolytic cells. The company resolved the issue quickly, and the affected tanks resumed production that afternoon. The update characterizes the production disruption as minimal, suggesting limited near-term impact on aluminium supply expectations. | The article reports that aluminium is leading base metals lower as market conditions point to a weaker supply-and-demand balance. This suggests reduced support from physical or demand fundamentals, contributing to downward price momentum in aluminium relative to other base metals over the near term. | Argentina’s minister says the pro-business mining incentive regime (RIGI) should attract major foreign investment and help new lithium and copper projects start later this year. Copper is framed as crucial for EVs and AI, with large capex needs for projects, implying improved near-term supply/export expectations.  | Times of Oman reports Omani project “Greenlium,” using lignin extracted from palm waste to convert commercial aluminum into a more sustainable “organic” aluminum. It claims lower environmental impact and potential applications in packaging and electronics. The article does not indicate commercial scale or near-term supply/cost changes for aluminum prices. | US steel and aluminium groups and the UAW urged the USTR to expand Section 301 tariff rates, including proposals to target third-country imports that incorporate Chinese-origin aluminium. The Aluminum Association argues current trade diversion undermines US producers, seeking added tariff coverage. USTR reviews comments for a final decision in August.

Canada’s Department of Finance announced 100% tariffs on Chinese-made EVs and a 25% tariff on certain steel and aluminum products from China, with measures starting in October. The aluminum tariff can tighten supply from China into Canada, offering near-term support to aluminium prices. | The Overnight Report links overnight weakness in industrial metals to a stronger USD and specific aluminium factors. It says aluminium led metals lower as worries persist over China’s demand recovery, while ample supply grew. Rusal’s record first-half aluminium shipments lifted China inventories to their highest levels since 2019. | Unite union workers at Alvance Aluminium’s Lochaber smelter near Fort William agreed a two-year pay rise above inflation, including 5%/4% increases in 2024 and additional scheduled raises in 2025. The deal covers smelter and administrative staff and follows no reported production disruption; a recycling and billet project is planned. | U.S. Trade Representative Katherine Tai said Canada’s new high tariffs on some Chinese imports are an important step for fair competition in electric vehicles and the steel and aluminum industries. By targeting Chinese aluminum imports, the measure is likely to tighten supply and support near-term aluminum prices.

Tindo partnered with Capral Aluminium to supply extruded aluminium frames for Australian-made solar panels, with a pathway to sourcing from Capral’s domestic facilities as Tindo scales output toward 1GW. The collaboration is aimed at strengthening Australia’s PV supply chain and could increase near-term aluminium demand.

The White House issued updated Section 232 proclamations covering aluminum imports. New “smelt and most recent cast” origin rules for Mexico-linked aluminum and higher duties apply when smelt/cast is reported in China or other listed countries, raising compliance costs and reducing duty-free inflows into the US. | US steel and aluminum groups and the Aluminum Association urged USTR to expand proposed Section 301 tariff rates to cover Chinese-origin aluminium embedded in third-country products re-exported to the US. AISI also sought broader steel rules of origin. USTR will finalize changes in August, taking effect shortly after publication. | Alpha HPA began stage two construction at its Gladstone, Queensland high-purity alumina facility, building on stage one and planning over 10,000 tonnes annually of high-purity aluminium products. The expansion is funded with government debt and additional equity and targets electronics for the energy transition, supporting aluminium supply expectations. | Fastmarkets launched new FOB “alumina index inferred” assessments for Indonesia (and also Vietnam/India), reflecting rising Indonesian influence. The piece links Indonesia’s growing alumina output to capacity constraints in Australia, where refinery outages and force majeure have disrupted alumina supply.

ADEM and Ecovery entered a consent order over alleged air-permit violations at an Alabama secondary aluminum recycling facility. The plant processes aluminum-copper radiators and had visible particulate emissions linked to south dust collector maintenance. The firm replaced filters and seals; later stack tests showed compliance. A $15,000 civil penalty was assessed. | European aluminium premiums in Rotterdam and Italy remain well-supported, with the duty-unpaid premium edging higher amid tight regional supply, concerns over unpaid stock safety, low inventories, and high costs to source replacements. Japanese spot premiums narrowed on bearish sentiment and contango dynamics, while US activity stayed sluggish.

SMM data show China’s August 2024 aluminum output rose 1.8% YoY to 3.69 Mt, supported by new capacity in Inner Mongolia and little disruption from Sichuan power rationing. Operating rate reached 95.88%. September output is forecast stable near 3.57 Mt amid improving downstream billet activity. | An August 2024 wrap-up for aluminium highlights a moderate price increase on LME and SHFE, attributed to supply chain constraints and selective demand strength, despite volatility from geopolitics and weaker European foil deliveries. It also covers power/production factors (China output, renewable power caps) and supply chain initiatives affecting aluminium costs. | Kyrgyzstan has increased aluminium exports to China fourfold. The report indicates a large jump in outbound shipments, which can raise near-term supply availability in China. In the absence of offsetting demand or cost factors, such export growth is generally associated with downward pressure on aluminium prices.

ARM says weaker platinum group metals and coal prices cut its annual profit, prompting a pivot toward chrome and copper. It approved a chrome recovery plant at Bokoni and acquired a 15% stake in Surge Copper. South African miners also eye copper assets, but timing to production is long-dated. | China filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canadian tariffs on Chinese EVs plus 25% tariffs on steel and aluminium. Canada’s measures take effect next month and are aimed at unfair pricing and environmental/labour standards. China signals retaliation risk, adding near-term uncertainty for industrial demand. | China has filed a WTO complaint against Canada over Canada’s new tariffs on Chinese electric vehicles and 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum, with Canada’s measures set to start next month. Analysts warn retaliation could raise costs and pressure markets, adding near-term uncertainty for aluminum supply and prices. | Fairfax India will invest about $83 million for a 65% stake in Hyderabad-based Global Aluminium, a major Indian aluminium extrusions producer with anodized and powder-coated product capabilities. The transaction is expected to close in Q4 2024 and may modestly influence near-term aluminium-related demand, but no major market supply effects are specified.

A research team developed a prototype aluminium-polymer battery using aluminium foil as the anode and a solid polymer electrolyte based on ionic liquid. Funded further for scale-up and roll-to-roll production testing through 2025, it targets stationary storage for small PV systems, implying potential longer-term aluminium demand. | Ducab Metals Business (DMB) plans to double annual aluminium capacity from 55,000tpa to 110,000tpa at KEZAD and expand bare copper output. The company frames the move as supporting growing global demand and UAE industrial strategy, including green aluminium initiatives. It signals incremental supply expansion for aluminium and copper. | China’s Ministry of Ecology and Environment is consulting on expanding the national ETS to cover primary aluminium (plus cement and steel). The plan adds firms above a CO2e threshold, uses free allowance allocations initially, and increases trading/compliance pressure over time, likely raising costs for aluminium producers. | Falcon Gold mobilizes drill crews to begin an inaugural drilling campaign at its Great Burnt Copper Project in Newfoundland after prior access delays. The release cites high-grade prior copper drilling, outlines testing of geophysical targets, and notes ongoing permitting for additional drilling. | China is consulting on adding aluminium (along with steel and cement) to its national emissions trading scheme by end-2023. The ETS would expand in stages from 2024–2026 with free initial allowances and quota reductions from 2027. EU carbon tariffs also pressure Chinese decarbonisation of heavy industries. | CBA’s CEO says green aluminium currently cannot command higher premiums, though volume benefits exist. Fastmarkets’ low-carbon aluminium differentials for Europe/US have been assessed near zero, with recent declines. CBA plans process upgrades and emphasizes value-added product sales, potentially affecting low-carbon ingot supply in the short term.

Kentucky Aluminum Processors broke ground on a $40 million aluminum recycling and processing facility in Russellville. The plant, a joint venture involving Tri-Arrows Aluminum and Tennessee Aluminum Processors, will process post-industrial and post-consumer scrap for later melt and casting. Officials cite jobs and improved recycled-content output, supporting aluminum activity. | Power Nickel said its summer drilling at the Lion Zone discovery intercepted sulphides and expanded the mineralized footprint, including massive/semi-massive chalcopyrite, with the high-grade portion extending west and deeper. The company expects assays later in September and plans a larger winter drill program, but no production changes are announced. | Ghana’s parliament has ratified a mining lease allowing the Ghana Bauxite Company to mine bauxite at Awaso. GIADEC expects the lease to increase bauxite production and supports plans for improved mining and potential refining to build an integrated aluminum industry, boosting near-term aluminium supply expectations.

China proposed expanding its national carbon trading market to cover cement, steel, and electrolytic aluminum. The ministry says these sectors are ready to join this year, extending coverage to about 60% of emissions. Inclusion into carbon compliance frameworks may lift costs for electrolytic aluminum producers, supporting aluminium pricing near term. | Kentucky Aluminum Processors plans a new aluminum recycling facility in Russellville, Kentucky, to process dross and scrap from nearby Logan Aluminum’s casting operations. The state preliminarily approved incentives and tax breaks to encourage investment and jobs. The project expands secondary aluminum recycling capacity in the region. | Ducab Metals Business, a UAE subsidiary of Ducab Group, will double its aluminium product capacity from 55,000 to 110,000 tonnes per year by building a new plant at Kezad near EGA’s smelter. The facility is expected to commission by year-end and start producing in 2025, targeting rising domestic and export demand. | LME data show copper stocks up 29% in August and nickel open-tonnage stocks up 6.7%, with China-origin both rising. Aluminium stocks were broadly unchanged, with on-warrant volumes slightly higher. Rising inventories point to reduced prompt tightness, weighing on short-term base metal prices despite some copper inflow trend softening. | LME data show copper warehouse stocks up 29% in August and nickel open-tonnage stocks up 6.7%, with China-origin volumes rising. Copper cancellations increased as China import premiums improved. Aluminium stocks were broadly steady though. Overall, higher inventories suggest reduced near-term supply tightness, bearish for copper and nickel. | Century Aluminum plans a new “green” primary aluminium smelter in northeastern Kentucky and has been slated for up to $500m in U.S. DOE funding, aimed at using carbon-free power and reducing emissions. The project could nearly double U.S. primary output (~600,000 t/year), supporting aluminium supply expectations. | Zimbabwe police arrested suspects for stealing roughly 760kg of copper cables, amid a ZESA crackdown on electricity infrastructure vandalism. The utility is replacing copper cables with aluminium, and the government tightened copper possession rules with higher minimum sentences and origin documentation requirements for dealers. | Alba CEO Ali Al Baqali told the Fastmarkets International Aluminium Conference that primary aluminium producers face challenges including rising smelter costs. He also cited EU CBAM, carbon-emissions reduction and supply-chain sustainability as key drivers shaping costs and demand, with attention to regional market dynamics in MENA and Alba’s low-carbon products.

Esperance Port workers face potential job losses as major customers shut down over the next year, including First Quantum Minerals’ Ravensthorpe nickel operation and Mineral Resources’ Yilgarn iron ore project, with Arcadium Lithium also mothballing Mt Cattlin. The reduced export outlook affects 2025 trade volumes and associated sentiment.

At the International Aluminium Conference, Trafigura said the rising alumina price is the key issue, calling it “terrible” for aluminium producers. Fastmarkets put fob Australia alumina at a six-year high after Australian supply disruptions. Speakers also discussed China’s production cap and scrap shortages, amid focus on low-carbon premiums.

Aluminium prices rose on technical support and key resistance break, helped by lower LME inventories and a weaker U.S. dollar. Investors also increased expectations for a larger Fed rate cut this week. Three-month aluminium on the LME gained about 1.9%, hitting the highest level since late August. | The piece attributes recent and near-term pressure on aluminum prices to soft spot demand amid record-high China production and rising LME inventories (highest since 2021), suggesting oversupply. It notes possible upside if Fed rate cuts boost growth, but near-term demand concerns dominate. | The article frames aluminum as facing near-term pressure from China’s property slowdown, soft spot demand, and rising LME inventories, despite record Chinese production. It notes potential upside if Fed rate cuts support demand and borrowing costs. It highlights Avery Dennison’s aluminum-related products but offers no direct price catalyst for the metal itself. | The piece argues aluminum prices face near-term downside as soft spot demand persists amid record-high China production and rising LME inventories (highest since 2021), reflecting oversupply. It notes potential support later in Q4 if Fed rate cuts occur, but current demand weakness dominates sentiment. | The piece discusses aluminum market conditions, citing sluggish Chinese factory activity and a property sector crisis that dampen demand. It also notes record Chinese aluminum production and rising LME inventories at the highest since 2021, indicating oversupply. A later Q4 recovery is mentioned but near-term pressure appears bearish. | The article argues aluminum prices face near-term pressure from weaker global demand, China’s property slowdown, record Chinese output, and rising LME inventories. It also notes possible upside if the Fed cuts rates and borrowing costs ease, plus longer-run vehicle aluminum content trends. Ball is discussed mainly as a packaging demand beneficiary. | Ma’aden agreed to buy Alcoa’s remaining 25.1% stakes in Ma’aden’s bauxite and alumina venture and Ma’aden Aluminium Company for $1.04 billion, moving to 100% ownership. Completion is expected in 1H 2025, subject to approvals, potentially improving supply and refining certainty for aluminium markets. | Saudi miner Maaden is in talks to sell its aluminum and bauxite/alumina subsidiaries to Bahrain’s Alba in exchange for Alba shares, with regulatory approvals pending. The proposed expanded producer would boost global aluminum capacity and supply reliability, amid buyer anxiety over Russia-related shipment risks. Shares rose on the news.

Industry groups urged the U.S. ITC to reject proposed antidumping and countervailing duties on imports of aluminum lithographic printing plates from Japan and China. They argue the duties would increase costs, limit supply, and harm printing and publishing businesses. A rejection suggests less near-term demand downside for aluminum inputs. | Vedanta Aluminium said a process water storage pond breached at its Lanjigarh alumina refinery in Odisha due to harsh weather, affecting nearby agricultural land. It mobilised workers and is providing drinking water and medical support. The company stressed there is no disruption to current operations and production continues compliantly. | Saudi miner Ma’aden is exploring a non-binding merger of its aluminium subsidiaries with Bahrain’s Alba, potentially creating a large, vertically integrated aluminium producer. The proposal would involve due diligence and regulatory approvals, with the definitive terms still undetermined. Alba flagged the deal as uncertain, limiting immediate price impact. | Ma’aden agreed to acquire SABIC’s 20.62% stake in Aluminium Bahrain (ALBA), subject to conditions. The deal follows talks with ALBA on forming a regional aluminium powerhouse and Ma’aden’s plan to consolidate its aluminium business, including a share exchange with Alcoa.

Press Metal Aluminium said it is forming a joint venture with Indonesian firms to build a $750 million alumina refinery phase in Indonesia. The move comes after concerns about alumina supply shortages in China, a key raw material for aluminium. Press Metal will hold an 80% stake and expects offtake after commissioning.

Press Metal Aluminium said it agreed to a joint venture to develop an alumina refinery in West Kalimantan, Indonesia, with Phase 1 capacity of 1 to 1.2 million tonnes per year and potential doubling. The $750 million project is intended to secure alumina supply for its aluminium operations, supporting efficiency and growth. | Alcoa said it will curtail operations at its Kwinana alumina refinery due to high operating costs and permitting delays, while also citing stronger ESG constraints because operations sit near urban areas. Related Worsley project conditions were challenged. These supply-side pressures help underpin rising alumina prices and are likely supportive for aluminum. | Hitachi Industrial Products commercialized a copper die-cast rotor for railway traction motors, designed to be about 15 kg lighter than conventional copper bar-type rotors. The firm says it adapted its aluminum die-casting know-how to copper despite copper’s higher melting point, and will apply the rotor to railway projects. | The municipality said Exlabesa, an aluminium extrusion company, has inaugurated a new aluminium extrusion factory in Soure, Portugal, with two 2,800-tonne lines and 16,000 tonnes annual capacity. It also plans further expansion to 45,000 tonnes. The report implies more processing capacity, but not a near-term supply shock.

Delegates at an aluminium scrap conference said Europe is very short of aluminium scrap, with wrought-friendly grades increasingly scarce as demand rises and material exports to Asia tighten supply. Investors in processing (e.g., X-ray/LIBS sorting) aim to recover more usable grades, while scrap prices have been rising toward LME parity. | Rio Tinto reduced its fair value estimate, citing weaker commodity price expectations, including lower copper and aluminum, alongside softer demand linked to the end of the China boom. The article notes China is Rio Tinto’s largest customer and that earnings outlook could decline as commodity demand softens. | AEROBAL says members’ global deliveries of aluminium aerosol containers rose 4.4% in 1H 2024, led by Europe and the Americas, with deodorant and hairspray demand strengthening. It notes limited access to high-quality recyclate/scrap and ongoing EU “design for recycling” work, suggesting supportive near-term aluminium consumption.

Golden Deeps announced new drill intersections at its NSW Havilah project, including long zones of disseminated copper-silver sulphides and higher-grade chalcopyrite, with pXRF copper grades up to 3.6%. The results extend the target trend and may boost copper exploration sentiment, but remain exploration-stage with no immediate supply impact. | China’s primary aluminium imports rose in August 2024 to about 163,000 mt (+25.7% MoM), lifting cumulative imports Jan-Aug to 1.512 million mt (+100.2% YoY). Primary aluminium exports fell to about 14,900 mt (-21.3% MoM) and cumulative exports dropped 41.3% YoY, indicating higher net import demand. | Alcoa announced it will sell its 25.1% stake in a Ma’aden joint venture in Saudi Arabia to Ma’aden’s partner for about $1.1bn. Separately, Ma’aden and Alba agreed to due diligence on a possible combination, while Ma’aden also acquired Sabic’s 20.62% stake in Alba.

Aluminum Dynamics’ $2.5 billion aluminium rolling mill in Mississippi is more than halfway complete, with construction employing hundreds of contractors and nearly 400 staff. The project aims to have the facility shipping aluminium by next year and expects up to 750 employees by summer 2025, indicating rising aluminium demand. | Indonesia launched a $941 million smelter-grade alumina refinery in West Kalimantan, supplying 1 million tons per year and planned expansion, using bauxite feedstock. The project is run by Antam and Inalum, with plans to add an aluminium plant and reduce imports, potentially supporting aluminium supply expectations. | Indonesia launched a $941 million smelter-grade alumina refinery in West Kalimantan, with 1 million mt/year capacity and plans to expand by another 1 million mt plus build an aluminium plant. The project follows Indonesia’s bauxite export ban and aims to reduce aluminium imports by scaling domestic processing. | Mercedes-Benz and Hydro expand their aluminum supply-chain partnership in Brazil’s Pará, focusing on community development along Hydro’s bauxite slurry pipeline to the Alunorte alumina refinery and on low-carbon aluminium initiatives. The MoU with NGOs targets decarbonization and responsible sourcing, without any stated near-term production or tariff changes. | Indonesia inaugurated phase 1 construction of an alumina refinery smelter in West Kalimantan, backed by PT Borneo Alumina Indonesia, Inalum and Antam. The project aims to cut reliance on imported alumina and reduce raw material exports. More domestic alumina supply supports aluminium refining and may underpin aluminium prices. | FTTH Council Europe welcomed a Draghi Report proposal to set a date for shutting down legacy copper networks to accelerate Europe’s fibre (FTTH) deployment. The group argues copper switch-off supports investment and demand for fibre. The piece contains no quantified copper or aluminium supply/demand figures, limiting near-term price impact. | The U.S. ITC held a hearing on proposed AD/CVD tariffs on aluminum lithographic printing plates from China and Japan. Industry stakeholders argued the duties would raise costs, limit supply, and harm printers and publishers, noting no U.S. supply for the aluminum sheet used to make the plates. No testimony supported new tariffs. | Technavio projects the aluminum pigment market to grow by about USD 168.6 million (2024-2028), driven by automotive demand and tightening VOC regulations leading to low-VOC aluminum pigments. It also notes aluminum price volatility historically affects pigment costs, implying supportive demand expectations for aluminium.

Investopedia reports Freeport-McMoRan shares jumped as copper and gold prices rose. Copper futures climbed about 4% after China unveiled stimulus aimed at supporting sluggish housing and financial markets, boosting expected demand. The piece also notes Fed rate cuts weaken the dollar, further supporting copper and gold prices. | Western Metallica announced the start of a first drilling program in October at its Luz Maria copper (Cu-Mo) porphyry prospect within the Caña Brava project in Peru, following permits. The news is an exploration sentiment driver with no immediate supply or demand change; nickel and aluminium are not addressed. | GGL Resources announced Teck’s option agreement for GGL’s Le Champ copper-molybdenum-gold porphyry target in Nevada, including cash option payments and a production-decision milestone. The release supports potential future copper/molybdenum development but provides no near-term output, so any immediate copper price impact is likely limited. | Sputnik reports Russia boosted aluminium shipments to China 1.4x to a record $2.3B in Jan-August, far above 2019’s $60.6M. Total metal supplies to China rose 9%. The increased aluminium supply is likely to pressure near-term aluminium prices, while copper and nickel were more stable.

Vietnam’s trade ministry extended anti-dumping duties on some Chinese-origin aluminum products until October 2029, with rates from 2.85% to 35.58%. The ministry said imports could harm Vietnam’s domestic industry if dumping measures lapsed, implying continued restriction of cheap aluminum inflows. | Vietnam’s Ministry of Industry and Trade has issued final results extending anti-dumping measures on Chinese-origin aluminum products for an additional five years. Duty rates range from 2.85% to 35.58%, following findings of dumping and harm to Vietnam’s domestic aluminum industry, likely reducing import competition and supporting prices. | The PIVOT project, backed by UK government funding, uses AI and casting simulation to create up to 30% lighter cast aluminium vehicle components, including designs based on 100% recycled aluminium. The stated goal is major reductions in embedded carbon. This may support aluminium demand expectations, though it is R&D-focused. | India’s DPIIT has made BIS certification mandatory for aluminium wire rods, sheets, plates, and strips sold in the country, effective Thursday. Vedanta Aluminium says it is the first and only producer to receive BIS certification for products from BALCO and Jharsuguda facilities, implying tighter supply compliance for aluminium rolled goods. | Copper rallied above US$10,000 on the LME, driven by China’s Politburo pledging real-estate stabilization and “forceful” rate cuts, plus a potential large capital injection into state banks. U.S. growth data also improved risk appetite. Aluminium rose about 2% alongside copper strength.

India’s DPIIT mandate requires all aluminium wire rods and rolled products sold domestically to carry BIS certification starting September 26, 2024. Vedanta Aluminium says it is the first and only producer to receive BIS certification for these items. The regulation could limit near-term supply from non-compliant producers. | Carnaby Resources reported results from a VTEM magnetic survey at the Greater Duchess copper-gold JV, identifying multiple new undrilled conductive targets and highlighting the Mohawk corridor with prior 21m at 2% copper. Management expects immediate drilling focus and possible resource expansion, but no near-term output change is provided. | Novelis’ “3x30” initiative targets 75% recycled content and lower CO2 intensity for flat-rolled aluminium by 2030, alongside investments in circularity. It also cites record recycled-content levels, 2.3 million tons recycled in 2023, and plant upgrades to handle contaminated scrap, supporting aluminium demand for low-carbon metal.

China’s ETS is set to expand in 2024 to cover aluminium (plus steel and cement), raising covered emissions to 60%. New sectors will initially receive free allowances with no overall cap, while analysts warn the system’s intensity focus and allowance oversupply may limit carbon-price strength, so aluminium price impact appears muted. | Bonnell Aluminum said it is pleased with the U.S. Department of Commerce’s final determinations that impose anti-dumping and countervailing duties on aluminum extrusion imports from 14 countries. The decision is expected to reduce unfairly traded imports and support pricing for aluminum extrusion products.

Canada’s Finance Minister said Ottawa could implement a framework to consider requests for tariff relief from a 25% surtax on imported Chinese steel and aluminum due Oct 22. The government is responding to stakeholder concerns about adjustment time, with details to be released later. | Canada will impose a 25% surtax on selected Chinese-made steel and aluminum products. The aluminum component can increase the cost competitiveness of Chinese imports versus domestic supply, tightening near-term availability and supporting aluminium price expectations over coming weeks. | Canada plans to implement a 25% surtax on certain steel and aluminum products from China on Oct. 22, while considering tariff relief where alternative supply is in short demand. The move follows a same-day 100% surtax on Chinese-made electric vehicles. Trade barriers may tighten aluminum availability and support prices. | The report headlines that Canada plans to impose tariffs on Chinese steel and aluminum starting Oct. 22. By restricting Chinese aluminum imports, the measure could reduce near-term availability and shift demand toward alternative suppliers, potentially lifting aluminum price expectations in the coming days. | PA Resources said the US Commerce Department granted it a 0% dumping rate for aluminium extrusion exports after anti-dumping proceedings. The ruling means the products are considered fairly priced and face no extra tariffs in the US, improving competitiveness and potentially supporting aluminium extrusion demand and pricing expectations. | Canada will impose 25% tariffs on a wide range of Chinese steel and aluminum items starting Oct. 22, citing China’s overcapacity and protecting domestic producers. Goods already in transit on the start date are exempt. The policy is framed as aligning with allied trade actions against Chinese industrial supply.

The commentary says Jamaica’s Cockpit Country contains Jamaica’s last proven bauxite reserves and describes a new protected-area designation after long Maroon opposition. It argues bauxite/alumina mining harmed health and the environment and questions whether mining areas are included, implying potential supply disruption and downside risk for aluminium. | LME is monitoring tightness after the aluminium October contract premium vs November surged to about $18/ton. One trader holds a large long position in October while most nearby inventory is concentrated in Port Klang. Higher ISTIM re-warrant/cancellation fees could deter delivery, raising prospects of short-covering and sharp price moves near expiry.

Granite Creek Copper reports discovery of a new “Gap Zone” at its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, with copper mineralization in 3 of 4 holes and results pending from sampled sections. The company suggests potential to expand resources and mine life, but this is exploration news without immediate supply impact. | ALRO opened a new aluminium processing (cutting) facility, CUTSMART SYSTEMS, following a RON13.7M investment. The move is aimed at diversifying processing capabilities and expanding production support. While it may modestly affect aluminium supply/demand expectations, the article lacks volume or market-wide implications.

Romanian aluminium producer Alro has begun operations of a new aluminium processing facility after a EUR 2.7mn investment, aimed at high-precision cutting and broader product offerings via Cutsmart Systems. The company says it improves efficiency and can reduce CO2 via optimized transportation and exact metal quantities, but the scale suggests limited near-term price impact. | ALRO opened a new precision aluminium cutting facility (Cutsmart Systems) after a RON 13.7 million investment. The company says the plant improves efficiency and reduces CO2 emissions via optimized transportation and ordering exact quantities. The move expands ALRO’s processing capabilities and product tailoring for customer needs. | Freeport CEO Andrea Quirk says the medium/long-term copper outlook is positive and short-term price action is sentiment-driven. She cites China stimulus and US rate cuts for improving sentiment and supporting LME copper prices. Demand drivers remain strong, mine supply growth is limited, and copper-to-aluminium substitution is expected to be small (1–2%).

Rusal plans to double capacity at the Boguchansky aluminium smelter in Krasnoyarsk to 600,000 tonnes by the end of the decade, with construction on additional work scheduled to begin in 2025. The move could add to long-term supply expectations, though near-term output changes are limited. | Argus will launch a European monthly upcharge for low-carbon aluminium to reflect payment structures for lower-emission metal as manufacturers seek greener inputs. The move formalizes trading of sustainability-linked premiums, suggesting increased willingness to pay for low-carbon supply and supporting higher aluminium premium expectations in the coming weeks.

Jaguar Land Rover said production was restricted due to supply disruptions from a key high-grade aluminium supplier affecting multiple OEMs. The disruption follows Novelis force majeure after Switzerland floods shut a plant. JLR also held some vehicles pending quality checks. The company expects supply to normalise in H2.

Japan’s Q4 aluminium premium for October–December was set at $175/t, up 1.7% QoQ, with producers citing tighter regional supplies and firmer European premiums. The premium follows higher offers and is tied to benchmark LME cash pricing. Weak Japanese demand and higher inventories likely limit upside. | The interview says Indonesia’s nickel industry is scaling into an EV battery-material ecosystem, citing commissioned precursor and lithium refining capacity and existing copper-foil operations near Freeport. It also notes forced-labor/environmental allegations and IRA rules limiting subsidized EV inputs from highly foreign-controlled entities, plus LFP’s nickel-free shift. | Novelis and TSR signed a 3-year contract for about 75,000 tonnes of presorted end-of-life aluminum scrap to be processed at Novelis’ Nachterstedt recycling center and converted into low-carbon automotive sheet. The deal boosts recycled-content aluminum availability and supports demand for high-recycling feedstock in Europe.

Commerce issued affirmative final AD/CVD determinations in a U.S. aluminum extrusions investigation, deferring most scope-related comments. Importers face rules around suspension of liquidation and depositing duties (CVD/AD mechanics pending an ITC injury decision within 45 days). The outcome could support domestic aluminum-extrusion supply expectations. | TSR and Novelis agreed a three-year supply deal for 75,000 tonnes of high-quality, pre-sorted end-of-life aluminium scrap for automotive sheet production. The scrap grades TSR130/136 target specific alloys and decarbonization goals. Novelis cites limited scrap availability and the need for long-term supply resilience.

Canada will implement tariff surcharges on Chinese enterprises for aluminium (and steel) effective Oct 22, 2024, as part of measures against China’s perceived overcapacity and unfair trade policies. The action is likely to increase costs for Chinese aluminium imports into Canada and support nearby aluminium price expectations. | The article headline says alumina in China has risen to a record high on supply woes while demand remains strong. As alumina is the key feedstock for aluminium smelting, tighter supply and higher input costs tend to lift expectations for aluminium prices over the coming days to weeks. | Fastmarkets issued a correction to its previously published premium for aluminium 6063 extrusion billet (in-warehouse Rotterdam) for Oct 11. The premium range was adjusted from $530-560/tonne to $525-560/tonne and the pricing database was updated accordingly within its North Europe aluminium billet package.

SMM reports overseas metallurgical-grade alumina output fell 2.7% YoY in September, with refinery operating rates down year-on-year. Multiple disruptions and guidance cuts (Alcoa shutdown, Rio Tinto reductions, South32 maintenance/environment delays) contribute. October is forecast to decline further 2.7% YoY, with weaker operating rates. | Reuters columnist Clyde Russell says investors reacted differently to Beijing’s latest stimulus rhetoric. Shanghai copper futures opened higher while London copper fell, reflecting sentiment on possible refinancing of local government debt and renewed construction/property demand, though the author argues demand effects may be limited and later. | Xanadu released a large prefeasibility study for its Kharmagtai copper-gold project in Mongolia, projecting ~60,000 tonnes of copper annually initially, low cash costs, and a long 29-year mine life. The company expects a bankable feasibility study in 2026, supporting sentiment for future copper supply.

Alcoa said it will supply up to 16.5 million tonnes of smelter-grade alumina to Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) from 2026–2035, mainly sourced from Western Australia. The deal extends a long relationship and is intended to provide Alba reliable alumina supply, though it is not a near-term shock to prices. | Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) has renewed a long-term alumina supply agreement with Alcoa for up to 16.5 million tonnes of smelter-grade alumina over 10 years, beginning in 2026. Both firms said the deal secures stable feedstock for Alba’s operations and reinforces Alcoa’s alumina-supply position.

Rio Tinto said its Amrun bauxite mine is operating above nameplate capacity, lifting bauxite output, but primary aluminum production fell due to an electricity reduction request at its New Zealand smelter that has ended and ramp-up is underway. The firm also reported lower mined copper output. Overall effects may offset near term. | Australia’s Aluminium Council renewed its call for bauxite, alumina and aluminium to be listed as Critical Minerals, arguing risks from geopolitics, higher costs, and lengthy environmental approvals have impaired and curtailed alumina refining. The group urges faster approvals to secure bauxite feed for refineries and avoid supply disruption. | Supply disruption in Guinea suspended bauxite exports from Guinea Alumina Corporation over customs issues. With Guinea accounting for about 4% of global bauxite supply, alumina prices have surged, creating potential gains for bauxite producers like Metro Mining. The tighter feedstock outlook points to near-term bullish aluminium price pressure. | Rio Tinto reported Q3 copper and aluminium production updates.
